search_text: Controlling and scripting laboratory hardware with open-source, intuitive interfaces: OpenFlexure Voice Control and OpenFlexure Blockly 使用开源、直观的界面控制和编写实验室硬件脚本：OpenFlexure Voice Control 和 OpenFlexure Blockly Making user interaction with laboratory equipment more convenient and intuitive should promote experimental work and help researchers to complete their tasks efficiently. The most common form of interaction in current instrumentation is either direct tactile, with buttons and knobs, or interfaced through a computer, using a mouse and keyboard. Scripting is another function typical of smart and automated laboratory equipment, yet users are currently required to learn bespoke programming languages and libraries for individual pieces of equipment. In this paper, we present two open-source, novel and intuitive ways of interacting with and scripting laboratory equipment. We choose the OpenFlexure family of microscopes as our exemplar, due to their open-source nature and smart control system. Firstly, we demonstrate 'OpenFlexure Voice Control' to enable users to control the microscope hands-free. Secondly, we present 'OpenFlexure Blockly' which uses the Blockly Visual Programming Language to enable users to easily create scripts for the microscope, using a drag and drop web interface. We explain the design choices when developing these tools, and discuss more typical use cases and more general applications. 让用户与实验室设备的交互更加方便和直观，应该会促进实验工作并帮助研究人员高效地完成任务。当前仪器中最常见的交互形式是使用按钮和旋钮的直接触觉，或者使用鼠标和键盘通过计算机进行交互。脚本编写是智能和自动化实验室设备的另一个典型功能，但用户目前需要学习针对各个设备的定制编程语言和库。在本文中，我们提出了两种与实验室设备交互和编写脚本的开源、新颖且直观的方式。我们选择 OpenFlexure 系列显微镜作为示例，因为它们的开源特性和智能控制系统。首先，我们演示“OpenFlexure 语音控制”，使用户能够免提控制显微镜。其次，我们推出“OpenFlexure Blockly”，它使用 Blockly 可视化编程语言，使用户能够使用拖放 Web 界面轻松创建显微镜脚本。我们解释了开发这些工具时的设计选择，并讨论了更典型的用例和更通用的应用。 本文介绍了两种开源工具：OpenFlexure Voice Control和OpenFlexure Blockly，旨在通过语音控制和可视化编程界面提升实验室设备的操作便捷性。 Samuel McDermott,Richard Bowman,Kerrianne Harrington,William Wadsworth,Pietro Cicuta physics.ins-det,cs.HC

title: EiHi Net: Out-of-Distribution Generalization Paradigm
title_zh: EiHi Net：分布外泛化范式
abstract: This paper develops a new EiHi net to solve the out-of-distribution (OoD) generalization problem in deep learning. EiHi net is a model learning paradigm that can be blessed on any visual backbone. This paradigm can change the previous learning method of the deep model, namely find out correlations between inductive sample features and corresponding categories, which suffers from pseudo correlations between indecisive features and labels. We fuse SimCLR and VIC-Reg via explicitly and dynamically establishing the original - positive - negative sample pair as a minimal learning element, the deep model iteratively establishes a relationship close to the causal one between features and labels, while suppressing pseudo correlations. To further validate the proposed model, and strengthen the established causal relationships, we develop a human-in-the-loop strategy, with few guidance samples, to prune the representation space directly. Finally, it is shown that the developed EiHi net makes significant improvements in the most difficult and typical OoD dataset Nico, compared with the current SOTA results, without any domain ($e.g.$ background, irrelevant features) information.
summary: This paper develops a new EiHi net to solve the out-of-distribution (OoD) generalization problem in deep learning. EiHi net is a model learning paradigm that can be blessed on any visual backbone. This paradigm can change the previous learning method of the deep model, namely find out correlations between inductive sample features and corresponding categories, which suffers from pseudo correlations between indecisive features and labels. We fuse SimCLR and VIC-Reg via explicitly and dynamically establishing the original - positive - negative sample pair as a minimal learning element, the deep model iteratively establishes a relationship close to the causal one between features and labels, while suppressing pseudo correlations. To further validate the proposed model, and strengthen the established causal relationships, we develop a human-in-the-loop strategy, with few guidance samples, to prune the representation space directly. Finally, it is shown that the developed EiHi net makes significant improvements in the most difficult and typical OoD dataset Nico, compared with the current SOTA results, without any domain ($e.g.$ background, irrelevant features) information.
abstract_zh: 本文开发了一种新的 EiHi 网络来解决深度学习中的分布外（OoD）泛化问题。 EiHi net 是一个模型学习范例，可以在任何视觉主干上得到支持。该范式可以改变以往深度模型的学习方法，即找出归纳样本特征与对应类别之间的相关性，这种方法存在不确定性特征与标签之间的伪相关性。我们通过显式地动态建立原始-正-负样本对作为最小学习元素来融合 SimCLR 和 VIC-Reg，深度模型迭代地建立特征和标签之间接近因果关系的关系，同时抑制伪相关性。为了进一步验证所提出的模型，并加强已建立的因果关系，我们开发了一种人机循环策略，用很少的指导样本来直接修剪表示空间。最后，结果表明，与当前的 SOTA 结果相比，开发的 EiHi 网络在最困难和典型的 OoD 数据集 Nico 上取得了显着改进，并且没有任何域（$e.g.$背景、不相关特征）信息。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为EiHi Net的新模型学习范式，旨在解决深度学习中的分布外（OoD）泛化问题。
AI.motivation: 现有深度模型通过寻找归纳样本特征与类别间的相关性进行学习，但易受不确定特征与标签间的伪相关性影响，导致在OoD场景下表现不佳。
AI.method: 融合SimCLR和VIC-Reg方法，通过显式动态构建原始-正样本-负样本对作为最小学习单元，建立特征与标签间的因果关系，并引入少量人类指导样本直接修剪表示空间。
AI.result: 在最具挑战性的OoD数据集Nico上，EiHi Net相比当前最先进方法显著提升了性能，且无需任何领域（如背景、无关特征）信息。
AI.conclusion: EiHi Net通过抑制伪相关性并强化因果关系，有效提升了模型在分布外场景下的泛化能力，为OoD问题提供了新范式。

authors_text: Yanmin Wu,Xinhua Cheng,Renrui Zhang,Zesen Cheng,Jian Zhang
title: EDA: Explicit Text-Decoupling and Dense Alignment for 3D Visual Grounding
title_zh: EDA：显式文本解耦和密集对齐以实现 3D 视觉基础
abstract: 3D visual grounding aims to find the object within point clouds mentioned by free-form natural language descriptions with rich semantic cues. However, existing methods either extract the sentence-level features coupling all words or focus more on object names, which would lose the word-level information or neglect other attributes. To alleviate these issues, we present EDA that Explicitly Decouples the textual attributes in a sentence and conducts Dense Alignment between such fine-grained language and point cloud objects. Specifically, we first propose a text decoupling module to produce textual features for every semantic component. Then, we design two losses to supervise the dense matching between two modalities: position alignment loss and semantic alignment loss. On top of that, we further introduce a new visual grounding task, locating objects without object names, which can thoroughly evaluate the model's dense alignment capacity. Through experiments, we achieve state-of-the-art performance on two widely-adopted 3D visual grounding datasets, ScanRefer and SR3D/NR3D, and obtain absolute leadership on our newly-proposed task. The source code is available at https://github.com/yanmin-wu/EDA.
summary: 3D visual grounding aims to find the object within point clouds mentioned by free-form natural language descriptions with rich semantic cues. However, existing methods either extract the sentence-level features coupling all words or focus more on object names, which would lose the word-level information or neglect other attributes. To alleviate these issues, we present EDA that Explicitly Decouples the textual attributes in a sentence and conducts Dense Alignment between such fine-grained language and point cloud objects. Specifically, we first propose a text decoupling module to produce textual features for every semantic component. Then, we design two losses to supervise the dense matching between two modalities: position alignment loss and semantic alignment loss. On top of that, we further introduce a new visual grounding task, locating objects without object names, which can thoroughly evaluate the model's dense alignment capacity. Through experiments, we achieve state-of-the-art performance on two widely-adopted 3D visual grounding datasets, ScanRefer and SR3D/NR3D, and obtain absolute leadership on our newly-proposed task. The source code is available at https://github.com/yanmin-wu/EDA.
abstract_zh: 3D视觉基础旨在找到具有丰富语义线索的自由形式自然语言描述所提到的点云中的对象。然而，现有的方法要么提取耦合所有单词的句子级特征，要么更多地关注对象名称，这会丢失单词级信息或忽略其他属性。为了缓解这些问题，我们提出了 EDA，它可以显式解耦句子中的文本属性，并在这种细粒度语言和点云对象之间进行密集对齐。具体来说，我们首先提出一个文本解耦模块来为每个语义组件生成文本特征。然后，我们设计了两种损失来监督两种模态之间的密集匹配：位置对齐损失和语义对齐损失。除此之外，我们还引入了一种新的视觉基础任务，定位没有对象名称的对象，可以彻底评估模型的密集对齐能力。通过实验，我们在两个广泛采用的 3D 视觉基础数据集 ScanRefer 和 SR3D/NR3D 上实现了最先进的性能，并在我们新提出的任务中获得了绝对领先。源代码可在 https://github.com/yanmin-wu/EDA 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EDA方法，通过显式解耦文本属性并进行密集对齐，解决3D视觉定位任务中细粒度语言与点云对象匹配的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理自然语言描述时要么耦合所有词汇丢失细粒度信息，要么仅关注物体名称而忽略其他属性，导致3D视觉定位效果受限。
AI.method: 设计文本解耦模块提取每个语义组件的特征，结合位置对齐损失和语义对齐损失进行跨模态密集匹配，并引入无物体名称定位任务验证模型能力。
AI.result: 在ScanRefer和SR3D/NR3D数据集上达到SOTA性能，在新提出的无名称定位任务中取得绝对优势。
AI.conclusion: EDA通过细粒度语言-点云对齐机制，有效提升了3D视觉定位的准确性和鲁棒性。

search_text: EDA: Explicit Text-Decoupling and Dense Alignment for 3D Visual Grounding EDA：显式文本解耦和密集对齐以实现 3D 视觉基础 3D visual grounding aims to find the object within point clouds mentioned by free-form natural language descriptions with rich semantic cues. However, existing methods either extract the sentence-level features coupling all words or focus more on object names, which would lose the word-level information or neglect other attributes. To alleviate these issues, we present EDA that Explicitly Decouples the textual attributes in a sentence and conducts Dense Alignment between such fine-grained language and point cloud objects. Specifically, we first propose a text decoupling module to produce textual features for every semantic component. Then, we design two losses to supervise the dense matching between two modalities: position alignment loss and semantic alignment loss. On top of that, we further introduce a new visual grounding task, locating objects without object names, which can thoroughly evaluate the model's dense alignment capacity. Through experiments, we achieve state-of-the-art performance on two widely-adopted 3D visual grounding datasets, ScanRefer and SR3D/NR3D, and obtain absolute leadership on our newly-proposed task. The source code is available at https://github.com/yanmin-wu/EDA. 3D视觉基础旨在找到具有丰富语义线索的自由形式自然语言描述所提到的点云中的对象。然而，现有的方法要么提取耦合所有单词的句子级特征，要么更多地关注对象名称，这会丢失单词级信息或忽略其他属性。为了缓解这些问题，我们提出了 EDA，它可以显式解耦句子中的文本属性，并在这种细粒度语言和点云对象之间进行密集对齐。具体来说，我们首先提出一个文本解耦模块来为每个语义组件生成文本特征。然后，我们设计了两种损失来监督两种模态之间的密集匹配：位置对齐损失和语义对齐损失。除此之外，我们还引入了一种新的视觉基础任务，定位没有对象名称的对象，可以彻底评估模型的密集对齐能力。通过实验，我们在两个广泛采用的 3D 视觉基础数据集 ScanRefer 和 SR3D/NR3D 上实现了最先进的性能，并在我们新提出的任务中获得了绝对领先。源代码可在 https://github.com/yanmin-wu/EDA 获取。 本文提出EDA方法，通过显式解耦文本属性并进行密集对齐，解决3D视觉定位任务中细粒度语言与点云对象匹配的问题。 Yanmin Wu,Xinhua Cheng,Renrui Zhang,Zesen Cheng,Jian Zhang cs.CV,cs.RO

title: Reinforcement Learning Algorithms: An Overview and Classification
title_zh: 强化学习算法：概述和分类
comment: ef:2021 IEEE Canadian Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (CCECE), 2021, pp. 1-7
comments: ef:2021 IEEE Canadian Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (CCECE), 2021, pp. 1-7
abstract: The desire to make applications and machines more intelligent and the aspiration to enable their operation without human interaction have been driving innovations in neural networks, deep learning, and other machine learning techniques. Although reinforcement learning has been primarily used in video games, recent advancements and the development of diverse and powerful reinforcement algorithms have enabled the reinforcement learning community to move from playing video games to solving complex real-life problems in autonomous systems such as self-driving cars, delivery drones, and automated robotics. Understanding the environment of an application and the algorithms' limitations plays a vital role in selecting the appropriate reinforcement learning algorithm that successfully solves the problem on hand in an efficient manner. Consequently, in this study, we identify three main environment types and classify reinforcement learning algorithms according to those environment types. Moreover, within each category, we identify relationships between algorithms. The overview of each algorithm provides insight into the algorithms' foundations and reviews similarities and differences among algorithms. This study provides a perspective on the field and helps practitioners and researchers to select the appropriate algorithm for their use case.

Although reinforcement learning has been primarily used in video games, recent advancements and the development of diverse and powerful reinforcement algorithms have enabled the reinforcement learning community to move from playing video games to solving complex real-life problems in autonomous systems such as self-driving cars, delivery drones, and automated robotics. Understanding the environment of an application and the algorithms' limitations plays a vital role in selecting the appropriate reinforcement learning algorithm that successfully solves the problem on hand in an efficient manner. Consequently, in this study, we identify three main environment types and classify reinforcement learning algorithms according to those environment types. Moreover, within each category, we identify relationships between algorithms. The overview of each algorithm provides insight into the algorithms' foundations and reviews similarities and differences among algorithms. This study provides a perspective on the field and helps practitioners and researchers to select the appropriate algorithm for their use case.
abstract_zh: 让应用程序和机器更加智能的愿望以及无需人类交互即可运行的愿望一直在推动神经网络、深度学习和其他机器学习技术的创新。尽管强化学习主要用于视频游戏，但最近的进步和多样化且强大的强化算法的发展使强化学习社区能够从玩视频游戏转向解决自动驾驶汽车、送货无人机和自动化机器人等自主系统中复杂的现实问题。了解应用程序的环境和算法的局限性对于选择合适的强化学习算法以有效地成功解决现有问题至关重要。因此，在本研究中，我们确定了三种主要的环境类型，并根据这些环境类型对强化学习算法进行分类。此外，在每个类别中，我们确定了算法之间的关系。每个算法的概述提供了对算法基础的深入了解，并回顾了算法之间的相似点和差异。这项研究提供了该领域的视角，并帮助从业者和研究人员为其用例选择合适的算法。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在对强化学习算法进行分类概述，并根据环境类型进行分类，以帮助选择适合实际问题的算法。
AI.motivation: 随着对智能化应用和自主系统的需求增加，强化学习从游戏领域扩展到现实问题，但算法选择缺乏系统性指导，需明确环境类型与算法关系。
AI.method: 通过识别三种主要环境类型，按环境分类强化学习算法，并分析算法间的关联性与差异性。
AI.result: 建立了基于环境类型的强化学习算法分类框架，总结了各算法的基础原理及适用场景的异同。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为从业者和研究人员提供了算法选择的参考视角，助力高效解决实际问题。

authors_text: Rudra P. K. Poudel,Harit Pandya,Roberto Cipolla
title: Contrastive Unsupervised Learning of World Model with Invariant Causal Features
title_zh: 具有不变因果特征的世界模型的对比无监督学习
abstract: In this paper we present a world model, which learns causal features using the invariance principle. In particular, we use contrastive unsupervised learning to learn the invariant causal features, which enforces invariance across augmentations of irrelevant parts or styles of the observation. The world-model-based reinforcement learning methods independently optimize representation learning and the policy. Thus naive contrastive loss implementation collapses due to a lack of supervisory signals to the representation learning module. We propose an intervention invariant auxiliary task to mitigate this issue. Specifically, we utilize depth prediction to explicitly enforce the invariance and use data augmentation as style intervention on the RGB observation space. Our design leverages unsupervised representation learning to learn the world model with invariant causal features. Our proposed method significantly outperforms current state-of-the-art model-based and model-free reinforcement learning methods on out-of-distribution point navigation tasks on the iGibson dataset. Moreover, our proposed model excels at the sim-to-real transfer of our perception learning module. Finally, we evaluate our approach on the DeepMind control suite and enforce invariance only implicitly since depth is not available. Nevertheless, our proposed model performs on par with the state-of-the-art counterpart.
summary: In this paper we present a world model, which learns causal features using the invariance principle. In particular, we use contrastive unsupervised learning to learn the invariant causal features, which enforces invariance across augmentations of irrelevant parts or styles of the observation. The world-model-based reinforcement learning methods independently optimize representation learning and the policy. Thus naive contrastive loss implementation collapses due to a lack of supervisory signals to the representation learning module. We propose an intervention invariant auxiliary task to mitigate this issue. Specifically, we utilize depth prediction to explicitly enforce the invariance and use data augmentation as style intervention on the RGB observation space. Our design leverages unsupervised representation learning to learn the world model with invariant causal features. Our proposed method significantly outperforms current state-of-the-art model-based and model-free reinforcement learning methods on out-of-distribution point navigation tasks on the iGibson dataset. Moreover, our proposed model excels at the sim-to-real transfer of our perception learning module. Finally, we evaluate our approach on the DeepMind control suite and enforce invariance only implicitly since depth is not available. Nevertheless, our proposed model performs on par with the state-of-the-art counterpart.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一个世界模型，它使用不变性原理学习因果特征。特别是，我们使用对比无监督学习来学习不变的因果特征，这可以在观察的不相关部分或风格的增强中强制不变性。基于世界模型的强化学习方法独立优化表示学习和策略。因此，由于缺乏表示学习模块的监督信号，天真的对比损失实现崩溃了。我们提出了一种干预不变的辅助任务来缓解这个问题。具体来说，我们利用深度预测来显式地强制不变性，并使用数据增强作为 RGB 观察空间的风格干预。我们的设计利用无监督表示学习来学习具有不变因果特征的世界模型。我们提出的方法在 iGibson 数据集上的分布外点导航任务上显着优于当前最先进的基于模型和无模型的强化学习方法。此外，我们提出的模型擅长感知学习模块的模拟到真实的迁移。最后，我们在 DeepMind 控制套件上评估我们的方法，并仅隐式地强制不变性，因为深度不可用。尽管如此，我们提出的模型的性能与最先进的模型相当。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于对比无监督学习的世界模型，通过不变因果特征学习提升强化学习性能。
AI.motivation: 现有基于世界模型的强化学习方法独立优化表征学习与策略，导致对比损失函数因缺乏监督信号而失效。
AI.method: 设计干预不变辅助任务，结合深度预测与RGB观测空间的数据增强，强制学习不变因果特征。
AI.result: 在iGibson数据集的分布外导航任务中显著优于现有方法，并实现感知模块的仿真到现实迁移。
AI.conclusion: 所提模型即使在无深度数据的场景下（如DeepMind控制套件）仍能保持与最先进方法相当的性能。

authors_text: Hwan Kim,Byung Suk Lee,Won-Yong Shin,Sungsu Lim
title: Graph Anomaly Detection with Graph Neural Networks: Current Status and Challenges
title_zh: 使用图神经网络进行图异常检测：现状和挑战
comment: 9 pages, 2 figures, 1 tables; to appear in the IEEE Access (Please cite our journal version.)
comments: 9 pages, 2 figures, 1 tables; to appear in the IEEE Access (Please cite our journal version.)
abstract: Graphs are used widely to model complex systems, and detecting anomalies in a graph is an important task in the analysis of complex systems. Graph anomalies are patterns in a graph that do not conform to normal patterns expected of the attributes and/or structures of the graph. In recent years, graph neural networks (GNNs) have been studied extensively and have successfully performed difficult machine learning tasks in node classification, link prediction, and graph classification thanks to the highly expressive capability via message passing in effectively learning graph representations. To solve the graph anomaly detection problem, GNN-based methods leverage information about the graph attributes (or features) and/or structures to learn to score anomalies appropriately. In this survey, we review the recent advances made in detecting graph anomalies using GNN models. Specifically, we summarize GNN-based methods according to the graph type (i.e., static and dynamic), the anomaly type (i.e., node, edge, subgraph, and whole graph), and the network architecture (e.g., graph autoencoder, graph convolutional network). To the best of our knowledge, this survey is the first comprehensive review of graph anomaly detection methods based on GNNs.
summary: Graphs are used widely to model complex systems, and detecting anomalies in a graph is an important task in the analysis of complex systems. Graph anomalies are patterns in a graph that do not conform to normal patterns expected of the attributes and/or structures of the graph. In recent years, graph neural networks (GNNs) have been studied extensively and have successfully performed difficult machine learning tasks in node classification, link prediction, and graph classification thanks to the highly expressive capability via message passing in effectively learning graph representations. To solve the graph anomaly detection problem, GNN-based methods leverage information about the graph attributes (or features) and/or structures to learn to score anomalies appropriately. In this survey, we review the recent advances made in detecting graph anomalies using GNN models. Specifically, we summarize GNN-based methods according to the graph type (i.e., static and dynamic), the anomaly type (i.e., node, edge, subgraph, and whole graph), and the network architecture (e.g., graph autoencoder, graph convolutional network). To the best of our knowledge, this survey is the first comprehensive review of graph anomaly detection methods based on GNNs.
abstract_zh: 图广泛用于对复杂系统进行建模，检测图中的异常是复杂系统分析中的一项重要任务。图异常是图中的模式不符合图的属性和/或结构预期的正常模式。近年来，图神经网络（GNN）得到了广泛的研究，并成功地执行了节点分类、链路预测和图分类等困难的机器学习任务，这得益于通过消息传递有效学习图表示的高表达能力。为了解决图异常检测问题，基于 GNN 的方法利用有关图属性（或特征）和/或结构的信息来学习对异常进行适当的评分。在本次调查中，我们回顾了使用 GNN 模型检测图异常方面取得的最新进展。具体来说，我们根据图类型（即静态和动态）、异常类型（即节点、边、子图和整个图）和网络架构（例如图自动编码器、图卷积网络）总结了基于 GNN 的方法。据我们所知，这项调查是对基于 GNN 的图异常检测方法的首次全面回顾。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了基于图神经网络（GNN）的图异常检测方法的最新进展。
AI.motivation: 图广泛用于建模复杂系统，而检测图异常对于分析复杂系统至关重要。传统方法难以处理图的复杂结构和属性，GNN凭借强大的表达能力成为解决该问题的有力工具。
AI.method: 根据图类型（静态/动态）、异常类型（节点、边、子图、整体图）和网络架构（如图自编码器、图卷积网络）对GNN方法进行分类总结。
AI.result: 系统梳理了GNN在图异常检测领域的研究现状，明确了不同场景下的方法特点与挑战。
AI.conclusion: 本文是首份全面综述GNN基于图异常检测方法的文献，为后续研究提供了方向与参考。

Specifically, we summarize GNN-based methods according to the graph type (i.e., static and dynamic), the anomaly type (i.e., node, edge, subgraph, and whole graph), and the network architecture (e.g., graph autoencoder, graph convolutional network). title: Spotlight: Mobile UI Understanding using Vision-Language Models with a Focus
title_zh: 聚焦：重点使用视觉语言模型进行移动 UI 理解
comment: Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2023
comments: Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2023
abstract: Mobile UI understanding is important for enabling various interaction tasks such as UI automation and accessibility. Previous mobile UI modeling often depends on the view hierarchy information of a screen, which directly provides the structural data of the UI, with the hope to bypass challenging tasks of visual modeling from screen pixels. However, view hierarchies are not always available, and are often corrupted with missing object descriptions or misaligned structure information. As a result, despite the use of view hierarchies could offer short-term gains, it may ultimately hinder the applicability and performance of the model. In this paper, we propose Spotlight, a vision-only approach for mobile UI understanding. Specifically, we enhance a vision-language model that only takes the screenshot of the UI and a region of interest on the screen -- the focus -- as the input. This general architecture of Spotlight is easily scalable and capable of performing a range of UI modeling tasks. Our experiments show that our model establishes SoTA results on several representative UI tasks and outperforms previous methods that use both screenshots and view hierarchies as inputs. Furthermore, we explore multi-task learning and few-shot prompting capacities of the proposed models, demonstrating promising results in the multi-task learning direction.
summary: Mobile UI understanding is important for enabling various interaction tasks such as UI automation and accessibility. Previous mobile UI modeling often depends on the view hierarchy information of a screen, which directly provides the structural data of the UI, with the hope to bypass challenging tasks of visual modeling from screen pixels. However, view hierarchies are not always available, and are often corrupted with missing object descriptions or misaligned structure information. As a result, despite the use of view hierarchies could offer short-term gains, it may ultimately hinder the applicability and performance of the model. In this paper, we propose Spotlight, a vision-only approach for mobile UI understanding. Specifically, we enhance a vision-language model that only takes the screenshot of the UI and a region of interest on the screen -- the focus -- as the input. This general architecture of Spotlight is easily scalable and capable of performing a range of UI modeling tasks. Our experiments show that our model establishes SoTA results on several representative UI tasks and outperforms previous methods that use both screenshots and view hierarchies as inputs. Furthermore, we explore multi-task learning and few-shot prompting capacities of the proposed models, demonstrating promising results in the multi-task learning direction.
abstract_zh: 移动 UI 理解对于实现各种交互任务（例如 UI 自动化和可访问性）非常重要。以前的移动UI建模往往依赖于屏幕的视图层次结构信息，它直接提供UI的结构数据，希望绕过屏幕像素视觉建模的挑战性任务。然而，视图层次结构并不总是可用，并且经常因丢失对象描述或未对齐的结构信息而损坏。因此，尽管使用视图层次结构可以带来短期收益，但它最终可能会阻碍模型的适用性和性能。在本文中，我们提出了 Spotlight，一种用于理解移动 UI 的纯视觉方法。具体来说，我们增强了一种视觉语言模型，该模型仅将 UI 的屏幕截图和屏幕上感兴趣的区域（焦点）作为输入。 Spotlight 的这种通用架构易于扩展，并且能够执行一系列 UI 建模任务。我们的实验表明，我们的模型在几个代表性 UI 任务上建立了 SoTA 结果，并且优于以前使用屏幕截图和视图层次结构作为输入的方法。此外，我们探索了所提出模型的多任务学习和少样本提示能力，展示了多任务学习方向的有希望的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于视觉语言模型的移动用户界面理解方法Spotlight，仅使用屏幕截图和感兴趣区域进行UI建模。
AI.motivation: 传统方法依赖于视图层次结构信息，但这些信息可能缺失或错误，限制了模型的适用性和性能。
AI.method: 提出Spotlight方法，仅使用屏幕截图和感兴趣区域作为输入，增强视觉语言模型以进行UI建模。
AI.result: 实验表明，该模型在多个代表性UI任务中取得了最先进的结果，并优于使用截图和视图层次结构的方法。
AI.conclusion: Spotlight方法在UI建模任务中表现出色，展示了多任务学习和少样本提示的潜力。

title: Domain-Unified Prompt Representations for Source-Free Domain Generalization
title_zh: 用于无源域泛化的域统一提示表示
abstract: Domain generalization (DG), aiming to make models work on unseen domains, is a surefire way toward general artificial intelligence. Limited by the scale and diversity of current DG datasets, it is difficult for existing methods to scale to diverse domains in open-world scenarios (e.g., science fiction and pixelate style). Therefore, the source-free domain generalization (SFDG) task is necessary and challenging. To address this issue, we propose an approach based on large-scale vision-language pretraining models (e.g., CLIP), which exploits the extensive domain information embedded in it. The proposed scheme generates diverse prompts from a domain bank that contains many more diverse domains than existing DG datasets. Furthermore, our method yields domain-unified representations from these prompts, thus being able to cope with samples from open-world domains. Extensive experiments on mainstream DG datasets, namely PACS, VLCS, OfficeHome, and DomainNet, show that the proposed method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art (SOTA) DG methods that require source domain data for training. Besides, we collect a small datasets consists of two domains to evaluate the open-world domain generalization ability of the proposed method. The source code and the dataset will be made publicly available at https://github.com/muse1998/Source-Free-Domain-Generalization
summary: Domain generalization (DG), aiming to make models work on unseen domains, is a surefire way toward general artificial intelligence. Limited by the scale and diversity of current DG datasets, it is difficult for existing methods to scale to diverse domains in open-world scenarios (e.g., science fiction and pixelate style). Therefore, the source-free domain generalization (SFDG) task is necessary and challenging. To address this issue, we propose an approach based on large-scale vision-language pretraining models (e.g., CLIP), which exploits the extensive domain information embedded in it. The proposed scheme generates diverse prompts from a domain bank that contains many more diverse domains than existing DG datasets. Furthermore, our method yields domain-unified representations from these prompts, thus being able to cope with samples from open-world domains. Extensive experiments on mainstream DG datasets, namely PACS, VLCS, OfficeHome, and DomainNet, show that the proposed method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art (SOTA) DG methods that require source domain data for training. Besides, we collect a small datasets consists of two domains to evaluate the open-world domain generalization ability of the proposed method. The source code and the dataset will be made publicly available at https://github.com/muse1998/Source-Free-Domain-Generalization
abstract_zh: 领域泛化（DG）旨在使模型在未见过的领域中发挥作用，是通向通用人工智能的必由之路。受当前 DG 数据集的规模和多样性的限制，现有方法很难扩展到开放世界场景中的不同领域（例如科幻小说和像素化风格）。因此，无源域泛化（SFDG）任务是必要且具有挑战性的。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种基于大规模视觉语言预训练模型（例如 CLIP）的方法，该方法利用了嵌入其中的广泛领域信息。所提出的方案从域库生成不同的提示，该域库包含比现有 DG 数据集更多不同的域。此外，我们的方法根据这些提示生成域统一表示，从而能够处理来自开放世界域的样本。对主流 DG 数据集（即 PACS、VLCS、OfficeHome 和 DomainNet）的大量实验表明，与需要源域数据进行训练的最先进（SOTA）DG 方法相比，所提出的方法实现了有竞争力的性能。此外，我们收集了一个由两个域组成的小数据集，以评估所提出方法的开放世界域泛化能力。源代码和数据集将在 https://github.com/muse1998/Source-Free-Domain-Generalization 上公开提供

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于大规模视觉-语言预训练模型的源自由域泛化方法，通过生成多样化提示实现开放世界域适应。
AI.motivation: 现有域泛化方法因数据集规模和多样性限制，难以应对开放世界中如科幻风格等新型域的挑战，需开发无需源域数据的源自由域泛化方案。
AI.method: 利用CLIP等预训练模型的跨域信息，从包含更多域的领域库生成多样化提示，并提取域统一表示以适应开放世界样本。
AI.result: 在PACS、DomainNet等主流数据集上性能优于需源域数据的SOTA方法，在自建小数据集上验证了开放域泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 通过视觉-语言预训练模型与域统一提示表示，提出的源自由域泛化方法有效提升了模型对开放世界新域的适应能力。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于大规模视觉-语言预训练模型的源自由域泛化方法，通过生成多样化提示实现开放世界域适应。
ai_content.motivation: 现有域泛化方法因数据集规模和多样性限制，难以应对开放世界中如科幻风格等新型域的挑战，需开发无需源域数据的源自由域泛化方案。

ai_content.result: 在PACS、DomainNet等主流数据集上性能优于需源域数据的SOTA方法，在自建小数据集上验证了开放域泛化能力。

search_text: Domain-Unified Prompt Representations for Source-Free Domain Generalization 用于无源域泛化的域统一提示表示 Domain generalization (DG), aiming to make models work on unseen domains, is a surefire way toward general artificial intelligence. Limited by the scale and diversity of current DG datasets, it is difficult for existing methods to scale to diverse domains in open-world scenarios (e.g., science fiction and pixelate style). Therefore, the source-free domain generalization (SFDG) task is necessary and challenging. To address this issue, we propose an approach based on large-scale vision-language pretraining models (e.g., CLIP), which exploits the extensive domain information embedded in it. The proposed scheme generates diverse prompts from a domain bank that contains many more diverse domains than existing DG datasets. Furthermore, our method yields domain-unified representations from these prompts, thus being able to cope with samples from open-world domains. Extensive experiments on mainstream DG datasets, namely PACS, VLCS, OfficeHome, and DomainNet, show that the proposed method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art (SOTA) DG methods that require source domain data for training. Besides, we collect a small datasets consists of two domains to evaluate the open-world domain generalization ability of the proposed method. The source code and the dataset will be made publicly available at https://github.com/muse1998/Source-Free-Domain-Generalization 领域泛化（DG）旨在使模型在未见过的领域中发挥作用，是通向通用人工智能的必由之路。受当前 DG 数据集的规模和多样性的限制，现有方法很难扩展到开放世界场景中的不同领域（例如科幻小说和像素化风格）。因此，无源域泛化（SFDG）任务是必要且具有挑战性的。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种基于大规模视觉语言预训练模型（例如 CLIP）的方法，该方法利用了嵌入其中的广泛领域信息。所提出的方案从域库生成不同的提示，该域库包含比现有 DG 数据集更多不同的域。此外，我们的方法根据这些提示生成域统一表示，从而能够处理来自开放世界域的样本。对主流 DG 数据集（即 PACS、VLCS、OfficeHome 和 DomainNet）的大量实验表明，与需要源域数据进行训练的最先进（SOTA）DG 方法相比，所提出的方法实现了有竞争力的性能。此外，我们收集了一个由两个域组成的小数据集，以评估所提出方法的开放世界域泛化能力。源代码和数据集将在 https://github.com/muse1998/Source-Free-Domain-Generalization 上公开提供 本文提出一种基于大规模视觉-语言预训练模型的源自由域泛化方法，通过生成多样化提示实现开放世界域适应。 Hongjing Niu,Hanting Li,Feng Zhao,Bin Li cs.CV

authors_text: Sanket Kalwar,Dhruv Patel,Aakash Aanegola,Krishna Reddy Konda,Sourav Garg,K Madhava Krishna
title: GDIP: Gated Differentiable Image Processing for Object-Detection in Adverse Conditions
title_zh: GDIP：用于不利条件下目标检测的门控可微分图像处理
comment: Submitted to ICRA2023. More information at https://gatedip.github.io
comments: Submitted to ICRA2023. More information at https://gatedip.github.io
abstract: Detecting objects under adverse weather and lighting conditions is crucial for the safe and continuous operation of an autonomous vehicle, and remains an unsolved problem. We present a Gated Differentiable Image Processing (GDIP) block, a domain-agnostic network architecture, which can be plugged into existing object detection networks (e.g., Yolo) and trained end-to-end with adverse condition images such as those captured under fog and low lighting. Our proposed GDIP block learns to enhance images directly through the downstream object detection loss. This is achieved by learning parameters of multiple image pre-processing (IP) techniques that operate concurrently, with their outputs combined using weights learned through a novel gating mechanism. We further improve GDIP through a multi-stage guidance procedure for progressive image enhancement. Finally, trading off accuracy for speed, we propose a variant of GDIP that can be used as a regularizer for training Yolo, which eliminates the need for GDIP-based image enhancement during inference, resulting in higher throughput and plausible real-world deployment. We demonstrate significant improvement in detection performance over several state-of-the-art methods through quantitative and qualitative studies on synthetic datasets such as PascalVOC, and real-world foggy (RTTS) and low-lighting (ExDark) datasets.
summary: Detecting objects under adverse weather and lighting conditions is crucial for the safe and continuous operation of an autonomous vehicle, and remains an unsolved problem. We present a Gated Differentiable Image Processing (GDIP) block, a domain-agnostic network architecture, which can be plugged into existing object detection networks (e.g., Yolo) and trained end-to-end with adverse condition images such as those captured under fog and low lighting. Our proposed GDIP block learns to enhance images directly through the downstream object detection loss. This is achieved by learning parameters of multiple image pre-processing (IP) techniques that operate concurrently, with their outputs combined using weights learned through a novel gating mechanism. We further improve GDIP through a multi-stage guidance procedure for progressive image enhancement. Finally, trading off accuracy for speed, we propose a variant of GDIP that can be used as a regularizer for training Yolo, which eliminates the need for GDIP-based image enhancement during inference, resulting in higher throughput and plausible real-world deployment. We demonstrate significant improvement in detection performance over several state-of-the-art methods through quantitative and qualitative studies on synthetic datasets such as PascalVOC, and real-world foggy (RTTS) and low-lighting (ExDark) datasets.
abstract_zh: 在恶劣天气和照明条件下检测物体对于自动驾驶车辆的安全和连续运行至关重要，并且仍然是一个尚未解决的问题。我们提出了门控可微图像处理（GDIP）块，这是一种与领域无关的网络架构，可以插入现有的对象检测网络（例如 Yolo），并使用不利条件图像（例如在雾和低光照下捕获的图像）进行端到端训练。我们提出的 GDIP 块学习通过下游对象检测损失直接增强图像。这是通过学习同时运行的多个图像预处理 (IP) 技术的参数来实现的，并使用通过新颖的门控机制学习的权重来组合它们的输出。我们通过渐进式图像增强的多阶段指导程序进一步改进 GDIP。最后，在准确性和速度之间进行权衡，我们提出了一种 GDIP 的变体，可以用作训练 Yolo 的正则化器，从而消除了推理过程中基于 GDIP 的图像增强的需要，从而实现更高的吞吐量和合理的实际部署。通过对 PascalVOC、真实世界雾天 (RTTS) 和低照度 (ExDark) 数据集等合成数据集的定量和定性研究，我们证明了检测性能比几种最先进的方法有了显着提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为GDIP的门控可微图像处理模块，用于在恶劣天气和光照条件下提升目标检测性能。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶车辆在恶劣天气和光照条件下进行目标检测存在重大挑战，现有方法难以有效应对这些场景下的图像退化问题。
AI.method: 设计可插拔的GDIP模块，通过端到端训练学习多种图像预处理技术的参数，并利用门控机制动态组合不同预处理结果，结合多阶段增强策略优化性能。
AI.result: 在PascalVOC、RTTS和ExDark等数据集上，GDIP在检测精度和速度上均优于现有先进方法，且推理阶段无需图像增强即可部署。
AI.conclusion: GDIP通过端到端学习图像增强策略，有效解决了恶劣条件下目标检测难题，为自动驾驶等实际应用提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Guy Tevet,Sigal Raab,Brian Gordon,Yonatan Shafir,Daniel Cohen-Or,Amit H. Bermano
title: Human Motion Diffusion Model
title_zh: 人体运动扩散模型
abstract: Natural and expressive human motion generation is the holy grail of computer animation. It is a challenging task, due to the diversity of possible motion, human perceptual sensitivity to it, and the difficulty of accurately describing it. Therefore, current generative solutions are either low-quality or limited in expressiveness. Diffusion models, which have already shown remarkable generative capabilities in other domains, are promising candidates for human motion due to their many-to-many nature, but they tend to be resource hungry and hard to control. In this paper, we introduce Motion Diffusion Model (MDM), a carefully adapted classifier-free diffusion-based generative model for the human motion domain. MDM is transformer-based, combining insights from motion generation literature. A notable design-choice is the prediction of the sample, rather than the noise, in each diffusion step. This facilitates the use of established geometric losses on the locations and velocities of the motion, such as the foot contact loss. As we demonstrate, MDM is a generic approach, enabling different modes of conditioning, and different generation tasks. We show that our model is trained with lightweight resources and yet achieves state-of-the-art results on leading benchmarks for text-to-motion and action-to-motion. https://guytevet.github.io/mdm-page/ .
summary: Natural and expressive human motion generation is the holy grail of computer animation. It is a challenging task, due to the diversity of possible motion, human perceptual sensitivity to it, and the difficulty of accurately describing it. Therefore, current generative solutions are either low-quality or limited in expressiveness. Diffusion models, which have already shown remarkable generative capabilities in other domains, are promising candidates for human motion due to their many-to-many nature, but they tend to be resource hungry and hard to control. In this paper, we introduce Motion Diffusion Model (MDM), a carefully adapted classifier-free diffusion-based generative model for the human motion domain. MDM is transformer-based, combining insights from motion generation literature. A notable design-choice is the prediction of the sample, rather than the noise, in each diffusion step. This facilitates the use of established geometric losses on the locations and velocities of the motion, such as the foot contact loss. As we demonstrate, MDM is a generic approach, enabling different modes of conditioning, and different generation tasks. We show that our model is trained with lightweight resources and yet achieves state-of-the-art results on leading benchmarks for text-to-motion and action-to-motion. https://guytevet.github.io/mdm-page/ .
abstract_zh: 自然而富有表现力的人体动作生成是计算机动画的圣杯。由于可能的运动的多样性、人类对运动的感知敏感性以及准确描述运动的难度，这是一项具有挑战性的任务。因此，当前的生成解决方案要么质量低下，要么表达能力有限。扩散模型已经在其他领域表现出了卓越的生成能力，由于其多对多的性质，是人类运动的有希望的候选者，但它们往往资源匮乏且难以控制。在本文中，我们介绍了运动扩散模型（MDM），这是一种针对人体运动领域精心调整的基于无分类器扩散的生成模型。 MDM 基于 Transformer，结合了运动生成文献中的见解。一个值得注意的设计选择是在每个扩散步骤中预测样本，而不是噪声。这有利于使用已建立的运动位置和速度的几何损失，例如脚接触损失。正如我们所演示的，MDM 是一种通用方法，支持不同的调节模式和不同的生成任务。我们表明，我们的模型是使用轻量级资源进行训练的，但在文本到运动和动作到运动的领先基准上取得了最先进的结果。 https://guytevet.github.io/mdm-page/ 。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Motion Diffusion Model (MDM)的生成模型，用于解决人类动作生成中的挑战性问题。
AI.motivation: 当前生成方法在质量或表现力上存在局限，而扩散模型虽具潜力但资源消耗大且难以控制，需改进以适应人类动作生成需求。
AI.method: 基于Transformer架构的无分类器扩散模型，通过预测样本而非噪声，并结合几何损失（如足部接触损失）优化动作生成。
AI.result: 在文本到动作和动作到动作的基准测试中达到最先进水平，且训练资源消耗较低。
AI.conclusion: MDM是一种通用方法，支持多种条件生成模式，验证了其在效率与效果上的优势。

Diffusion models, which have already shown remarkable generative capabilities in other domains, are promising candidates for human motion due to their many-to-many nature, but they tend to be resource hungry and hard to control. In this paper, we introduce Motion Diffusion Model (MDM), a carefully adapted classifier-free diffusion-based generative model for the human motion domain. MDM is transformer-based, combining insights from motion generation literature. A notable design-choice is the prediction of the sample, rather than the noise, in each diffusion step. This facilitates the use of established geometric losses on the locations and velocities of the motion, such as the foot contact loss. As we demonstrate, MDM is a generic approach, enabling different modes of conditioning, and different generation tasks. We show that our model is trained with lightweight resources and yet achieves state-of-the-art results on leading benchmarks for text-to-motion and action-to-motion. https://guytevet.github.io/mdm-page/ . 自然而富有表现力的人体动作生成是计算机动画的圣杯。由于可能的运动的多样性、人类对运动的感知敏感性以及准确描述运动的难度，这是一项具有挑战性的任务。因此，当前的生成解决方案要么质量低下，要么表达能力有限。扩散模型已经在其他领域表现出了卓越的生成能力，由于其多对多的性质，是人类运动的有希望的候选者，但它们往往资源匮乏且难以控制。在本文中，我们介绍了运动扩散模型（MDM），这是一种针对人体运动领域精心调整的基于无分类器扩散的生成模型。 MDM 基于 Transformer，结合了运动生成文献中的见解。一个值得注意的设计选择是在每个扩散步骤中预测样本，而不是噪声。这有利于使用已建立的运动位置和速度的几何损失，例如脚接触损失。正如我们所演示的，MDM 是一种通用方法，支持不同的调节模式和不同的生成任务。我们表明，我们的模型是使用轻量级资源进行训练的，但在文本到运动和动作到运动的领先基准上取得了最先进的结果。 https://guytevet.github.io/mdm-page/ 。 本文提出了一种名为Motion Diffusion Model (MDM)的生成模型，用于解决人类动作生成中的挑战性问题。 Guy Tevet,Sigal Raab,Brian Gordon,Yonatan Shafir,Daniel Cohen-Or,Amit H. Bermano cs.CV,cs.GR

authors_text: Alex Vicente-Sola,Davide L. Manna,Paul Kirkland,Gaetano Di Caterina,Trevor Bihl
title: Spiking Neural Networks for event-based action recognition: A new task to understand their advantage
title_zh: 用于基于事件的动作识别的尖峰神经网络：了解其优势的新任务
comment: Updated version: Added extra clarifications and references
comments: Updated version: Added extra clarifications and references
abstract: Spiking Neural Networks (SNN) are characterised by their unique temporal dynamics, but the properties and advantages of such computations are still not well understood. In order to provide answers, in this work we demonstrate how Spiking neurons can enable temporal feature extraction in feed-forward neural networks without the need for recurrent synapses, and how recurrent SNNs can achieve comparable results to LSTM with a smaller number of parameters. This shows how their bio-inspired computing principles can be successfully exploited beyond energy efficiency gains and evidences their differences with respect to conventional artificial neural networks. These results are obtained through a new task, DVS-Gesture-Chain (DVS-GC), which allows, for the first time, to evaluate the perception of temporal dependencies in a real event-based action recognition dataset. Our study proves how the widely used DVS Gesture benchmark can be solved by networks without temporal feature extraction when its events are accumulated in frames, unlike the new DVS-GC which demands an understanding of the order in which events happen. Furthermore, this setup allowed us to reveal the role of the leakage rate in spiking neurons for temporal processing tasks and demonstrated the benefits of "hard reset" mechanisms. Additionally, we also show how time-dependent weights and normalization can lead to understanding order by means of temporal attention.
summary: Spiking Neural Networks (SNN) are characterised by their unique temporal dynamics, but the properties and advantages of such computations are still not well understood. In order to provide answers, in this work we demonstrate how Spiking neurons can enable temporal feature extraction in feed-forward neural networks without the need for recurrent synapses, and how recurrent SNNs can achieve comparable results to LSTM with a smaller number of parameters. This shows how their bio-inspired computing principles can be successfully exploited beyond energy efficiency gains and evidences their differences with respect to conventional artificial neural networks. These results are obtained through a new task, DVS-Gesture-Chain (DVS-GC), which allows, for the first time, to evaluate the perception of temporal dependencies in a real event-based action recognition dataset. Our study proves how the widely used DVS Gesture benchmark can be solved by networks without temporal feature extraction when its events are accumulated in frames, unlike the new DVS-GC which demands an understanding of the order in which events happen. Furthermore, this setup allowed us to reveal the role of the leakage rate in spiking neurons for temporal processing tasks and demonstrated the benefits of "hard reset" mechanisms. Additionally, we also show how time-dependent weights and normalization can lead to understanding order by means of temporal attention.
abstract_zh: 尖峰神经网络（SNN）的特点是其独特的时间动态，但这种计算的属性和优势仍然没有得到很好的理解。为了提供答案，在这项工作中，我们演示了尖峰神经元如何在前馈神经网络中实现时间特征提取而无需循环突触，以及循环 SNN 如何使用更少的参数实现与 LSTM 相当的结果。这展示了他们的仿生计算原理如何在能源效率增益之外成功利用，并证明了它们与传统人工神经网络的差异。这些结果是通过一项新任务 DVS-Gesture-Chain (DVS-GC) 获得的，该任务首次允许评估真实的基于事件的动作识别数据集中对时间依赖性的感知。我们的研究证明了广泛使用的 DVS 手势基准如何通过网络在其事件累积在帧中时无需时间特征提取来解决，这与需要了解事件发生顺序的新 DVS-GC 不同。此外，这种设置使我们能够揭示泄漏率在尖峰神经元在时间处理任务中的作用，并证明了“硬重置”机制的好处。此外，我们还展示了时间相关权重和归一化如何通过时间注意力来理解顺序。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新的事件驱动动作识别任务DVS-Gesture-Chain（DVS-GC），用于评估脉冲神经网络（SNN）在时间依赖性感知中的优势。
AI.motivation: 尽管SNN具有独特的时序动态特性，但其计算优势尚未被充分理解，尤其在事件驱动场景中如何超越传统神经网络尚需验证。
AI.method: 设计DVS-GC数据集，通过对比SNN与LSTM在该任务中的表现，分析SNN的时序特征提取能力，并研究泄漏率、硬重置机制等参数对时间感知的影响。
AI.result: SNN在DVS-GC任务中以更少参数达到与LSTM相当的效果，证明其生物启发计算原理在时序任务中的有效性，并揭示了泄漏率和硬重置机制的关键作用。
AI.conclusion: SNN在事件驱动动作识别中展现出超越传统网络的时序处理潜力，其优势不仅体现在能效上，更在于对事件发生顺序的感知能力。

title: Graph Modeling in Computer Assisted Automotive Development
title_zh: 计算机辅助汽车开发中的图形建模
abstract: We consider graph modeling for a knowledge graph for vehicle development, with a focus on crash safety. An organized schema that incorporates information from various structured and unstructured data sources is provided, which includes relevant concepts within the domain. In particular, we propose semantics for crash computer aided engineering (CAE) data, which enables searchability, filtering, recommendation, and prediction for crash CAE data during the development process. This graph modeling considers the CAE data in the context of the R\&D development process and vehicle safety. Consequently, we connect CAE data to the protocols that are used to assess vehicle safety performances. The R\&D process includes CAD engineering and safety attributes, with a focus on multidisciplinary problem-solving. We describe previous efforts in graph modeling in comparison to our proposal, discuss its strengths and limitations, and identify areas for future work.
summary: We consider graph modeling for a knowledge graph for vehicle development, with a focus on crash safety. An organized schema that incorporates information from various structured and unstructured data sources is provided, which includes relevant concepts within the domain. In particular, we propose semantics for crash computer aided engineering (CAE) data, which enables searchability, filtering, recommendation, and prediction for crash CAE data during the development process. This graph modeling considers the CAE data in the context of the R\&D development process and vehicle safety. Consequently, we connect CAE data to the protocols that are used to assess vehicle safety performances. The R\&D process includes CAD engineering and safety attributes, with a focus on multidisciplinary problem-solving. We describe previous efforts in graph modeling in comparison to our proposal, discuss its strengths and limitations, and identify areas for future work.
abstract_zh: 我们考虑对车辆开发的知识图进行图建模，重点关注碰撞安全。提供了一个有组织的模式，它合并了来自各种结构化和非结构化数据源的信息，其中包括域内的相关概念。特别是，我们提出了碰撞计算机辅助工程（CAE）数据的语义，这使得在开发过程中能够搜索、过滤、推荐和预测碰撞CAE数据。该图形建模考虑了研发过程和车辆安全背景下的 CAE 数据。因此，我们将 CAE 数据连接到用于评估车辆安全性能的协议。研发过程包括 CAD 工程和安全属性，重点是解决多学科问题。我们描述了之前在图建模方面的努力与我们的建议的比较，讨论了它的优点和局限性，并确定了未来工作的领域。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种用于汽车开发的知识图谱建模方法，重点针对碰撞安全领域，整合多源数据并增强CAE数据的可操作性。
AI.motivation: 传统汽车研发中碰撞安全数据分散且难以高效利用，需通过知识图谱整合多源异构数据以支持多学科协同研发。
AI.method: 构建包含结构化与非结构化数据的图谱模式，定义碰撞CAE数据语义，并将其与安全评估协议及研发流程中的CAD工程关联。
AI.result: 实现了CAE数据的搜索、过滤、推荐与预测功能，建立了数据与安全评估标准的关联，优化了研发流程中的多学科问题解决能力。
AI.conclusion: 该图谱模型提升了碰撞安全分析效率，但需进一步验证其在复杂场景下的适用性，并探索更多自动化应用方向。

authors_text: A. N. M. Sajedul Alam,Rimi Reza,Asir Abrar,Tanvir Ahmed,Salsabil Ahmed,Shihab Sharar,Annajiat Alim Rasel
title: Patients' Severity States Classification based on Electronic Health Record (EHR) Data using Multiple Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approaches
title_zh: 使用多种机器学习和深度学习方法，基于电子健康记录 (EHR) 数据对患者的严重程度进行分类
abstract: This research presents an examination of categorizing the severity states of patients based on their electronic health records during a certain time range using multiple machine learning and deep learning approaches. The suggested method uses an EHR dataset collected from an open-source platform to categorize severity. Some tools were used in this research, such as openRefine was used to pre-process, RapidMiner was used for implementing three algorithms (Fast Large Margin, Generalized Linear Model, Multi-layer Feed-forward Neural Network) and Tableau was used to visualize the data, for implementation of algorithms we used Google Colab. Here we implemented several supervised and unsupervised algorithms along with semi-supervised and deep learning algorithms. The experimental results reveal that hyperparameter-tuned Random Forest outperformed all the other supervised machine learning algorithms with 76% accuracy as well as Generalized Linear algorithm achieved the highest precision score 78%, whereas the hyperparameter-tuned Hierarchical Clustering with 86% precision score and Gaussian Mixture Model with 61% accuracy outperformed other unsupervised approaches. Dimensionality Reduction improved results a lot for most unsupervised techniques. For implementing Deep Learning we employed a feed-forward neural network (multi-layer) and the Fast Large Margin approach for semi-supervised learning. The Fast Large Margin performed really well with a recall score of 84% and an F1 score of 78%. Finally, the Multi-layer Feed-forward Neural Network performed admirably with 75% accuracy, 75% precision, 87% recall, 81% F1 score.
summary: This research presents an examination of categorizing the severity states of patients based on their electronic health records during a certain time range using multiple machine learning and deep learning approaches. The suggested method uses an EHR dataset collected from an open-source platform to categorize severity. Some tools were used in this research, such as openRefine was used to pre-process, RapidMiner was used for implementing three algorithms (Fast Large Margin, Generalized Linear Model, Multi-layer Feed-forward Neural Network) and Tableau was used to visualize the data, for implementation of algorithms we used Google Colab. Here we implemented several supervised and unsupervised algorithms along with semi-supervised and deep learning algorithms. The experimental results reveal that hyperparameter-tuned Random Forest outperformed all the other supervised machine learning algorithms with 76% accuracy as well as Generalized Linear algorithm achieved the highest precision score 78%, whereas the hyperparameter-tuned Hierarchical Clustering with 86% precision score and Gaussian Mixture Model with 61% accuracy outperformed other unsupervised approaches. Dimensionality Reduction improved results a lot for most unsupervised techniques. For implementing Deep Learning we employed a feed-forward neural network (multi-layer) and the Fast Large Margin approach for semi-supervised learning. The Fast Large Margin performed really well with a recall score of 84% and an F1 score of 78%. Finally, the Multi-layer Feed-forward Neural Network performed admirably with 75% accuracy, 75% precision, 87% recall, 81% F1 score.
abstract_zh: 这项研究提出了一种检查，使用多种机器学习和深度学习方法，根据特定时间范围内的电子健康记录对患者的严重程度进行分类。建议的方法使用从开源平台收集的 EHR 数据集来对严重程度进行分类。本研究中使用了一些工具，例如openRefine用于预处理，RapidMiner用于实现三种算法（快速大裕度，广义线性模型，多层前馈神经网络），Tableau用于数据可视化，算法的实现我们使用Google Colab。在这里，我们实现了几种监督和无监督算法以及半监督和深度学习算法。实验结果表明，超参数调整的随机森林以 76% 的准确率优于所有其他有监督机器学习算法，广义线性算法实现了最高的精度分数 78%，而超参数调整的层次聚类以 86% 的精度分数和高斯混合模型以 61% 的准确率优于其他无监督方法。对于大多数无监督技术来说，降维大大改善了结果。为了实现深度学习，我们采用了前馈神经网络（多层）和快速大裕度方法进行半监督学习。 Fast Large Margin 的表现非常好，召回率为 84%，F1 得分为 78%。最后，多层前馈神经网络表现出色，准确率 75%，精确度 75%，召回率 87%，F1 分数 81%。

AI.tldr: 该论文旨在利用电子健康记录（EHR）数据，通过多种机器学习和深度学习方法对患者病情严重程度进行分类。
AI.motivation: 通过准确分类患者病情严重程度，可优化医疗资源分配并提升临床决策效率，但现有方法在复杂EHR数据上的表现仍有待改进。
AI.method: 使用开源EHR数据集，结合openRefine预处理、RapidMiner实现算法（如随机森林、广义线性模型、多层神经网络）、Tableau可视化及Google Colab部署。对比监督、非监督、半监督及深度学习方法，并应用降维技术优化结果。
AI.result: 调参后的随机森林准确率达76%，广义线性模型精确率为78%；非监督方法中层次聚类精确率86%，高斯混合模型准确率61%；深度学习模型多层神经网络表现最佳（准确率75%，召回率87%，F1 81%）。
AI.conclusion: 调参的随机森林和深度学习模型在病情分类中表现优异，降维技术显著提升非监督方法效果，未来可进一步探索模型融合与实时数据处理。

search_text: Patients' Severity States Classification based on Electronic Health Record (EHR) Data using Multiple Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approaches 使用多种机器学习和深度学习方法，基于电子健康记录 (EHR) 数据对患者的严重程度进行分类 This research presents an examination of categorizing the severity states of patients based on their electronic health records during a certain time range using multiple machine learning and deep learning approaches. The suggested method uses an EHR dataset collected from an open-source platform to categorize severity. Some tools were used in this research, such as openRefine was used to pre-process, RapidMiner was used for implementing three algorithms (Fast Large Margin, Generalized Linear Model, Multi-layer Feed-forward Neural Network) and Tableau was used to visualize the data, for implementation of algorithms we used Google Colab. Here we implemented several supervised and unsupervised algorithms along with semi-supervised and deep learning algorithms. The experimental results reveal that hyperparameter-tuned Random Forest outperformed all the other supervised machine learning algorithms with 76% accuracy as well as Generalized Linear algorithm achieved the highest precision score 78%, whereas the hyperparameter-tuned Hierarchical Clustering with 86% precision score and Gaussian Mixture Model with 61% accuracy outperformed other unsupervised approaches. Dimensionality Reduction improved results a lot for most unsupervised techniques. For implementing Deep Learning we employed a feed-forward neural network (multi-layer) and the Fast Large Margin approach for semi-supervised learning. The Fast Large Margin performed really well with a recall score of 84% and an F1 score of 78%. Finally, the Multi-layer Feed-forward Neural Network performed admirably with 75% accuracy, 75% precision, 87% recall, 81% F1 score. 这项研究提出了一种检查，使用多种机器学习和深度学习方法，根据特定时间范围内的电子健康记录对患者的严重程度进行分类。建议的方法使用从开源平台收集的 EHR 数据集来对严重程度进行分类。本研究中使用了一些工具，例如openRefine用于预处理，RapidMiner用于实现三种算法（快速大裕度，广义线性模型，多层前馈神经网络），Tableau用于数据可视化，算法的实现我们使用Google Colab。在这里，我们实现了几种监督和无监督算法以及半监督和深度学习算法。实验结果表明，超参数调整的随机森林以 76% 的准确率优于所有其他有监督机器学习算法，广义线性算法实现了最高的精度分数 78%，而超参数调整的层次聚类以 86% 的精度分数和高斯混合模型以 61% 的准确率优于其他无监督方法。对于大多数无监督技术来说，降维大大改善了结果。为了实现深度学习，我们采用了前馈神经网络（多层）和快速大裕度方法进行半监督学习。 Fast Large Margin 的表现非常好，召回率为 84%，F1 得分为 78%。最后，多层前馈神经网络表现出色，准确率 75%，精确度 75%，召回率 87%，F1 分数 81%。 该论文旨在利用电子健康记录（EHR）数据，通过多种机器学习和深度学习方法对患者病情严重程度进行分类。 A. authors_text: Yanjun Gao,Dmitriy Dligach,Timothy Miller,John Caskey,Brihat Sharma,Matthew M Churpek,Majid Afshar
title: DR.BENCH: Diagnostic Reasoning Benchmark for Clinical Natural Language Processing
title_zh: DR.BENCH：临床自然语言处理的诊断推理基准
abstract: The meaningful use of electronic health records (EHR) continues to progress in the digital era with clinical decision support systems augmented by artificial intelligence. A priority in improving provider experience is to overcome information overload and reduce the cognitive burden so fewer medical errors and cognitive biases are introduced during patient care. One major type of medical error is diagnostic error due to systematic or predictable errors in judgment that rely on heuristics. The potential for clinical natural language processing (cNLP) to model diagnostic reasoning in humans with forward reasoning from data to diagnosis and potentially reduce the cognitive burden and medical error has not been investigated. Existing tasks to advance the science in cNLP have largely focused on information extraction and named entity recognition through classification tasks. We introduce a novel suite of tasks coined as Diagnostic Reasoning Benchmarks, DR.BENCH, as a new benchmark for developing and evaluating cNLP models with clinical diagnostic reasoning ability. The suite includes six tasks from ten publicly available datasets addressing clinical text understanding, medical knowledge reasoning, and diagnosis generation. DR.BENCH is the first clinical suite of tasks designed to be a natural language generation framework to evaluate pre-trained language models. Experiments with state-of-the-art pre-trained generative language models using large general domain models and models that were continually trained on a medical corpus demonstrate opportunities for improvement when evaluated in DR. BENCH. We share DR. BENCH as a publicly available GitLab repository with a systematic approach to load and evaluate models for the cNLP community.
summary: The meaningful use of electronic health records (EHR) continues to progress in the digital era with clinical decision support systems augmented by artificial intelligence. A priority in improving provider experience is to overcome information overload and reduce the cognitive burden so fewer medical errors and cognitive biases are introduced during patient care. One major type of medical error is diagnostic error due to systematic or predictable errors in judgment that rely on heuristics. The potential for clinical natural language processing (cNLP) to model diagnostic reasoning in humans with forward reasoning from data to diagnosis and potentially reduce the cognitive burden and medical error has not been investigated. Existing tasks to advance the science in cNLP have largely focused on information extraction and named entity recognition through classification tasks. We introduce a novel suite of tasks coined as Diagnostic Reasoning Benchmarks, DR.BENCH, as a new benchmark for developing and evaluating cNLP models with clinical diagnostic reasoning ability. The suite includes six tasks from ten publicly available datasets addressing clinical text understanding, medical knowledge reasoning, and diagnosis generation. DR.BENCH is the first clinical suite of tasks designed to be a natural language generation framework to evaluate pre-trained language models. Experiments with state-of-the-art pre-trained generative language models using large general domain models and models that were continually trained on a medical corpus demonstrate opportunities for improvement when evaluated in DR. BENCH. We share DR. BENCH as a publicly available GitLab repository with a systematic approach to load and evaluate models for the cNLP community.
abstract_zh: 随着人工智能增强的临床决策支持系统，电子健康记录 (EHR) 的有意义的使用在数字时代继续取得进展。改善提供者体验的首要任务是克服信息过载并减轻认知负担，从而减少患者护理过程中引入的医疗错误和认知偏差。医疗错误的一种主要类型是由于依赖启发法的判断中的系统性或可预测错误而导致的诊断错误。临床自然语言处理 (cNLP) 通过从数据到诊断的正向推理来模拟人类诊断推理，并可能减少认知负担和医疗错误，这一潜力尚未得到研究。推进 cNLP 科学发展的现有任务主要集中在通过分类任务进行信息提取和命名实体识别。我们引入了一套新颖的任务，称为诊断推理基准 DR.BENCH，作为开发和评估具有临床诊断推理能力的 cNLP 模型的新基准。该套件包括来自十个公开数据集的六项任务，涉及临床文本理解、医学知识推理和诊断生成。 DR.BENCH 是第一个临床任务套件，旨在成为评估预训练语言模型的自然语言生成框架。使用大型通用领域模型和在医学语料库上持续训练的模型对最先进的预训练生成语言模型进行的实验表明，在 DR 中进行评估时有改进的机会。长椅。我们共享 DR。 BENCH 是一个公开可用的 GitLab 存储库，具有为 cNLP 社区加载和评估模型的系统方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DR.BENCH，一个用于评估临床自然语言处理（cNLP）模型诊断推理能力的新基准测试套件。
AI.motivation: 现有cNLP任务主要聚焦信息提取和命名实体识别，缺乏对诊断推理能力的评估，而诊断错误是医疗事故的主要原因之一。
AI.method: 构建包含六个任务的基准套件，涵盖临床文本理解、医学知识推理和诊断生成，基于十个公开数据集，并设计为自然语言生成框架评估预训练模型。
AI.result: 实验表明，当前最先进的预训练生成模型在DR.BENCH上仍有改进空间，验证了该基准的有效性。
AI.conclusion: DR.BENCH是首个面向临床诊断推理的基准测试，为cNLP社区提供公开的模型评估框架和系统化方法。

ai_content.result: 实验表明，当前最先进的预训练生成模型在DR.BENCH上仍有改进空间，验证了该基准的有效性。
ai_content.conclusion: DR.BENCH是首个面向临床诊断推理的基准测试，为cNLP社区提供公开的模型评估框架和系统化方法。

authors_text: Yunliang Jiang,Chenyang Gu,Zhenfeng Xue,Xiongtao Zhang,Yong Liu
title: Mask-Guided Image Person Removal with Data Synthesis
title_zh: 通过数据合成进行掩模引导图像人物去除
abstract: As a special case of common object removal, image person removal is playing an increasingly important role in social media and criminal investigation domains. Due to the integrity of person area and the complexity of human posture, person removal has its own dilemmas. In this paper, we propose a novel idea to tackle these problems from the perspective of data synthesis. Concerning the lack of dedicated dataset for image person removal, two dataset production methods are proposed to automatically generate images, masks and ground truths respectively. Then, a learning framework similar to local image degradation is proposed so that the masks can be used to guide the feature extraction process and more texture information can be gathered for final prediction. A coarse-to-fine training strategy is further applied to refine the details. The data synthesis and learning framework combine well with each other. Experimental results verify the effectiveness of our method quantitatively and qualitatively, and the trained network proves to have good generalization ability either on real or synthetic images.
summary: As a special case of common object removal, image person removal is playing an increasingly important role in social media and criminal investigation domains. Due to the integrity of person area and the complexity of human posture, person removal has its own dilemmas. In this paper, we propose a novel idea to tackle these problems from the perspective of data synthesis. Concerning the lack of dedicated dataset for image person removal, two dataset production methods are proposed to automatically generate images, masks and ground truths respectively. Then, a learning framework similar to local image degradation is proposed so that the masks can be used to guide the feature extraction process and more texture information can be gathered for final prediction. A coarse-to-fine training strategy is further applied to refine the details. The data synthesis and learning framework combine well with each other. Experimental results verify the effectiveness of our method quantitatively and qualitatively, and the trained network proves to have good generalization ability either on real or synthetic images.
abstract_zh: 作为常见物体去除的一个特例，图像人物去除在社交媒体和刑事调查领域发挥着越来越重要的作用。由于人体区域的完整性和人体姿态的复杂性，人体去除有其自身的困境。在本文中，我们提出了一种从数据合成的角度来解决这些问题的新想法。鉴于缺乏用于图像人物去除的专用数据集，提出了两种数据集生成方法来分别自动生成图像、掩模和地面实况。然后，提出了一种类似于局部图像退化的学习框架，以便可以使用掩模来指导特征提取过程，并可以收集更多纹理信息以进行最终预测。进一步应用从粗到细的训练策略来细化细节。数据合成和学习框架很好地结合在一起。实验结果定量和定性地验证了我们方法的有效性，并且训练后的网络在真实图像或合成图像上都具有良好的泛化能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于数据合成与掩码引导的图像人物移除方法，通过生成合成数据集和改进学习框架解决人物区域完整性与姿态复杂性难题。
AI.motivation: 现有数据集不足且人物移除面临区域完整性与姿态复杂性挑战，需通过数据合成与新型学习框架提升移除效果。
AI.method: 提出两种数据集生成方法合成图像/掩码/真实值，设计掩码引导的局部退化学习框架，结合粗到细训练策略优化细节。
AI.result: 实验验证方法在定量与定性指标上有效，训练网络对真实与合成图像均具有良好的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 数据合成与学习框架的有效结合解决了人物移除难题，方法在多个维度表现优异。

A coarse-to-fine training strategy is further applied to refine the details. The data synthesis and learning framework combine well with each other. title: Learning Low-Frequency Motion Control for Robust and Dynamic Robot Locomotion
title_zh: 学习低频运动控制以实现稳健和动态的机器人运动
abstract: Robotic locomotion is often approached with the goal of maximizing robustness and reactivity by increasing motion control frequency. We challenge this intuitive notion by demonstrating robust and dynamic locomotion with a learned motion controller executing at as low as 8 Hz on a real ANYmal C quadruped. The robot is able to robustly and repeatably achieve a high heading velocity of 1.5 m/s, traverse uneven terrain, and resist unexpected external perturbations. We further present a comparative analysis of deep reinforcement learning (RL) based motion control policies trained and executed at frequencies ranging from 5 Hz to 200 Hz. We show that low-frequency policies are less sensitive to actuation latencies and variations in system dynamics. This is to the extent that a successful sim-to-real transfer can be performed even without any dynamics randomization or actuation modeling. We support this claim through a set of rigorous empirical evaluations. Moreover, to assist reproducibility, we provide the training and deployment code along with an extended analysis at https://ori-drs.github.io/lfmc/.
summary: Robotic locomotion is often approached with the goal of maximizing robustness and reactivity by increasing motion control frequency. We challenge this intuitive notion by demonstrating robust and dynamic locomotion with a learned motion controller executing at as low as 8 Hz on a real ANYmal C quadruped. The robot is able to robustly and repeatably achieve a high heading velocity of 1.5 m/s, traverse uneven terrain, and resist unexpected external perturbations. We further present a comparative analysis of deep reinforcement learning (RL) based motion control policies trained and executed at frequencies ranging from 5 Hz to 200 Hz. We show that low-frequency policies are less sensitive to actuation latencies and variations in system dynamics. This is to the extent that a successful sim-to-real transfer can be performed even without any dynamics randomization or actuation modeling. We support this claim through a set of rigorous empirical evaluations. Moreover, to assist reproducibility, we provide the training and deployment code along with an extended analysis at https://ori-drs.github.io/lfmc/.
abstract_zh: 机器人运动的目标通常是通过增加运动控制频率来最大化鲁棒性和反应性。我们通过在真正的 ANYmal C 四足动物上以低至 8 Hz 的频率执行的学习运动控制器来展示稳健和动态的运动，从而挑战这一直观概念。该机器人能够稳健且可重复地实现 1.5 m/s 的高航向速度，穿越不平坦的地形，并抵抗意外的外部扰动。我们进一步对基于深度强化学习 (RL) 的运动控制策略进行了比较分析，这些策略以 5 Hz 至 200 Hz 的频率进行训练和执行。我们表明，低频策略对驱动延迟和系统动态变化不太敏感。即使没有任何动态随机化或驱动建模，也可以成功执行模拟到真实的转换。我们通过一系列严格的实证评估来支持这一主张。此外，为了提高可重复性，我们在 https://ori-drs.github.io/lfmc/ 上提供了培训和部署代码以及扩展分析。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种低频运动控制方法，使四足机器人在8Hz低频下实现稳健且动态的行走，并验证其在复杂环境中的有效性。
AI.motivation: 传统机器人运动控制依赖高频控制以提高鲁棒性，但高频控制存在计算开销大、对系统延迟敏感等问题，本文挑战这一假设，探索低频控制的可行性。
AI.method: 基于深度强化学习训练不同频率（5Hz-200Hz）的运动策略，通过实验证明低频策略对执行延迟和动态变化的鲁棒性，并实现仿真到现实的迁移。
AI.result: 低频策略在8Hz下成功实现1.5m/s高速行走、复杂地形穿越和抗干扰能力，且无需动力学随机化即可完成sim-to-real迁移。
AI.conclusion: 低频运动控制在保证动态性能的同时更鲁棒，为资源受限场景下的机器人控制提供新思路，代码与分析已开源促进复现。

authors_text: Bobak T. Kiani,Randall Balestriero,Yubei Chen,Seth Lloyd,Yann LeCun
title: Joint Embedding Self-Supervised Learning in the Kernel Regime
title_zh: 在内核机制中联合嵌入自监督学习
abstract: The fundamental goal of self-supervised learning (SSL) is to produce useful representations of data without access to any labels for classifying the data. Modern methods in SSL, which form representations based on known or constructed relationships between samples, have been particularly effective at this task. Here, we aim to extend this framework to incorporate algorithms based on kernel methods where embeddings are constructed by linear maps acting on the feature space of a kernel. In this kernel regime, we derive methods to find the optimal form of the output representations for contrastive and non-contrastive loss functions. This procedure produces a new representation space with an inner product denoted as the induced kernel which generally correlates points which are related by an augmentation in kernel space and de-correlates points otherwise. We analyze our kernel model on small datasets to identify common features of self-supervised learning algorithms and gain theoretical insights into their performance on downstream tasks.
summary: The fundamental goal of self-supervised learning (SSL) is to produce useful representations of data without access to any labels for classifying the data. Modern methods in SSL, which form representations based on known or constructed relationships between samples, have been particularly effective at this task. Here, we aim to extend this framework to incorporate algorithms based on kernel methods where embeddings are constructed by linear maps acting on the feature space of a kernel. In this kernel regime, we derive methods to find the optimal form of the output representations for contrastive and non-contrastive loss functions. This procedure produces a new representation space with an inner product denoted as the induced kernel which generally correlates points which are related by an augmentation in kernel space and de-correlates points otherwise. We analyze our kernel model on small datasets to identify common features of self-supervised learning algorithms and gain theoretical insights into their performance on downstream tasks.
abstract_zh: 自监督学习 (SSL) 的基本目标是生成有用的数据表示，而无需访问任何标签来对数据进行分类。 SSL 中的现代方法根据样本之间已知或构建的关系形成表示，在这项任务中特别有效。在这里，我们的目标是扩展这个框架，以合并基于内核方法的算法，其中嵌入是通过作用于内核特征空间的线性映射来构造的。在这个内核机制中，我们推导了找到对比和非对比损失函数的输出表示的最佳形式的方法。该过程产生一个新的表示空间，其内积表示为导出核，该导出核通常与通过核空间中的增强相关的点进行关联，否则对点进行去相关。我们在小数据集上分析我们的内核模型，以识别自监督学习算法的共同特征，并获得对其在下游任务上的性能的理论见解。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在将自监督学习框架扩展到核方法，通过在核特征空间中使用线性映射构造嵌入，推导对比和非对比损失函数的最优表示形式。
AI.motivation: 现有自监督学习方法未充分利用核方法的潜力，而核方法在处理复杂数据关系时具有优势，需探索其在SSL中的应用。
AI.method: 在核特征空间中构造嵌入，通过线性映射推导对比和非对比损失函数的最优输出表示，并定义诱导核内积以关联增强数据点。
AI.result: 在小数据集上验证了方法的有效性，揭示了SSL算法的共性特征，并为下游任务性能提供了理论解释。
AI.conclusion: 核方法为自监督学习提供了新的表示空间，通过诱导核内积增强相关性，理论上提升了下游任务的表现。

search_text: Joint Embedding Self-Supervised Learning in the Kernel Regime 在内核机制中联合嵌入自监督学习 The fundamental goal of self-supervised learning (SSL) is to produce useful representations of data without access to any labels for classifying the data. Modern methods in SSL, which form representations based on known or constructed relationships between samples, have been particularly effective at this task. Here, we aim to extend this framework to incorporate algorithms based on kernel methods where embeddings are constructed by linear maps acting on the feature space of a kernel. In this kernel regime, we derive methods to find the optimal form of the output representations for contrastive and non-contrastive loss functions. This procedure produces a new representation space with an inner product denoted as the induced kernel which generally correlates points which are related by an augmentation in kernel space and de-correlates points otherwise. We analyze our kernel model on small datasets to identify common features of self-supervised learning algorithms and gain theoretical insights into their performance on downstream tasks. 自监督学习 (SSL) 的基本目标是生成有用的数据表示，而无需访问任何标签来对数据进行分类。 SSL 中的现代方法根据样本之间已知或构建的关系形成表示，在这项任务中特别有效。在这里，我们的目标是扩展这个框架，以合并基于内核方法的算法，其中嵌入是通过作用于内核特征空间的线性映射来构造的。在这个内核机制中，我们推导了找到对比和非对比损失函数的输出表示的最佳形式的方法。该过程产生一个新的表示空间，其内积表示为导出核，该导出核通常与通过核空间中的增强相关的点进行关联，否则对点进行去相关。我们在小数据集上分析我们的内核模型，以识别自监督学习算法的共同特征，并获得对其在下游任务上的性能的理论见解。 本文旨在将自监督学习框架扩展到核方法，通过在核特征空间中使用线性映射构造嵌入，推导对比和非对比损失函数的最优表示形式。 Bobak T. Kiani,Randall Balestriero,Yubei Chen,Seth Lloyd,Yann LeCun cs.LG,cs.AI,stat.ML

title: Enumerating Regular Languages with Bounded Delay
title_zh: 枚举具有有限延迟的常规语言
comment: This is the full versions with proofs of the STACS'23 article
comments: This is the full versions with proofs of the STACS'23 article
abstract: We study the task, for a given language $L$, of enumerating the (generally infinite) sequence of its words, without repetitions, while bounding the delay between two consecutive words. To allow for delay bounds that do not depend on the current word length, we assume a model where we produce each word by editing the preceding word with a small edit script, rather than writing out the word from scratch. In particular, this witnesses that the language is orderable, i.e., we can write its words as an infinite sequence such that the Levenshtein edit distance between any two consecutive words is bounded by a value that depends only on the language. For instance, $(a+b)^*$ is orderable (with a variant of the Gray code), but $a^* + b^*$ is not. We characterize which regular languages are enumerable in this sense, and show that this can be decided in PTIME in an input deterministic finite automaton (DFA) for the language. In fact, we show that, given a DFA $A$, we can compute in PTIME automata $A_1, \ldots, A_t$ such that $L(A)$ is partitioned as $L(A_1) \sqcup \ldots \sqcup L(A_t)$ and every $L(A_i)$ is orderable in this sense. Further, we show that the value of $t$ obtained is optimal, i.e., we cannot partition $L(A)$ into less than $t$ orderable languages. In the case where $L(A)$ is orderable (i.e., $t=1$), we show that the ordering can be produced by a bounded-delay algorithm: specifically, the algorithm runs in a suitable pointer machine model, and produces a sequence of bounded-length edit scripts to visit the words of $L(A)$ without repetitions, with bounded delay -- exponential in $|A|$ -- between each script. In fact, we show that we can achieve this while only allowing the edit operations push and pop at the beginning and end of the word, which implies that the word can in fact be maintained in a double-ended queue.

In particular, this witnesses that the language is orderable, i.e., we can write its words as an infinite sequence such that the Levenshtein edit distance between any two consecutive words is bounded by a value that depends only on the language. For instance, $(a+b)^*$ is orderable (with a variant of the Gray code), but $a^* + b^*$ is not. We characterize which regular languages are enumerable in this sense, and show that this can be decided in PTIME in an input deterministic finite automaton (DFA) for the language. In fact, we show that, given a DFA $A$, we can compute in PTIME automata $A_1, \ldots, A_t$ such that $L(A)$ is partitioned as $L(A_1) \sqcup \ldots \sqcup L(A_t)$ and every $L(A_i)$ is orderable in this sense. Further, we show that the value of $t$ obtained is optimal, i.e., we cannot partition $L(A)$ into less than $t$ orderable languages. In the case where $L(A)$ is orderable (i.e., $t=1$), we show that the ordering can be produced by a bounded-delay algorithm: specifically, the algorithm runs in a suitable pointer machine model, and produces a sequence of bounded-length edit scripts to visit the words of $L(A)$ without repetitions, with bounded delay -- exponential in $|A|$ -- between each script. In fact, we show that we can achieve this while only allowing the edit operations push and pop at the beginning and end of the word, which implies that the word can in fact be maintained in a double-ended queue.
abstract_zh: 我们研究的任务是，对于给定的语言 $L$，枚举其单词的（通常是无限的）序列，不重复，同时限制两个连续单词之间的延迟。为了允许不依赖于当前单词长度的延迟界限，我们假设一个模型，通过使用小型编辑脚本编辑前面的单词来生成每个单词，而不是从头开始写出单词。特别是，这证明了语言是可排序的，即我们可以将其单词写为无限序列，使得任何两个连续单词之间的编辑距离受到仅取决于语言的值的限制。例如，$(a+b)^*$ 是可排序的（使用格雷码的变体），但 $a^* + b^*$ 则不是。我们描述了哪些常规语言在这个意义上是可枚举的，并表明这可以在该语言的输入确定性有限自动机（DFA）中的 PTIME 中决定。事实上，我们证明，给定一个 DFA $A$，我们可以在 PTIME 自动机 $A_1​​, \ldots, A_t$ 中进行计算，使得 $L(A)$ 被划分为 $L(A_1) \sqcup \ldots \sqcup L(A_t)$ 并且每个 $L(A_i)$ 在这个意义上都是可排序的。此外，我们表明获得的 $t$ 的值是最优的，即我们不能将 $L(A)$ 划分为小于 $t$ 的可排序语言。在 $L(A)$ 是可排序的情况下（即 $t=1$），我们表明排序可以通过有界延迟算法产生：具体来说，该算法在合适的指针机模型中运行，并生成一系列有界长度的编辑脚本来访问 $L(A)$ 的单词，而无需重复，每个脚本之间具有有界延迟（$|A|$ 呈指数）。事实上，我们表明我们可以实现这一点，同时只允许在单词的开头和结尾进行编辑操作推送和弹出，这意味着该单词实际上可以保存在双端队列中。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何以有限延迟枚举正则语言中的无限单词序列，通过编辑脚本而非从头生成单词，确保延迟与单词长度无关。
AI.motivation: 传统枚举方法需从头生成单词导致延迟依赖长度，而本文旨在设计独立于长度的延迟机制，提升枚举效率。
AI.method: 通过将语言划分为多个可排序子语言，利用DFA在多项式时间内判定并生成子自动机，结合双端队列的push/pop操作实现有限延迟枚举。
AI.result: 证明正则语言可被划分为最少t个可排序子语言，且当t=1时可实现指数级延迟的枚举算法，延迟仅依赖于DFA规模。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为正则语言提供了高效、可预测的枚举框架，适用于需有限延迟的应用场景，如自动补全与协议验证。

authors_text: Jialu Zhang,José Cambronero,Sumit Gulwani,Vu Le,Ruzica Piskac,Gustavo Soares,Gust Verbruggen
title: Repairing Bugs in Python Assignments Using Large Language Models
title_zh: 使用大型语言模型修复 Python 作业中的错误
abstract: Students often make mistakes on their introductory programming assignments as part of their learning process. Unfortunately, providing custom repairs for these mistakes can require a substantial amount of time and effort from class instructors. Automated program repair (APR) techniques can be used to synthesize such fixes. Prior work has explored the use of symbolic and neural techniques for APR in the education domain. Both types of approaches require either substantial engineering efforts or large amounts of data and training. We propose to use a large language model trained on code, such as Codex, to build an APR system -- MMAPR -- for introductory Python programming assignments. Our system can fix both syntactic and semantic mistakes by combining multi-modal prompts, iterative querying, test-case-based selection of few-shots, and program chunking. We evaluate MMAPR on 286 real student programs and compare to a baseline built by combining a state-of-the-art Python syntax repair engine, BIFI, and state-of-the-art Python semantic repair engine for student assignments, Refactory. We find that MMAPR can fix more programs and produce smaller patches on average.
summary: Students often make mistakes on their introductory programming assignments as part of their learning process. Unfortunately, providing custom repairs for these mistakes can require a substantial amount of time and effort from class instructors. Automated program repair (APR) techniques can be used to synthesize such fixes. Prior work has explored the use of symbolic and neural techniques for APR in the education domain. Both types of approaches require either substantial engineering efforts or large amounts of data and training. We propose to use a large language model trained on code, such as Codex, to build an APR system -- MMAPR -- for introductory Python programming assignments. Our system can fix both syntactic and semantic mistakes by combining multi-modal prompts, iterative querying, test-case-based selection of few-shots, and program chunking. We evaluate MMAPR on 286 real student programs and compare to a baseline built by combining a state-of-the-art Python syntax repair engine, BIFI, and state-of-the-art Python semantic repair engine for student assignments, Refactory. We find that MMAPR can fix more programs and produce smaller patches on average.
abstract_zh: 作为学习过程的一部分，学生在入门编程作业中经常会犯错误。不幸的是，为这些错误提供定制修复可能需要班级讲师投入大量的时间和精力。自动程序修复（APR）技术可用于综合此类修复。之前的工作已经探索了在教育领域使用符号和神经技术进行 APR。这两种方法都需要大量的工程工作或大量的数据和培训。我们建议使用在代码上训练的大型语言模型（例如 Codex）来构建 APR 系统 - MMAPR - 用于介绍性 Python 编程作业。我们的系统可以通过结合多模式提示、迭代查询、基于测试用例的少数镜头选择和程序分块来修复语法和语义错误。我们在 286 个真实的学生程序上评估 MMAPR，并与通过结合最先进的 Python 语法修复引擎 BIFI 和用于学生作业的最先进的 Python 语义修复引擎 Refactory 构建的基线进行比较。我们发现 MMAPR 平均可以修复更多程序并生成更小的补丁。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于大语言模型的MMAPR系统，用于自动修复入门级Python编程作业中的语法和语义错误。
AI.motivation: 学生编程作业中常见错误需要教师大量人工修正，而传统自动修复技术存在工程复杂度高或数据需求大的问题。
AI.method: 结合多模态提示、迭代查询、测试用例驱动的少样本选择及程序分块技术，利用Codex类大模型实现错误修复。
AI.result: 在286个学生程序测试中，MMAPR相比BIFI+Refactory基线修复成功率更高且生成补丁更简洁。
AI.conclusion: MMAPR在修复效果和效率上优于现有工具，为教育领域程序自动修复提供了可行方案。
