search_text: Repairing Bugs in Python Assignments Using Large Language Models 使用大型语言模型修复 Python 作业中的错误 Students often make mistakes on their introductory programming assignments as part of their learning process. Unfortunately, providing custom repairs for these mistakes can require a substantial amount of time and effort from class instructors. Automated program repair (APR) techniques can be used to synthesize such fixes. Prior work has explored the use of symbolic and neural techniques for APR in the education domain. Both types of approaches require either substantial engineering efforts or large amounts of data and training. We propose to use a large language model trained on code, such as Codex, to build an APR system -- MMAPR -- for introductory Python programming assignments. Our system can fix both syntactic and semantic mistakes by combining multi-modal prompts, iterative querying, test-case-based selection of few-shots, and program chunking. We evaluate MMAPR on 286 real student programs and compare to a baseline built by combining a state-of-the-art Python syntax repair engine, BIFI, and state-of-the-art Python semantic repair engine for student assignments, Refactory. We find that MMAPR can fix more programs and produce smaller patches on average. 作为学习过程的一部分，学生在入门编程作业中经常会犯错误。不幸的是，为这些错误提供定制修复可能需要班级讲师投入大量的时间和精力。自动程序修复（APR）技术可用于综合此类修复。之前的工作已经探索了在教育领域使用符号和神经技术进行 APR。这两种方法都需要大量的工程工作或大量的数据和培训。我们建议使用在代码上训练的大型语言模型（例如 Codex）来构建 APR 系统 - MMAPR - 用于介绍性 Python 编程作业。我们的系统可以通过结合多模式提示、迭代查询、基于测试用例的少数镜头选择和程序分块来修复语法和语义错误。我们在 286 个真实的学生程序上评估 MMAPR，并与通过结合最先进的 Python 语法修复引擎 BIFI 和用于学生作业的最先进的 Python 语义修复引擎 Refactory 构建的基线进行比较。我们发现 MMAPR 平均可以修复更多程序并生成更小的补丁。 本文提出基于大语言模型的MMAPR系统，用于自动修复入门级Python编程作业中的语法和语义错误。 Jialu Zhang,José Cambronero,Sumit Gulwani,Vu Le,Ruzica Piskac,Gustavo Soares,Gust Verbruggen cs.SE,cs.AI

authors_text: Gabriella Pizzuto,Hetong Wang,Hatem Fakhruldeen,Bei Peng,Kevin S. Luck,Andrew I. Cooper
title: Accelerating Laboratory Automation Through Robot Skill Learning For Sample Scraping
title_zh: 通过机器人抓取样品的技能学习加速实验室自动化
abstract: The use of laboratory robotics for autonomous experiments offers an attractive route to alleviate scientists from tedious tasks while accelerating material discovery for topical issues such as climate change and pharmaceuticals. While some experimental workflows can already benefit from automation, sample preparation is still carried out manually due to the high level of motor function and dexterity required when dealing with different tools, chemicals, and glassware. A fundamental workflow in chemical fields is crystallisation, where one application is polymorph screening, i.e., obtaining a three dimensional molecular structure from a crystal. For this process, it is of utmost importance to recover as much of the sample as possible since synthesising molecules is both costly in time and money. To this aim, chemists scrape vials to retrieve sample contents prior to imaging plate transfer. Automating this process is challenging as it goes beyond robotic insertion tasks due to a fundamental requirement of having to execute fine-granular movements within a constrained environment (sample vial). Motivated by how human chemists carry out this process of scraping powder from vials, our work proposes a model-free reinforcement learning method for learning a scraping policy, leading to a fully autonomous sample scraping procedure. We first create a scenario-specific simulation environment with a Panda Franka Emika robot using a laboratory scraper that is inserted into a simulated vial, to demonstrate how a scraping policy can be learned successfully in simulation. We then train and evaluate our method on a real robotic manipulator in laboratory settings, and show that our method can autonomously scrape powder across various setups.

While some experimental workflows can already benefit from automation, sample preparation is still carried out manually due to the high level of motor function and dexterity required when dealing with different tools, chemicals, and glassware. A fundamental workflow in chemical fields is crystallisation, where one application is polymorph screening, i.e., obtaining a three dimensional molecular structure from a crystal. For this process, it is of utmost importance to recover as much of the sample as possible since synthesising molecules is both costly in time and money. To this aim, chemists scrape vials to retrieve sample contents prior to imaging plate transfer. Automating this process is challenging as it goes beyond robotic insertion tasks due to a fundamental requirement of having to execute fine-granular movements within a constrained environment (sample vial). Motivated by how human chemists carry out this process of scraping powder from vials, our work proposes a model-free reinforcement learning method for learning a scraping policy, leading to a fully autonomous sample scraping procedure. We first create a scenario-specific simulation environment with a Panda Franka Emika robot using a laboratory scraper that is inserted into a simulated vial, to demonstrate how a scraping policy can be learned successfully in simulation. We then train and evaluate our method on a real robotic manipulator in laboratory settings, and show that our method can autonomously scrape powder across various setups.
abstract_zh: 使用实验室机器人进行自主实验提供了一条有吸引力的途径，可以将科学家从繁琐的任务中解放出来，同时加速针对气候变化和制药等热门问题的材料发现。虽然一些实验工作流程已经可以从自动化中受益，但由于处理不同工具、化学品和玻璃器皿时需要高水平的运动功能和灵活性，样品制备仍然需要手动进行。化学领域的一个基本工作流程是结晶，其中一个应用是多晶型筛选，即从晶体中获得三维分子结构。对于这个过程，回收尽可能多的样品至关重要，因为合成分子既费时又费钱。为此，化学家在成像板转移之前刮擦小瓶以检索样品内容物。自动化该过程具有挑战性，因为它超出了机器人插入任务的范围，因为必须在受限环境（样品瓶）内执行细粒度运动的基本要求。受人类化学家如何从小瓶中刮取粉末的过程的启发，我们的工作提出了一种无模型强化学习方法来学习刮取策略，从而实现完全自主的样品刮取程序。我们首先使用 Panda Franka Emika 机器人创建一个特定场景的模拟环境，使用插入模拟小瓶中的实验室刮刀，以演示如何在模拟中成功学习刮擦策略。然后，我们在实验室环境中的真实机器人操纵器上训练和评估我们的方法，并表明我们的方法可以在各种设置中自动刮除粉末。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于强化学习的自主样本刮取方法，用于加速实验室自动化中的样本制备流程。
AI.motivation: 实验室中样本刮取工作仍依赖人工操作，因其需要精细的运动控制和适应不同工具、化学品及玻璃器皿的灵活性，而自动化该过程能显著提升材料发现效率并减少成本。
AI.method: 采用无模型强化学习方法，构建基于Panda Franka Emika机器人的模拟环境，训练刮取策略，并在真实实验室场景中验证其有效性。
AI.result: 方法成功实现了在多种设置下机器人自主刮取粉末样本，验证了模拟到现实的迁移能力。
AI.conclusion: 该强化学习框架为实验室自动化提供了可行方案，可推广至其他需要精细操作的实验流程。

authors_text: Martin Radfar,Rohit Barnwal,Rupak Vignesh Swaminathan,Feng-Ju Chang,Grant P. Strimel,Nathan Susanj,Athanasios Mouchtaris
title: ConvRNN-T: Convolutional Augmented Recurrent Neural Network Transducers for Streaming Speech Recognition
title_zh: ConvRNN-T：用于流式语音识别的卷积增强循环神经网络传感器
comment: This paper was presented in Interspeech 2022
comments: This paper was presented in Interspeech 2022
abstract: The recurrent neural network transducer (RNN-T) is a prominent streaming end-to-end (E2E) ASR technology. In RNN-T, the acoustic encoder commonly consists of stacks of LSTMs. Very recently, as an alternative to LSTM layers, the Conformer architecture was introduced where the encoder of RNN-T is replaced with a modified Transformer encoder composed of convolutional layers at the frontend and between attention layers. In this paper, we introduce a new streaming ASR model, Convolutional Augmented Recurrent Neural Network Transducers (ConvRNN-T) in which we augment the LSTM-based RNN-T with a novel convolutional frontend consisting of local and global context CNN encoders. ConvRNN-T takes advantage of causal 1-D convolutional layers, squeeze-and-excitation, dilation, and residual blocks to provide both global and local audio context representation to LSTM layers. We show ConvRNN-T outperforms RNN-T, Conformer, and ContextNet on Librispeech and in-house data. In addition, ConvRNN-T offers less computational complexity compared to Conformer. ConvRNN-T's superior accuracy along with its low footprint make it a promising candidate for on-device streaming ASR technologies.
summary: The recurrent neural network transducer (RNN-T) is a prominent streaming end-to-end (E2E) ASR technology. In RNN-T, the acoustic encoder commonly consists of stacks of LSTMs. Very recently, as an alternative to LSTM layers, the Conformer architecture was introduced where the encoder of RNN-T is replaced with a modified Transformer encoder composed of convolutional layers at the frontend and between attention layers. In this paper, we introduce a new streaming ASR model, Convolutional Augmented Recurrent Neural Network Transducers (ConvRNN-T) in which we augment the LSTM-based RNN-T with a novel convolutional frontend consisting of local and global context CNN encoders. ConvRNN-T takes advantage of causal 1-D convolutional layers, squeeze-and-excitation, dilation, and residual blocks to provide both global and local audio context representation to LSTM layers. We show ConvRNN-T outperforms RNN-T, Conformer, and ContextNet on Librispeech and in-house data. In addition, ConvRNN-T offers less computational complexity compared to Conformer. ConvRNN-T's superior accuracy along with its low footprint make it a promising candidate for on-device streaming ASR technologies.
abstract_zh: 循环神经网络传感器 (RNN-T) 是一种著名的流式端到端 (E2E) ASR 技术。在 RNN-T 中，声学编码器通常由 LSTM 堆栈组成。最近，作为 LSTM 层的替代方案，Conformer 架构被引入，其中 RNN-T 的编码器被替换为由前端和注意层之间的卷积层组成的修改后的 Transformer 编码器。在本文中，我们介绍了一种新的流式 ASR 模型，即卷积增强循环神经网络转换器 (ConvRNN-T)，其中我们使用由局部和全局上下文 CNN 编码器组成的新型卷积前端来增强基于 LSTM 的 RNN-T。 ConvRNN-T 利用因果一维卷积层、挤压和激励、膨胀和残差块为 LSTM 层提供全局和局部音频上下文表示。我们表明 ConvRNN-T 在 Librispeech 和内部数据上优于 RNN-T、Conformer 和 ContextNet。此外，与 Conformer 相比，ConvRNN-T 的计算复杂度更低。 ConvRNN-T 的卓越准确性和低占用空间使其成为设备上流媒体 ASR 技术的有前途的候选者。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的流式语音识别模型ConvRNN-T，通过增强LSTM-based RNN-T的卷积前端来提升性能。
AI.motivation: 现有RNN-T模型依赖LSTM层，而Conformer虽引入Transformer但计算复杂度高，需探索更高效的流式语音识别架构。
AI.method: 在LSTM-based RNN-T中引入包含局部与全局上下文的卷积前端，结合因果卷积、squeeze-and-excitation、空洞卷积和残差块增强特征表示。
AI.result: ConvRNN-T在Librispeech和内部数据集上优于RNN-T、Conformer和ContextNet，且计算复杂度低于Conformer。
AI.conclusion: ConvRNN-T凭借高精度与低计算量，成为设备端流式语音识别的有前景方案。

ConvRNN-T takes advantage of causal 1-D convolutional layers, squeeze-and-excitation, dilation, and residual blocks to provide both global and local audio context representation to LSTM layers. We show ConvRNN-T outperforms RNN-T, Conformer, and ContextNet on Librispeech and in-house data. In addition, ConvRNN-T offers less computational complexity compared to Conformer. ConvRNN-T's superior accuracy along with its low footprint make it a promising candidate for on-device streaming ASR technologies. 循环神经网络传感器 (RNN-T) 是一种著名的流式端到端 (E2E) ASR 技术。在 RNN-T 中，声学编码器通常由 LSTM 堆栈组成。最近，作为 LSTM 层的替代方案，Conformer 架构被引入，其中 RNN-T 的编码器被替换为由前端和注意层之间的卷积层组成的修改后的 Transformer 编码器。在本文中，我们介绍了一种新的流式 ASR 模型，即卷积增强循环神经网络转换器 (ConvRNN-T)，其中我们使用由局部和全局上下文 CNN 编码器组成的新型卷积前端来增强基于 LSTM 的 RNN-T。 ConvRNN-T 利用因果一维卷积层、挤压和激励、膨胀和残差块为 LSTM 层提供全局和局部音频上下文表示。我们表明 ConvRNN-T 在 Librispeech 和内部数据上优于 RNN-T、Conformer 和 ContextNet。此外，与 Conformer 相比，ConvRNN-T 的计算复杂度更低。 ConvRNN-T 的卓越准确性和低占用空间使其成为设备上流媒体 ASR 技术的有前途的候选者。 本文提出了一种新的流式语音识别模型ConvRNN-T，通过增强LSTM-based RNN-T的卷积前端来提升性能。 Martin Radfar,Rohit Barnwal,Rupak Vignesh Swaminathan,Feng-Ju Chang,Grant P. Strimel,Nathan Susanj,Athanasios Mouchtaris cs.SD,cs.CL,eess.AS

authors_text: Negar Nouri,Snehanjali Kalamkar,Forouzan Farzinnejad,Verena Biener,Fabian Schick,Stefan Kalkhof,Jens Grubert
title: Improving Understanding of Biocide Availability in Facades through Immersive Analytics
title_zh: 通过沉浸式分析提高对外墙杀菌剂可用性的了解
abstract: The durability of facades is heavily affected by multiple factors like microbial growth and weather conditions among others. Biocides are often used to resist these factors and protect the facades. However, the biocides get washed out due to rains and other factors like geometric structure of the facade, orientation of the building. It is therefore, important to understand how these factors affect the durability of facades, leading to a requirement of expert analysis. In this paper, we propose a technical pipeline and a set of interaction techniques to support data analysis within the immersive environment for our case study. Our technical pipeline mainly consists of three steps: 3D reconstruction, embedding sensor data and visualization and interaction techniques. We made a formative evaluation of our prototype to get insights from microbiology, biology and VR experts. The remarks from the experts and the results of the evaluation suggest that an immersive analytic system in our case study could be beneficial for both experts and non-expert users.
summary: The durability of facades is heavily affected by multiple factors like microbial growth and weather conditions among others. Biocides are often used to resist these factors and protect the facades. However, the biocides get washed out due to rains and other factors like geometric structure of the facade, orientation of the building. It is therefore, important to understand how these factors affect the durability of facades, leading to a requirement of expert analysis. In this paper, we propose a technical pipeline and a set of interaction techniques to support data analysis within the immersive environment for our case study. Our technical pipeline mainly consists of three steps: 3D reconstruction, embedding sensor data and visualization and interaction techniques. We made a formative evaluation of our prototype to get insights from microbiology, biology and VR experts. The remarks from the experts and the results of the evaluation suggest that an immersive analytic system in our case study could be beneficial for both experts and non-expert users.
abstract_zh: 外墙的耐久性很大程度上受到微生物生长和天气条件等多种因素的影响。杀菌剂通常用于抵抗这些因素并保护外墙。然而，由于降雨和其他因素（例如立面的几何结构、建筑物的方向），杀菌剂会被冲走。因此，了解这些因素如何影响外墙的耐久性非常重要，从而需要进行专家分析。在本文中，我们提出了一个技术管道和一组交互技术来支持我们案例研究的沉浸式环境中的数据分析。我们的技术流程主要包括三个步骤：3D重建、嵌入传感器数据以及可视化和交互技术。我们对原型进行了形成性评估，以获取微生物学、生物学和 VR 专家的见解。专家的评论和评估结果表明，我们案例研究中的沉浸式分析系统对专家和非专家用户都有益。

AI.tldr: 本文通过沉浸式分析技术研究幕墙中杀菌剂可用性，以提升对幕墙耐久性影响因素的理解。
AI.motivation: 幕墙耐久性受微生物生长和天气条件影响，杀菌剂易被雨水冲刷流失，需分析其影响因素并开发有效分析工具。
AI.method: 提出包含3D重建、传感器数据嵌入及可视化交互技术的流程，并通过专家评估验证系统有效性。
AI.result: 专家评估表明，沉浸式分析系统能有效辅助专家和非专家用户理解杀菌剂在幕墙中的分布与流失规律。
AI.conclusion: 沉浸式分析系统可提升对幕墙杀菌剂可用性及耐久性影响因素的分析效率，具有实际应用价值。

authors_text: Maximilian Seitzer,Max Horn,Andrii Zadaianchuk,Dominik Zietlow,Tianjun Xiao,Carl-Johann Simon-Gabriel,Tong He,Zheng Zhang,Bernhard Schölkopf,Thomas Brox,Francesco Locatello
title: Bridging the Gap to Real-World Object-Centric Learning
title_zh: 缩小现实世界中以对象为中心的学习的差距
abstract: Humans naturally decompose their environment into entities at the appropriate level of abstraction to act in the world. Allowing machine learning algorithms to derive this decomposition in an unsupervised way has become an important line of research. However, current methods are restricted to simulated data or require additional information in the form of motion or depth in order to successfully discover objects. In this work, we overcome this limitation by showing that reconstructing features from models trained in a self-supervised manner is a sufficient training signal for object-centric representations to arise in a fully unsupervised way. Our approach, DINOSAUR, significantly out-performs existing image-based object-centric learning models on simulated data and is the first unsupervised object-centric model that scales to real-world datasets such as COCO and PASCAL VOC. DINOSAUR is conceptually simple and shows competitive performance compared to more involved pipelines from the computer vision literature.
summary: Humans naturally decompose their environment into entities at the appropriate level of abstraction to act in the world. Allowing machine learning algorithms to derive this decomposition in an unsupervised way has become an important line of research. However, current methods are restricted to simulated data or require additional information in the form of motion or depth in order to successfully discover objects. In this work, we overcome this limitation by showing that reconstructing features from models trained in a self-supervised manner is a sufficient training signal for object-centric representations to arise in a fully unsupervised way. Our approach, DINOSAUR, significantly out-performs existing image-based object-centric learning models on simulated data and is the first unsupervised object-centric model that scales to real-world datasets such as COCO and PASCAL VOC. DINOSAUR is conceptually simple and shows competitive performance compared to more involved pipelines from the computer vision literature.
abstract_zh: 人类自然地将他们的环境分解为适当抽象级别的实体，以便在世界上采取行动。允许机器学习算法以无监督的方式导出这种分解已成为一个重要的研究方向。然而，当前的方法仅限于模拟数据或需要运动或深度形式的附加信息才能成功发现物体。在这项工作中，我们克服了这一限制，表明从以自监督方式训练的模型中重建特征对于以完全无监督的方式出现以对象为中心的表示来说是足够的训练信号。我们的方法 DINOSAUR 在模拟数据上显着优于现有的基于图像的以对象为中心的学习模型，并且是第一个可扩展到现实世界数据集（例如 COCO 和 PASCAL VOC）的无监督以对象为中心的模型。恐龙在概念上很简单，并且与计算机视觉文献中更复杂的管道相比，显示出具有竞争力的性能。

AI.tldr: 提出一种完全无监督的对象中心学习方法，以解决真实世界数据中的对象分解问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法无法在真实世界数据中有效无监督学习对象分解，需要更通用的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过自监督训练模型重建特征，作为无监督训练信号来生成对象中心表示，方法名为DINOSAUR。
AI.result: 在模拟数据上显著优于现有模型，首次在真实数据集（如COCO和PASCAL VOC）上实现无监督对象中心学习，且性能与复杂视觉方法相当。
AI.conclusion: DINOSAUR方法概念简单有效，为真实世界对象中心学习提供了可行的无监督解决方案。

authors_text: Lars Kreuzberg,Idil Esen Zulfikar,Sabarinath Mahadevan,Francis Engelmann,Bastian Leibe
title: 4D-StOP: Panoptic Segmentation of 4D LiDAR using Spatio-temporal Object Proposal Generation and Aggregation
title_zh: 4D-StOP：使用时空对象建议生成和聚合对 4D LiDAR 进行全景分割
comment: Accepted to the ECCV 2022 AVVision Workshop
comments: Accepted to the ECCV 2022 AVVision Workshop
abstract: In this work, we present a new paradigm, called 4D-StOP, to tackle the task of 4D Panoptic LiDAR Segmentation. 4D-StOP first generates spatio-temporal proposals using voting-based center predictions, where each point in the 4D volume votes for a corresponding center. These tracklet proposals are further aggregated using learned geometric features. The tracklet aggregation method effectively generates a video-level 4D scene representation over the entire space-time volume. This is in contrast to existing end-to-end trainable state-of-the-art approaches which use spatio-temporal embeddings that are represented by Gaussian probability distributions. Our voting-based tracklet generation method followed by geometric feature-based aggregation generates significantly improved panoptic LiDAR segmentation quality when compared to modeling the entire 4D volume using Gaussian probability distributions. 4D-StOP achieves a new state-of-the-art when applied to the SemanticKITTI test dataset with a score of 63.9 LSTQ, which is a large (+7%) improvement compared to current best-performing end-to-end trainable methods. The code and pre-trained models are available at: https://github.com/LarsKreuzberg/4D-StOP.
summary: In this work, we present a new paradigm, called 4D-StOP, to tackle the task of 4D Panoptic LiDAR Segmentation. 4D-StOP first generates spatio-temporal proposals using voting-based center predictions, where each point in the 4D volume votes for a corresponding center. These tracklet proposals are further aggregated using learned geometric features. The tracklet aggregation method effectively generates a video-level 4D scene representation over the entire space-time volume. This is in contrast to existing end-to-end trainable state-of-the-art approaches which use spatio-temporal embeddings that are represented by Gaussian probability distributions. Our voting-based tracklet generation method followed by geometric feature-based aggregation generates significantly improved panoptic LiDAR segmentation quality when compared to modeling the entire 4D volume using Gaussian probability distributions. 4D-StOP achieves a new state-of-the-art when applied to the SemanticKITTI test dataset with a score of 63.9 LSTQ, which is a large (+7%) improvement compared to current best-performing end-to-end trainable methods. The code and pre-trained models are available at: https://github.com/LarsKreuzberg/4D-StOP.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种称为 4D-StOP 的新范例来解决 4D 全景 LiDAR 分割的任务。 4D-StOP 首先使用基于投票的中心预测生成时空提案，其中 4D 体积中的每个点都投票给相应的中心。这些轨迹建议使用学习到的几何特征进一步聚合。 Tracklet 聚合方法有效地在整个时空体积上生成视频级 4D 场景表示。这与现有的端到端可训练的最先进方法形成对比，后者使用由高斯概率分布表示的时空嵌入。与使用高斯概率分布对整个 4D 体积进行建模相比，我们基于投票的轨迹生成方法以及基于几何特征的聚合可显着提高全景 LiDAR 分割质量。 4D-StOP 在应用于 SemanticKITTI 测试数据集时达到了新的最先进水平，得分为 63.9 LSTQ，与当前性能最佳的端到端可训练方法相比，这是一个很大的改进 (+7%)。代码和预训练模型可从以下网址获取：https://github.com/LarsKreuzberg/4D-StOP。

AI.tldr: 本文提出4D-StOP方法，通过时空对象提议生成与聚合实现4D LiDAR的全景分割。
AI.motivation: 现有基于高斯概率分布的端到端方法在建模4D体积时存在局限，难以有效捕捉时空结构信息。
AI.method: 采用投票机制生成时空提议（tracklet），通过学习几何特征进行聚合，构建全局时空场景表示。
AI.result: 在SemanticKITTI数据集上达到63.9 LSTQ指标，较最佳端到端方法提升7%。
AI.conclusion: 基于tracklet生成与几何特征聚合的方法显著优于传统高斯分布建模方式，确立新SOTA。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出4D-StOP方法，通过时空对象提议生成与聚合实现4D LiDAR的全景分割。
ai_content.motivation: 现有基于高斯概率分布的端到端方法在建模4D体积时存在局限，难以有效捕捉时空结构信息。
ai_content.method: 采用投票机制生成时空提议（tracklet），通过学习几何特征进行聚合，构建全局时空场景表示。

search_text: 4D-StOP: Panoptic Segmentation of 4D LiDAR using Spatio-temporal Object Proposal Generation and Aggregation 4D-StOP：使用时空对象建议生成和聚合对 4D LiDAR 进行全景分割 In this work, we present a new paradigm, called 4D-StOP, to tackle the task of 4D Panoptic LiDAR Segmentation. 4D-StOP first generates spatio-temporal proposals using voting-based center predictions, where each point in the 4D volume votes for a corresponding center. These tracklet proposals are further aggregated using learned geometric features. The tracklet aggregation method effectively generates a video-level 4D scene representation over the entire space-time volume. This is in contrast to existing end-to-end trainable state-of-the-art approaches which use spatio-temporal embeddings that are represented by Gaussian probability distributions. Our voting-based tracklet generation method followed by geometric feature-based aggregation generates significantly improved panoptic LiDAR segmentation quality when compared to modeling the entire 4D volume using Gaussian probability distributions. 4D-StOP achieves a new state-of-the-art when applied to the SemanticKITTI test dataset with a score of 63.9 LSTQ, which is a large (+7%) improvement compared to current best-performing end-to-end trainable methods. The code and pre-trained models are available at: https://github.com/LarsKreuzberg/4D-StOP. 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种称为 4D-StOP 的新范例来解决 4D 全景 LiDAR 分割的任务。 4D-StOP 首先使用基于投票的中心预测生成时空提案，其中 4D 体积中的每个点都投票给相应的中心。这些轨迹建议使用学习到的几何特征进一步聚合。 Tracklet 聚合方法有效地在整个时空体积上生成视频级 4D 场景表示。这与现有的端到端可训练的最先进方法形成对比，后者使用由高斯概率分布表示的时空嵌入。与使用高斯概率分布对整个 4D 体积进行建模相比，我们基于投票的轨迹生成方法以及基于几何特征的聚合可显着提高全景 LiDAR 分割质量。 4D-StOP 在应用于 SemanticKITTI 测试数据集时达到了新的最先进水平，得分为 63.9 LSTQ，与当前性能最佳的端到端可训练方法相比，这是一个很大的改进 (+7%)。代码和预训练模型可从以下网址获取：https://github.com/LarsKreuzberg/4D-StOP。 本文提出4D-StOP方法，通过时空对象提议生成与聚合实现4D LiDAR的全景分割。 Lars Kreuzberg,Idil Esen Zulfikar,Sabarinath Mahadevan,Francis Engelmann,Bastian Leibe cs.CV

authors_text: Yuan Yin,Matthieu Kirchmeyer,Jean-Yves Franceschi,Alain Rakotomamonjy,Patrick Gallinari
title: Continuous PDE Dynamics Forecasting with Implicit Neural Representations
title_zh: 使用隐式神经表示的连续 PDE 动力学预测
comment: ef:The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations, International Conference on Representation Learning, May 2023, Kigali, Rwanda
comments: ef:The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations, International Conference on Representation Learning, May 2023, Kigali, Rwanda
abstract: Effective data-driven PDE forecasting methods often rely on fixed spatial and / or temporal discretizations. This raises limitations in real-world applications like weather prediction where flexible extrapolation at arbitrary spatiotemporal locations is required. We address this problem by introducing a new data-driven approach, DINo, that models a PDE's flow with continuous-time dynamics of spatially continuous functions. This is achieved by embedding spatial observations independently of their discretization via Implicit Neural Representations in a small latent space temporally driven by a learned ODE. This separate and flexible treatment of time and space makes DINo the first data-driven model to combine the following advantages. It extrapolates at arbitrary spatial and temporal locations; it can learn from sparse irregular grids or manifolds; at test time, it generalizes to new grids or resolutions. DINo outperforms alternative neural PDE forecasters in a variety of challenging generalization scenarios on representative PDE systems.
summary: Effective data-driven PDE forecasting methods often rely on fixed spatial and / or temporal discretizations. This raises limitations in real-world applications like weather prediction where flexible extrapolation at arbitrary spatiotemporal locations is required. We address this problem by introducing a new data-driven approach, DINo, that models a PDE's flow with continuous-time dynamics of spatially continuous functions. This is achieved by embedding spatial observations independently of their discretization via Implicit Neural Representations in a small latent space temporally driven by a learned ODE. This separate and flexible treatment of time and space makes DINo the first data-driven model to combine the following advantages. It extrapolates at arbitrary spatial and temporal locations; it can learn from sparse irregular grids or manifolds; at test time, it generalizes to new grids or resolutions. DINo outperforms alternative neural PDE forecasters in a variety of challenging generalization scenarios on representative PDE systems.
abstract_zh: 有效的数据驱动的 PDE 预测方法通常依赖于固定的空间和/或时间离散化。这给现实世界的应用带来了限制，比如天气预报，需要在任意时空位置进行灵活的外推。我们通过引入一种新的数据驱动方法 DINo 来解决这个问题，该方法使用空间连续函数的连续时间动态对偏微分方程的流进行建模。这是通过通过隐式神经表示将空间观测结果独立于离散化嵌入到由学习的 ODE 暂时驱动的小潜在空间中来实现的。这种对时间和空间的独立而灵活的处理使得 DINo 成为第一个结合了以下优点的数据驱动模型。它在任意空间和时间位置进行推断；它可以从稀疏的不规则网格或流形中学习；在测试时，它会推广到新的网格或分辨率。在代表性 PDE 系统上的各种具有挑战性的泛化场景中，DINo 优于替代神经 PDE 预测器。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于隐式神经表示的连续偏微分方程（PDE）动态预测方法DINO，解决传统离散化方法在时空外推中的局限性。
AI.motivation: 传统PDE预测方法依赖固定时空离散化，难以满足天气预测等需要任意时空位置灵活外推的实际需求。
AI.method: 通过隐式神经表示（INRs）将空间观测独立于离散化嵌入到小潜在空间，时间动态由学习的ODE驱动，实现时空分离建模。
AI.result: DINO在多个代表性PDE系统上优于现有神经PDE预测器，尤其在稀疏网格、新分辨率等泛化场景表现突出。
AI.conclusion: DINO首次结合时空分离建模优势，实现连续时空外推与灵活数据适配，为复杂PDE预测提供新范式。

authors_text: Zijian Liu,Ta Duy Nguyen,Thien Hang Nguyen,Alina Ene,Huy L. Nguyen
title: META-STORM: Generalized Fully-Adaptive Variance Reduced SGD for Unbounded Functions
title_zh: META-STORM：无界函数的广义完全自适应方差降低 SGD
abstract: We study the application of variance reduction (VR) techniques to general non-convex stochastic optimization problems. In this setting, the recent work STORM [Cutkosky-Orabona '19] overcomes the drawback of having to compute gradients of "mega-batches" that earlier VR methods rely on. There, STORM utilizes recursive momentum to achieve the VR effect and is then later made fully adaptive in STORM+ [Levy et al., '21], where full-adaptivity removes the requirement for obtaining certain problem-specific parameters such as the smoothness of the objective and bounds on the variance and norm of the stochastic gradients in order to set the step size. However, STORM+ crucially relies on the assumption that the function values are bounded, excluding a large class of useful functions. In this work, we propose META-STORM, a generalized framework of STORM+ that removes this bounded function values assumption while still attaining the optimal convergence rate for non-convex optimization. META-STORM not only maintains full-adaptivity, removing the need to obtain problem specific parameters, but also improves the convergence rate's dependency on the problem parameters. Furthermore, META-STORM can utilize a large range of parameter settings that subsumes previous methods allowing for more flexibility in a wider range of settings. Finally, we demonstrate the effectiveness of META-STORM through experiments across common deep learning tasks. Our algorithm improves upon the previous work STORM+ and is competitive with widely used algorithms after the addition of per-coordinate update and exponential moving average heuristics.
summary: We study the application of variance reduction (VR) techniques to general non-convex stochastic optimization problems. In this setting, the recent work STORM [Cutkosky-Orabona '19] overcomes the drawback of having to compute gradients of "mega-batches" that earlier VR methods rely on. There, STORM utilizes recursive momentum to achieve the VR effect and is then later made fully adaptive in STORM+ [Levy et al., '21], where full-adaptivity removes the requirement for obtaining certain problem-specific parameters such as the smoothness of the objective and bounds on the variance and norm of the stochastic gradients in order to set the step size. However, STORM+ crucially relies on the assumption that the function values are bounded, excluding a large class of useful functions. In this work, we propose META-STORM, a generalized framework of STORM+ that removes this bounded function values assumption while still attaining the optimal convergence rate for non-convex optimization. META-STORM not only maintains full-adaptivity, removing the need to obtain problem specific parameters, but also improves the convergence rate's dependency on the problem parameters. Furthermore, META-STORM can utilize a large range of parameter settings that subsumes previous methods allowing for more flexibility in a wider range of settings. Finally, we demonstrate the effectiveness of META-STORM through experiments across common deep learning tasks. Our algorithm improves upon the previous work STORM+ and is competitive with widely used algorithms after the addition of per-coordinate update and exponential moving average heuristics.
abstract_zh: 我们研究方差减少（VR）技术在一般非凸随机优化问题中的应用。在这种情况下，最近的工作 STORM [Cutkosky-Orabona '19] 克服了早期 VR 方法所依赖的必须计算“大批量”梯度的缺点。在那里，STORM 利用递归动量来实现 VR 效果，然后在 STORM+ 中实现完全自适应[Levy et al., '21]，其中完全自适应消除了获取某些特定问题参数的要求，例如目标的平滑度以及随机梯度方差和范数的界限，以便设置步长。然而，STORM+ 关键依赖于函数值有界的假设，排除了大量有用的函数。在这项工作中，我们提出了 META-STORM，这是 STORM+ 的通用框架，它消除了有界函数值假设，同时仍然获得非凸优化的最佳收敛率。 META-STORM不仅保持了完全自适应性，无需获取问题特定参数，而且还提高了收敛速度对问题参数的依赖性。此外，META-STORM 可以利用大范围的参数设置，其中包含以前的方法，从而在更广泛的设置中提供更大的灵活性。最后，我们通过常见深度学习任务的实验证明了 META-STORM 的有效性。我们的算法改进了之前的 STORM+ 工作，并且在添加了每坐标更新和指数移动平均启发式之后，与广泛使用的算法具有竞争力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为META-STORM的通用完全自适应方差减少随机梯度下降算法，用于解决无界函数的非凸随机优化问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法如STORM+要求目标函数值有界，限制了其在实际问题中的应用，而META-STORM旨在消除这一限制并保持最优收敛速度。
AI.method: 通过推广STORM+框架，去除函数值有界假设，结合递归动量和自适应参数调整，改进收敛率对问题参数的依赖性，并引入坐标更新和指数移动平均策略增强灵活性。
AI.result: 实验表明，META-STORM在常见深度学习任务中优于STORM+，且在添加坐标更新和指数移动平均启发式方法后与主流算法具有竞争力。
AI.conclusion: META-STORM是一种适用于无界函数的通用优化框架，在保持完全自适应性的同时提升了收敛效率和参数灵活性。

Furthermore, META-STORM can utilize a large range of parameter settings that subsumes previous methods allowing for more flexibility in a wider range of settings. Finally, we demonstrate the effectiveness of META-STORM through experiments across common deep learning tasks. Our algorithm improves upon the previous work STORM+ and is competitive with widely used algorithms after the addition of per-coordinate update and exponential moving average heuristics. 我们研究方差减少（VR）技术在一般非凸随机优化问题中的应用。在这种情况下，最近的工作 STORM [Cutkosky-Orabona '19] 克服了早期 VR 方法所依赖的必须计算“大批量”梯度的缺点。在那里，STORM 利用递归动量来实现 VR 效果，然后在 STORM+ 中实现完全自适应[Levy et al., '21]，其中完全自适应消除了获取某些特定问题参数的要求，例如目标的平滑度以及随机梯度方差和范数的界限，以便设置步长。然而，STORM+ 关键依赖于函数值有界的假设，排除了大量有用的函数。在这项工作中，我们提出了 META-STORM，这是 STORM+ 的通用框架，它消除了有界函数值假设，同时仍然获得非凸优化的最佳收敛率。 META-STORM不仅保持了完全自适应性，无需获取问题特定参数，而且还提高了收敛速度对问题参数的依赖性。此外，META-STORM 可以利用大范围的参数设置，其中包含以前的方法，从而在更广泛的设置中提供更大的灵活性。最后，我们通过常见深度学习任务的实验证明了 META-STORM 的有效性。我们的算法改进了之前的 STORM+ 工作，并且在添加了每坐标更新和指数移动平均启发式之后，与广泛使用的算法具有竞争力。 本文提出了一种名为META-STORM的通用完全自适应方差减少随机梯度下降算法，用于解决无界函数的非凸随机优化问题。 Zijian Liu,Ta Duy Nguyen,Thien Hang Nguyen,Alina Ene,Huy L. Nguyen cs.LG,cs.DS

title: Meta Knowledge Condensation for Federated Learning
title_zh: 联邦学习的元知识压缩
abstract: Existing federated learning paradigms usually extensively exchange distributed models at a central solver to achieve a more powerful model. However, this would incur severe communication burden between a server and multiple clients especially when data distributions are heterogeneous. As a result, current federated learning methods often require a large number of communication rounds in training. Unlike existing paradigms, we introduce an alternative perspective to significantly decrease the communication cost in federate learning. In this work, we first introduce a meta knowledge representation method that extracts meta knowledge from distributed clients. The extracted meta knowledge encodes essential information that can be used to improve the current model. As the training progresses, the contributions of training samples to a federated model also vary. Thus, we introduce a dynamic weight assignment mechanism that enables samples to contribute adaptively to the current model update. Then, informative meta knowledge from all active clients is sent to the server for model update. Training a model on the combined meta knowledge without exposing original data among different clients can significantly mitigate the heterogeneity issues. Moreover, to further ameliorate data heterogeneity, we also exchange meta knowledge among clients as conditional initialization for local meta knowledge extraction. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of our proposed method. Remarkably, our method outperforms the state-of-the-art by a large margin (from $74.07\%$ to $92.95\%$) on MNIST with a restricted communication budget (i.e. 10 rounds).
summary: Existing federated learning paradigms usually extensively exchange distributed models at a central solver to achieve a more powerful model. However, this would incur severe communication burden between a server and multiple clients especially when data distributions are heterogeneous. As a result, current federated learning methods often require a large number of communication rounds in training. Unlike existing paradigms, we introduce an alternative perspective to significantly decrease the communication cost in federate learning. In this work, we first introduce a meta knowledge representation method that extracts meta knowledge from distributed clients. The extracted meta knowledge encodes essential information that can be used to improve the current model. As the training progresses, the contributions of training samples to a federated model also vary. Thus, we introduce a dynamic weight assignment mechanism that enables samples to contribute adaptively to the current model update. Then, informative meta knowledge from all active clients is sent to the server for model update. Training a model on the combined meta knowledge without exposing original data among different clients can significantly mitigate the heterogeneity issues. Moreover, to further ameliorate data heterogeneity, we also exchange meta knowledge among clients as conditional initialization for local meta knowledge extraction. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of our proposed method. Remarkably, our method outperforms the state-of-the-art by a large margin (from $74.07\%$ to $92.95\%$) on MNIST with a restricted communication budget (i.e. 10 rounds).
abstract_zh: 现有的联邦学习范例通常在中央求解器上广泛交换分布式模型，以实现更强大的模型。然而，这会在服务器和多个客户端之间带来严重的通信负担，尤其是当数据分布异构时。因此，当前的联邦学习方法在训练中往往需要大量的通信轮次。与现有范例不同，我们引入了另一种观点来显着降低联邦学习中的通信成本。在这项工作中，我们首先介绍一种从分布式客户端提取元知识的元知识表示方法。提取的元知识对可用于改进当前模型的基本信息进行编码。随着训练的进行，训练样本对联邦模型的贡献也会发生变化。因此，我们引入了一种动态权重分配机制，使样本能够自适应地为当前模型更新做出贡献。然后，来自所有活动客户端的信息元知识被发送到服务器以进行模型更新。在组合元知识上训练模型而不暴露不同客户端之间的原始数据可以显着缓解异质性问题。此外，为了进一步改善数据异构性，我们还在客户端之间交换元知识作为局部元知识提取的条件初始化。大量的实验证明了我们提出的方法的有效性和效率。值得注意的是，我们的方法在通信预算有限（即 10 轮）的 MNIST 上大幅优于最先进的方法（从 $74.07\%$ 到 $92.95\%$）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种元知识凝聚方法，通过提取和共享客户端的元知识来降低联邦学习中的通信成本。
AI.motivation: 现有联邦学习方法因频繁交换完整模型导致通信负担重，尤其在数据异质性环境下需要大量通信轮次。
AI.method: 引入元知识表示方法提取客户端关键信息，结合动态权重机制适应性更新模型，并在客户端间共享元知识以缓解异质性问题。
AI.result: 在MNIST数据集上，通信预算限制为10轮时准确率从74.07%提升至92.95%，显著优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 元知识凝聚有效降低通信成本并提升联邦学习效率，为异质性环境下的模型训练提供新思路。

title: Federated Stain Normalization for Computational Pathology
title_zh: 计算病理学联合染色归一化
abstract: Although deep federated learning has received much attention in recent years, progress has been made mainly in the context of natural images and barely for computational pathology. However, deep federated learning is an opportunity to create datasets that reflect the data diversity of many laboratories. Further, the effort of dataset construction can be divided among many. Unfortunately, existing algorithms cannot be easily applied to computational pathology since previous work presupposes that data distributions of laboratories must be similar. This is an unlikely assumption, mainly since different laboratories have different staining styles. As a solution, we propose BottleGAN, a generative model that can computationally align the staining styles of many laboratories and can be trained in a privacy-preserving manner to foster federated learning in computational pathology. We construct a heterogenic multi-institutional dataset based on the PESO segmentation dataset and improve the IOU by 42\% compared to existing federated learning algorithms. An implementation of BottleGAN is available at https://github.com/MECLabTUDA/BottleGAN
summary: Although deep federated learning has received much attention in recent years, progress has been made mainly in the context of natural images and barely for computational pathology. However, deep federated learning is an opportunity to create datasets that reflect the data diversity of many laboratories. Further, the effort of dataset construction can be divided among many. Unfortunately, existing algorithms cannot be easily applied to computational pathology since previous work presupposes that data distributions of laboratories must be similar. This is an unlikely assumption, mainly since different laboratories have different staining styles. As a solution, we propose BottleGAN, a generative model that can computationally align the staining styles of many laboratories and can be trained in a privacy-preserving manner to foster federated learning in computational pathology. We construct a heterogenic multi-institutional dataset based on the PESO segmentation dataset and improve the IOU by 42\% compared to existing federated learning algorithms. An implementation of BottleGAN is available at https://github.com/MECLabTUDA/BottleGAN
abstract_zh: 尽管深度联邦学习近年来受到了广泛关注，但主要是在自然图像的背景下取得了进展，而在计算病理学方面却几乎没有取得进展。然而，深度联合学习是创建反映许多实验室数据多样性的数据集的机会。此外，数据集构建的工作可以分摊到很多方面。不幸的是，现有算法不能轻易应用于计算病理学，因为以前的工作假设实验室的数据分布必须相似。这是一个不太可能的假设，主要是因为不同的实验室有不同的染色风格。作为解决方案，我们提出了 BottleGAN，这是一种生成模型，可以通过计算方式调整许多实验室的染色风格，并可以以保护隐私的方式进行训练，以促进计算病理学中的联合学习。我们基于 PESO 分割数据集构建了异构多机构数据集，与现有联邦学习算法相比，IOU 提高了 42%。 BottleGAN 的实现可在 https://github.com/MECLabTUDA/BottleGAN 上找到

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为BottleGAN的生成模型，用于在计算病理学中实现联邦染色归一化，解决多实验室数据分布差异问题。
AI.motivation: 现有联邦学习算法假设实验室数据分布相似，但实际中不同实验室的染色风格差异显著，阻碍了计算病理学的联邦学习应用。
AI.method: 设计BottleGAN生成模型，在隐私保护框架下对齐多实验室染色风格，并基于PESO分割数据集构建异构多机构数据集进行训练。
AI.result: 相比现有联邦学习算法，该方法在IOU指标上提升了42%。
AI.conclusion: BottleGAN有效解决了计算病理学中跨实验室染色差异问题，为隐私保护下的联邦学习提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Muhammad Shehram Shah Syed,Zafi Sherhan Syed,Abbas Syed
title: Classification of Vocal Bursts for ACII 2022 A-VB-Type Competition using Convolutional Neural Networks and Deep Acoustic Embeddings
title_zh: 使用卷积神经网络和深度声学嵌入对 ACII 2022 A-VB 类型比赛的声音爆发进行分类
comment: Report for our submission to the ACII 2022 Affective Vocal Bursts (A-VB) Competition
comments: Report for our submission to the ACII 2022 Affective Vocal Bursts (A-VB) Competition
abstract: This report provides a brief description of our proposed solution for the Vocal Burst Type classification task of the ACII 2022 Affective Vocal Bursts (A-VB) Competition. We experimented with two approaches as part of our solution for the task at hand. The first of which is based on convolutional neural networks trained on Mel Spectrograms, and the second is based on average pooling of deep acoustic embeddings from a pretrained wav2vec2 model. Our best performing model achieves an unweighted average recall (UAR) of 0.5190 for the test partition, compared to the chance-level UAR of 0.1250 and a baseline of 0.4172. Thus, an improvement of around 20% over the challenge baseline. The results reported in this document demonstrate the efficacy of our proposed approaches to solve the AV-B Type Classification task.
summary: This report provides a brief description of our proposed solution for the Vocal Burst Type classification task of the ACII 2022 Affective Vocal Bursts (A-VB) Competition. We experimented with two approaches as part of our solution for the task at hand. The first of which is based on convolutional neural networks trained on Mel Spectrograms, and the second is based on average pooling of deep acoustic embeddings from a pretrained wav2vec2 model. Our best performing model achieves an unweighted average recall (UAR) of 0.5190 for the test partition, compared to the chance-level UAR of 0.1250 and a baseline of 0.4172. Thus, an improvement of around 20% over the challenge baseline. The results reported in this document demonstrate the efficacy of our proposed approaches to solve the AV-B Type Classification task.
abstract_zh: 本报告简要描述了我们为 ACII 2022 情感声音爆发 (A-VB) 竞赛的声音爆发类型分类任务提出的解决方案。我们尝试了两种方法作为我们手头任务解决方案的一部分。第一个是基于在梅尔频谱图上训练的卷积神经网络，第二个是基于来自预训练的 wav2vec2 模型的深度声学嵌入的平均池化。我们性能最佳的模型在测试分区中实现了 0.5190 的未加权平均召回率 (UAR)，而机会级 UAR 为 0.1250，基线为 0.4172。因此，比挑战基线提高了约 20%。本文件中报告的结果证明了我们提出的解决 AV-B 类型分类任务的方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出使用卷积神经网络和深度声学嵌入方法对ACII 2022情感语音爆发类型进行分类。
AI.motivation: 为解决情感语音爆发分类任务中准确率不足的问题，探索更有效的声学特征提取与分类方法。
AI.method: 采用两种方法：1）基于Mel频谱图的卷积神经网络；2）预训练wav2vec2模型的深度声学嵌入平均池化。
AI.result: 最佳模型在测试集上达到0.5190的UAR，较基线提升20%，显著高于随机水平0.1250。
AI.conclusion: 实验验证了CNN与预训练声学模型在语音爆发分类任务中的有效性，为情感计算提供了可行方案。

search_text: Classification of Vocal Bursts for ACII 2022 A-VB-Type Competition using Convolutional Neural Networks and Deep Acoustic Embeddings 使用卷积神经网络和深度声学嵌入对 ACII 2022 A-VB 类型比赛的声音爆发进行分类 This report provides a brief description of our proposed solution for the Vocal Burst Type classification task of the ACII 2022 Affective Vocal Bursts (A-VB) Competition. We experimented with two approaches as part of our solution for the task at hand. The first of which is based on convolutional neural networks trained on Mel Spectrograms, and the second is based on average pooling of deep acoustic embeddings from a pretrained wav2vec2 model. Our best performing model achieves an unweighted average recall (UAR) of 0.5190 for the test partition, compared to the chance-level UAR of 0.1250 and a baseline of 0.4172. Thus, an improvement of around 20% over the challenge baseline. The results reported in this document demonstrate the efficacy of our proposed approaches to solve the AV-B Type Classification task. 本报告简要描述了我们为 ACII 2022 情感声音爆发 (A-VB) 竞赛的声音爆发类型分类任务提出的解决方案。我们尝试了两种方法作为我们手头任务解决方案的一部分。第一个是基于在梅尔频谱图上训练的卷积神经网络，第二个是基于来自预训练的 wav2vec2 模型的深度声学嵌入的平均池化。我们性能最佳的模型在测试分区中实现了 0.5190 的未加权平均召回率 (UAR)，而机会级 UAR 为 0.1250，基线为 0.4172。因此，比挑战基线提高了约 20%。本文件中报告的结果证明了我们提出的解决 AV-B 类型分类任务的方法的有效性。 本文提出使用卷积神经网络和深度声学嵌入方法对ACII 2022情感语音爆发类型进行分类。 Muhammad Shehram Shah Syed,Zafi Sherhan Syed,Abbas Syed cs.SD,eess.AS

title: Low-Latency Sliding Window Algorithms for Formal Languages
title_zh: 形式语言的低延迟滑动窗口算法
comment: A short version will be presented at the conference FSTTCS 2022
comments: A short version will be presented at the conference FSTTCS 2022
abstract: Low-latency sliding window algorithms for regular and context-free languages are studied, where latency refers to the worst-case time spent for a single window update or query. For every regular language $L$ it is shown that there exists a constant-latency solution that supports adding and removing symbols independently on both ends of the window (the so-called two-way variable-size model). We prove that this result extends to all visibly pushdown languages. For deterministic 1-counter languages we present a $\mathcal{O}(\log n)$ latency sliding window algorithm for the two-way variable-size model where $n$ refers to the window size. We complement these results with a conditional lower bound: there exists a fixed real-time deterministic context-free language $L$ such that, assuming the OMV (online matrix vector multiplication) conjecture, there is no sliding window algorithm for $L$ with latency $n^{1/2-ε}$ for any $ε>0$, even in the most restricted sliding window model (one-way fixed-size model). The above mentioned results all refer to the unit-cost RAM model with logarithmic word size. For regular languages we also present a refined picture using word sizes $\mathcal{O}(1)$, $\mathcal{O}(\log\log n)$, and $\mathcal{O}(\log n)$.
summary: Low-latency sliding window algorithms for regular and context-free languages are studied, where latency refers to the worst-case time spent for a single window update or query. For every regular language $L$ it is shown that there exists a constant-latency solution that supports adding and removing symbols independently on both ends of the window (the so-called two-way variable-size model). We prove that this result extends to all visibly pushdown languages. For deterministic 1-counter languages we present a $\mathcal{O}(\log n)$ latency sliding window algorithm for the two-way variable-size model where $n$ refers to the window size. We complement these results with a conditional lower bound: there exists a fixed real-time deterministic context-free language $L$ such that, assuming the OMV (online matrix vector multiplication) conjecture, there is no sliding window algorithm for $L$ with latency $n^{1/2-ε}$ for any $ε>0$, even in the most restricted sliding window model (one-way fixed-size model). The above mentioned results all refer to the unit-cost RAM model with logarithmic word size. For regular languages we also present a refined picture using word sizes $\mathcal{O}(1)$, $\mathcal{O}(\log\log n)$, and $\mathcal{O}(\log n)$.
abstract_zh: 研究了常规语言和上下文无关语言的低延迟滑动窗口算法，其中延迟是指单个窗口更新或查询所花费的最坏情况时间。对于每个常规语言$L$，都表明存在一个恒定延迟解决方案，支持在窗口两端独立添加和删除符号（所谓的双向可变大小模型）。我们证明这个结果可以扩展到所有可见的下推语言。对于确定性 1 计数器语言，我们为双向可变大小模型提出了 $\mathcal{O}(\log n)$ 延迟滑动窗口算法，其中 $n$ 指窗口大小。我们用条件下界补充这些结果：存在一个固定的实时确定性上下文无关语言$L$，这样，假设OMV（在线矩阵向量乘法）猜想，对于任何$ε>0$，$L$没有延迟$n^{1/2-ε}$的滑动窗口算法，即使在最受限制的滑动窗口模型（单向固定大小模型）中也是如此。上述结果均参考对数字长的单位成本RAM模型。对于常规语言，我们还使用字长 $\mathcal{O}(1)$、$\mathcal{O}(\log\log n)$ 和 $\mathcal{O}(\log n)$ 来呈现精确的图像。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了正则语言和上下文无关语言的低延迟滑动窗口算法，分析了不同模型下的时间复杂度。
AI.motivation: 滑动窗口算法在实时数据处理中至关重要，但现有方法在处理复杂语言时存在延迟瓶颈，需优化效率并明确理论界限。
AI.method: 基于自动机理论与复杂度分析，结合OMV猜想，设计适用于不同语言类别的算法并证明其复杂度下界。
AI.result: 为正则语言和可见下推语言提供常数延迟方案，确定性1-计数语言达到O(log n)延迟，并基于OMV猜想证明某些语言无法突破n^{1/2-ε}下界。
AI.conclusion: 明确了不同语言类别的滑动窗口算法效率边界，为实际应用提供了理论指导与实现方向。

title: Access Control with Encrypted Feature Maps for Object Detection Models
title_zh: 用于对象检测模型的加密特征图的访问控制
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2206.05422
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2206.05422
abstract: In this paper, we propose an access control method with a secret key for object detection models for the first time so that unauthorized users without a secret key cannot benefit from the performance of trained models. The method enables us not only to provide a high detection performance to authorized users but to also degrade the performance for unauthorized users. The use of transformed images was proposed for the access control of image classification models, but these images cannot be used for object detection models due to performance degradation. Accordingly, in this paper, selected feature maps are encrypted with a secret key for training and testing models, instead of input images. In an experiment, the protected models allowed authorized users to obtain almost the same performance as that of non-protected models but also with robustness against unauthorized access without a key.
summary: In this paper, we propose an access control method with a secret key for object detection models for the first time so that unauthorized users without a secret key cannot benefit from the performance of trained models. The method enables us not only to provide a high detection performance to authorized users but to also degrade the performance for unauthorized users. The use of transformed images was proposed for the access control of image classification models, but these images cannot be used for object detection models due to performance degradation. Accordingly, in this paper, selected feature maps are encrypted with a secret key for training and testing models, instead of input images. In an experiment, the protected models allowed authorized users to obtain almost the same performance as that of non-protected models but also with robustness against unauthorized access without a key.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们首次提出了一种用于对象检测模型的带有密钥的访问控制方法，使得没有密钥的未经授权的用户无法从训练模型的性能中受益。该方法使我们不仅能够为授权用户提供较高的检测性能，而且还能够降低未经授权用户的性能。提出使用变换后的图像来进行图像分类模型的访问控制，但由于性能下降，这些图像不能用于对象检测模型。因此，在本文中，使用秘密密钥对选定的特征图（而不是输入图像）进行加密，以用于训练和测试模型。在实验中，受保护的模型允许授权用户获得与非受保护模型几乎相同的性能，而且还具有鲁棒性，可以防止未经密钥的未经授权的访问。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于加密特征图的对象检测模型访问控制方法，通过密钥保护模型，使授权用户保持高性能检测，未授权用户则性能显著下降。
AI.motivation: 现有图像分类模型的访问控制方法（如转换输入图像）无法直接应用于对象检测模型，因其会导致性能严重退化，需探索新的保护机制。
AI.method: 在模型训练和测试阶段，使用密钥对选定的特征图进行加密，而非对输入图像进行转换，从而实现对授权用户的性能保护和对未授权用户的访问限制。
AI.result: 实验表明，受保护模型在授权用户使用时性能几乎与原始模型一致，且对无密钥的未授权访问具有鲁棒性，有效防止性能被窃取。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为对象检测模型提供了有效的访问控制方案，在保证授权用户性能的同时，显著削弱未授权用户的模型利用能力，为模型安全防护提供了新思路。

method: 在模型训练和测试阶段，使用密钥对选定的特征图进行加密，而非对输入图像进行转换，从而实现对授权用户的性能保护和对未授权用户的访问限制。
result: 实验表明，受保护模型在授权用户使用时性能几乎与原始模型一致，且对无密钥的未授权访问具有鲁棒性，有效防止性能被窃取。
conclusion: 该方法为对象检测模型提供了有效的访问控制方案，在保证授权用户性能的同时，显著削弱未授权用户的模型利用能力，为模型安全防护提供了新思路。
search_text: Access Control with Encrypted Feature Maps for Object Detection Models 用于对象检测模型的加密特征图的访问控制 In this paper, we propose an access control method with a secret key for object detection models for the first time so that unauthorized users without a secret key cannot benefit from the performance of trained models. The method enables us not only to provide a high detection performance to authorized users but to also degrade the performance for unauthorized users. The use of transformed images was proposed for the access control of image classification models, but these images cannot be used for object detection models due to performance degradation. Accordingly, in this paper, selected feature maps are encrypted with a secret key for training and testing models, instead of input images. In an experiment, the protected models allowed authorized users to obtain almost the same performance as that of non-protected models but also with robustness against unauthorized access without a key. 在本文中，我们首次提出了一种用于对象检测模型的带有密钥的访问控制方法，使得没有密钥的未经授权的用户无法从训练模型的性能中受益。该方法使我们不仅能够为授权用户提供较高的检测性能，而且还能够降低未经授权用户的性能。提出使用变换后的图像来进行图像分类模型的访问控制，但由于性能下降，这些图像不能用于对象检测模型。因此，在本文中，使用秘密密钥对选定的特征图（而不是输入图像）进行加密，以用于训练和测试模型。在实验中，受保护的模型允许授权用户获得与非受保护模型几乎相同的性能，而且还具有鲁棒性，可以防止未经密钥的未经授权的访问。 本文提出一种基于加密特征图的对象检测模型访问控制方法，通过密钥保护模型，使授权用户保持高性能检测，未授权用户则性能显著下降。 Teru Nagamori,Hiroki Ito,AprilPyone MaungMaung,Hitoshi Kiya cs.CV,cs.CR,cs.LG

authors_text: Xingshuai Dong,Matthew A. Garratt,Sreenatha G. Anavatti,Hussein A. Abbass,Junyu Dong
title: Lightweight Monocular Depth Estimation with an Edge Guided Network
title_zh: 使用边缘引导网络的轻量级单目深度估计
abstract: Monocular depth estimation is an important task that can be applied to many robotic applications. Existing methods focus on improving depth estimation accuracy via training increasingly deeper and wider networks, however these suffer from large computational complexity. Recent studies found that edge information are important cues for convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to estimate depth. Inspired by the above observations, we present a novel lightweight Edge Guided Depth Estimation Network (EGD-Net) in this study. In particular, we start out with a lightweight encoder-decoder architecture and embed an edge guidance branch which takes as input image gradients and multi-scale feature maps from the backbone to learn the edge attention features. In order to aggregate the context information and edge attention features, we design a transformer-based feature aggregation module (TRFA). TRFA captures the long-range dependencies between the context information and edge attention features through cross-attention mechanism. We perform extensive experiments on the NYU depth v2 dataset. Experimental results show that the proposed method runs about 96 fps on a Nvidia GTX 1080 GPU whilst achieving the state-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy.
summary: Monocular depth estimation is an important task that can be applied to many robotic applications. Existing methods focus on improving depth estimation accuracy via training increasingly deeper and wider networks, however these suffer from large computational complexity. Recent studies found that edge information are important cues for convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to estimate depth. Inspired by the above observations, we present a novel lightweight Edge Guided Depth Estimation Network (EGD-Net) in this study. In particular, we start out with a lightweight encoder-decoder architecture and embed an edge guidance branch which takes as input image gradients and multi-scale feature maps from the backbone to learn the edge attention features. In order to aggregate the context information and edge attention features, we design a transformer-based feature aggregation module (TRFA). TRFA captures the long-range dependencies between the context information and edge attention features through cross-attention mechanism. We perform extensive experiments on the NYU depth v2 dataset. Experimental results show that the proposed method runs about 96 fps on a Nvidia GTX 1080 GPU whilst achieving the state-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy.
abstract_zh: 单目深度估计是一项重要任务，可应用于许多机器人应用。现有方法侧重于通过训练越来越深和越来越宽的网络来提高深度估计精度，然而这些方法面临着巨大的计算复杂性。最近的研究发现，边缘信息是卷积神经网络（CNN）估计深度的重要线索。受上述观察的启发，我们在本研究中提出了一种新颖的轻量级边缘引导深度估计网络（EGD-Net）。特别是，我们从轻量级编码器-解码器架构开始，并嵌入一个边缘引导分支，该分支将主干的输入图像梯度和多尺度特征图作为输入，以学习边缘注意特征。为了聚合上下文信息和边缘注意特征，我们设计了一个基于变压器的特征聚合模块（TRFA）。 TRFA通过交叉注意力机制捕获上下文信息和边缘注意力特征之间的长程依赖关系。我们在 NYU 深度 v2 数据集上进行了广泛的实验。实验结果表明，所提出的方法在 Nvidia GTX 1080 GPU 上运行约 96 fps，同时在准确度方面实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种轻量级单目深度估计网络EGD-Net，通过引入边缘引导机制提升深度估计精度与效率。
AI.motivation: 现有深度估计方法依赖复杂网络结构导致计算量大，而边缘信息对深度估计具有重要价值，需设计轻量化模型有效利用边缘特征。
AI.method: 采用轻量编码器-解码器框架，结合边缘引导分支提取图像梯度与多尺度特征，并设计基于Transformer的特征聚合模块（TRFA）融合上下文信息与边缘注意力特征。
AI.result: 在NYU depth v2数据集上以96fps实现实时推理，精度达到当时最先进的水平。
AI.conclusion: EGD-Net通过边缘引导与Transformer特征融合，在保证深度估计精度的同时显著降低计算复杂度，适用于实时机器人应用。

authors_text: Edmund J. C. Findlay,Haozheng Zhang,Ziyi Chang,Hubert P. H. Shum
title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models for Styled Walking Synthesis
title_zh: 用于风格化步行合成的去噪扩散概率模型
abstract: Generating realistic motions for digital humans is time-consuming for many graphics applications. Data-driven motion synthesis approaches have seen solid progress in recent years through deep generative models. These results offer high-quality motions but typically suffer in motion style diversity. For the first time, we propose a framework using the denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) to synthesize styled human motions, integrating two tasks into one pipeline with increased style diversity compared with traditional motion synthesis methods. Experimental results show that our system can generate high-quality and diverse walking motions.
summary: Generating realistic motions for digital humans is time-consuming for many graphics applications. Data-driven motion synthesis approaches have seen solid progress in recent years through deep generative models. These results offer high-quality motions but typically suffer in motion style diversity. For the first time, we propose a framework using the denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) to synthesize styled human motions, integrating two tasks into one pipeline with increased style diversity compared with traditional motion synthesis methods. Experimental results show that our system can generate high-quality and diverse walking motions.
abstract_zh: 对于许多图形应用程序来说，为数字人物生成逼真的动作非常耗时。近年来，数据驱动的运动合成方法通过深度生成模型取得了坚实的进展。这些结果提供了高质量的动作，但通常会受到动作风格多样性的影响。我们首次提出了一种使用去噪扩散概率模型（DDPM）来合成风格人体运动的框架，将两个任务集成到一个管道中，与传统运动合成方法相比，增加了风格多样性。实验结果表明，我们的系统可以生成高质量且多样化的步行动作。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于去噪扩散概率模型（DDPM）的框架，用于生成风格多样化的行走动作。
AI.motivation: 传统运动合成方法生成的运动质量高但风格单一，难以满足数字人应用中对多样化运动需求。
AI.method: 设计整合双任务的DDPM框架，通过扩散过程学习运动风格分布并生成多样化行走动作。
AI.result: 实验验证系统能生成高质量且风格多样的行走动作，优于传统方法的风格表现力。
AI.conclusion: DDPM框架有效提升了运动合成的风格多样性，为数字人动画生成提供新解决方案。

authors_text: Zijian Liu,Ta Duy Nguyen,Alina Ene,Huy L. Nguyen
title: On the Convergence of AdaGrad(Norm) on $\R^{d}$: Beyond Convexity, Non-Asymptotic Rate and Acceleration
title_zh: 关于$\R^{d}$上AdaGrad(Norm)的收敛性：超越凸性、非渐近速率和加速度
comment: Updated manuscript from ICLR 2023 with fixed typos
comments: Updated manuscript from ICLR 2023 with fixed typos
abstract: Existing analysis of AdaGrad and other adaptive methods for smooth convex optimization is typically for functions with bounded domain diameter. In unconstrained problems, previous works guarantee an asymptotic convergence rate without an explicit constant factor that holds true for the entire function class. Furthermore, in the stochastic setting, only a modified version of AdaGrad, different from the one commonly used in practice, in which the latest gradient is not used to update the stepsize, has been analyzed. Our paper aims at bridging these gaps and developing a deeper understanding of AdaGrad and its variants in the standard setting of smooth convex functions as well as the more general setting of quasar convex functions. First, we demonstrate new techniques to explicitly bound the convergence rate of the vanilla AdaGrad for unconstrained problems in both deterministic and stochastic settings. Second, we propose a variant of AdaGrad for which we can show the convergence of the last iterate, instead of the average iterate. Finally, we give new accelerated adaptive algorithms and their convergence guarantee in the deterministic setting with explicit dependency on the problem parameters, improving upon the asymptotic rate shown in previous works.
summary: Existing analysis of AdaGrad and other adaptive methods for smooth convex optimization is typically for functions with bounded domain diameter. In unconstrained problems, previous works guarantee an asymptotic convergence rate without an explicit constant factor that holds true for the entire function class. Furthermore, in the stochastic setting, only a modified version of AdaGrad, different from the one commonly used in practice, in which the latest gradient is not used to update the stepsize, has been analyzed. Our paper aims at bridging these gaps and developing a deeper understanding of AdaGrad and its variants in the standard setting of smooth convex functions as well as the more general setting of quasar convex functions. First, we demonstrate new techniques to explicitly bound the convergence rate of the vanilla AdaGrad for unconstrained problems in both deterministic and stochastic settings. Second, we propose a variant of AdaGrad for which we can show the convergence of the last iterate, instead of the average iterate. Finally, we give new accelerated adaptive algorithms and their convergence guarantee in the deterministic setting with explicit dependency on the problem parameters, improving upon the asymptotic rate shown in previous works.
abstract_zh: AdaGrad 和其他平滑凸优化自适应方法的现有分析通常适用于具有有界域直径的函数。在无约束问题中，以前的工作保证了渐进收敛速度，而无需适用于整个函数类的显式常数因子。此外，在随机设置中，仅分析了 AdaGrad 的修改版本，与实际中常用的版本不同，其中不使用最新梯度来更新步长。我们的论文旨在弥合这些差距，并加深对 AdaGrad 及其变体在平滑凸函数标准设置以及类星体凸函数更一般设置中的理解。首先，我们展示了新技术，可以明确限制普通 AdaGrad 在确定性和随机设置中解决无约束问题的收敛速度。其次，我们提出了 AdaGrad 的一个变体，我们可以显示最后一次迭代的收敛性，而不是平均迭代的收敛性。最后，我们在明确依赖于问题参数的确定性设置中给出新的加速自适应算法及其收敛保证，从而改进了先前工作中显示的渐近率。

AI.tldr: 本文研究AdaGrad算法在无约束光滑凸函数及更一般的拟凸函数上的收敛性，提出新的分析方法并改进算法性能。
AI.motivation: 现有AdaGrad分析局限于凸函数且缺乏明确收敛常数，随机设置下仅分析了非实用的修改版算法，需填补无约束问题与非凸场景的理论空白。
AI.method: 提出显式界定收敛率的新技术，设计保证最后一次迭代收敛的AdaGrad变体，并构建具有参数显式依赖的加速自适应算法。
AI.result: 在确定性和随机设置下分析了普通AdaGrad的收敛率，证明新变体的最后一次迭代收敛性，提出加速算法并给出非渐近收敛保证。
AI.conclusion: 本文扩展了AdaGrad的理论适用范围，提供了更精确的收敛率分析，改进了算法设计并提升了收敛效率。

search_text: On the Convergence of AdaGrad(Norm) on $\R^{d}$: Beyond Convexity, Non-Asymptotic Rate and Acceleration 关于$\R^{d}$上AdaGrad(Norm)的收敛性：超越凸性、非渐近速率和加速度 Existing analysis of AdaGrad and other adaptive methods for smooth convex optimization is typically for functions with bounded domain diameter. In unconstrained problems, previous works guarantee an asymptotic convergence rate without an explicit constant factor that holds true for the entire function class. Furthermore, in the stochastic setting, only a modified version of AdaGrad, different from the one commonly used in practice, in which the latest gradient is not used to update the stepsize, has been analyzed. Our paper aims at bridging these gaps and developing a deeper understanding of AdaGrad and its variants in the standard setting of smooth convex functions as well as the more general setting of quasar convex functions. First, we demonstrate new techniques to explicitly bound the convergence rate of the vanilla AdaGrad for unconstrained problems in both deterministic and stochastic settings. Second, we propose a variant of AdaGrad for which we can show the convergence of the last iterate, instead of the average iterate. Finally, we give new accelerated adaptive algorithms and their convergence guarantee in the deterministic setting with explicit dependency on the problem parameters, improving upon the asymptotic rate shown in previous works. AdaGrad 和其他平滑凸优化自适应方法的现有分析通常适用于具有有界域直径的函数。在无约束问题中，以前的工作保证了渐进收敛速度，而无需适用于整个函数类的显式常数因子。此外，在随机设置中，仅分析了 AdaGrad 的修改版本，与实际中常用的版本不同，其中不使用最新梯度来更新步长。我们的论文旨在弥合这些差距，并加深对 AdaGrad 及其变体在平滑凸函数标准设置以及类星体凸函数更一般设置中的理解。首先，我们展示了新技术，可以明确限制普通 AdaGrad 在确定性和随机设置中解决无约束问题的收敛速度。其次，我们提出了 AdaGrad 的一个变体，我们可以显示最后一次迭代的收敛性，而不是平均迭代的收敛性。最后，我们在明确依赖于问题参数的确定性设置中给出新的加速自适应算法及其收敛保证，从而改进了先前工作中显示的渐近率。 本文研究AdaGrad算法在无约束光滑凸函数及更一般的拟凸函数上的收敛性，提出新的分析方法并改进算法性能。 Zijian Liu,Ta Duy Nguyen,Alina Ene,Huy L. Nguyen cs.LG,cs.DS

title: Physical Human-Robot Interaction Control of an Upper Limb Exoskeleton with a Decentralized Neuro-Adaptive Control Scheme
title_zh: 采用分散式神经自适应控制方案的上肢外骨骼物理人机交互控制
comment: This work has been accepted for publication by the IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
comments: This work has been accepted for publication by the IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Within the concept of physical human-robot interaction (pHRI), the most important criterion is the safety of the human operator interacting with a high degree of freedom (DoF) robot. Therefore, a robust control scheme is in high demand to establish safe pHRI and stabilize nonlinear, high DoF systems. In this paper, an adaptive decentralized control strategy is designed to accomplish the abovementioned objectives. To do so, a human upper limb model and an exoskeleton model are decentralized and augmented at the subsystem level to enable a decentralized control action design. Moreover, human exogenous force (HEF) that can resist exoskeleton motion is estimated using radial basis function neural networks (RBFNNs). Estimating both human upper limb and robot rigid body parameters, along with HEF estimation, makes the controller adaptable to different operators, ensuring their physical safety. The barrier Lyapunov function (BLF) is employed to guarantee that the robot can operate in a safe workspace while ensuring stability by adjusting the control law. Unknown actuator uncertainty and constraints are also considered in this study to ensure a smooth and safe pHRI. Then, the asymptotic stability of the whole system is established by means of the virtual stability concept and virtual power flows (VPFs) under the proposed robust controller. The experimental results are presented and compared to proportional-derivative (PD) and proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controllers. To show the robustness of the designed controller and its good performance, experiments are performed at different velocities, with different human users, and in the presence of unknown disturbances. The proposed controller showed perfect performance in controlling the robot, whereas PD and PID controllers could not even ensure stable motion in the wrist joints of the robot.
summary: Within the concept of physical human-robot interaction (pHRI), the most important criterion is the safety of the human operator interacting with a high degree of freedom (DoF) robot. Therefore, a robust control scheme is in high demand to establish safe pHRI and stabilize nonlinear, high DoF systems. In this paper, an adaptive decentralized control strategy is designed to accomplish the abovementioned objectives. To do so, a human upper limb model and an exoskeleton model are decentralized and augmented at the subsystem level to enable a decentralized control action design. Moreover, human exogenous force (HEF) that can resist exoskeleton motion is estimated using radial basis function neural networks (RBFNNs). Estimating both human upper limb and robot rigid body parameters, along with HEF estimation, makes the controller adaptable to different operators, ensuring their physical safety. The barrier Lyapunov function (BLF) is employed to guarantee that the robot can operate in a safe workspace while ensuring stability by adjusting the control law. Unknown actuator uncertainty and constraints are also considered in this study to ensure a smooth and safe pHRI. Then, the asymptotic stability of the whole system is established by means of the virtual stability concept and virtual power flows (VPFs) under the proposed robust controller. The experimental results are presented and compared to proportional-derivative (PD) and proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controllers. To show the robustness of the designed controller and its good performance, experiments are performed at different velocities, with different human users, and in the presence of unknown disturbances. The proposed controller showed perfect performance in controlling the robot, whereas PD and PID controllers could not even ensure stable motion in the wrist joints of the robot.
abstract_zh: 在物理人机交互 (pHRI) 的概念中，最重要的标准是人类操作员与高自由度 (DoF) 机器人交互的安全性。因此，迫切需要一种鲁棒的控制方案来建立安全的 pHRI 并稳定非线性、高自由度系统。本文设计了一种自适应分散控制策略来实现上述目标。为此，人类上肢模型和外骨骼模型在子系统级别进行分散和增强，以实现分散的控制动作设计。此外，可以使用径向基函数神经网络（RBFNN）估计可以抵抗外骨骼运动的人体外源力（HEF）。估计人体上肢和机器人刚体参数以及 HEF 估计，使控制器能够适应不同的操作员，确保他们的人身安全。采用障碍李亚普诺夫函数（BLF）来保证机器人能够在安全的工作空间中运行，同时通过调整控制律来保证稳定性。本研究还考虑了未知执行器的不确定性和约束，以确保 pHRI 顺利安全。然后，在所提出的鲁棒控制器下，通过虚拟稳定性概念和虚拟功率流（VPF）建立了整个系统的渐近稳定性。给出了实验结果，并与比例微分 (PD) 和比例积分微分 (PID) 控制器进行了比较。为了展示所设计的控制器的鲁棒性及其良好的性能，在不同的速度、不同的人类用户以及存在未知干扰的情况下进行了实验。所提出的控制器在控制机器人方面表现出完美的性能，而PD和PID控制器甚至无法确保机器人腕关节的稳定运动。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种去中心化神经自适应控制方案，用于上肢外骨骼的物理人机交互控制，以确保安全性和稳定性。
AI.motivation: 在高自由度机器人物理人机交互中，确保操作者安全是核心需求，传统控制器难以应对非线性系统和未知干扰。
AI.method: 通过子系统级去中心化建模、径向基函数神经网络估计人体外力与参数、屏障李雅普诺夫函数保障安全 workspace，并结合虚拟稳定性概念与虚拟功率流分析系统稳定性。
AI.result: 实验表明该控制器在不同速度、用户及干扰下均表现优异，而PD/PID控制器无法保证机械腕关节稳定运动。
AI.conclusion: 所提控制器实现了对高自由度外骨骼系统的安全稳定控制，验证了其在物理人机交互中的鲁棒性与适应性优势。

authors_text: Xingyi Li,Chaoyi Hong,Yiran Wang,Zhiguo Cao,Ke Xian,Guosheng Lin
title: SymmNeRF: Learning to Explore Symmetry Prior for Single-View View Synthesis
title_zh: SymmNeRF：学习探索单视图视图合成的对称先验
abstract: We study the problem of novel view synthesis of objects from a single image. Existing methods have demonstrated the potential in single-view view synthesis. However, they still fail to recover the fine appearance details, especially in self-occluded areas. This is because a single view only provides limited information. We observe that manmade objects usually exhibit symmetric appearances, which introduce additional prior knowledge. Motivated by this, we investigate the potential performance gains of explicitly embedding symmetry into the scene representation. In this paper, we propose SymmNeRF, a neural radiance field (NeRF) based framework that combines local and global conditioning under the introduction of symmetry priors. In particular, SymmNeRF takes the pixel-aligned image features and the corresponding symmetric features as extra inputs to the NeRF, whose parameters are generated by a hypernetwork. As the parameters are conditioned on the image-encoded latent codes, SymmNeRF is thus scene-independent and can generalize to new scenes. Experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets show that SymmNeRF synthesizes novel views with more details regardless of the pose transformation, and demonstrates good generalization when applied to unseen objects. Code is available at: https://github.com/xingyi-li/SymmNeRF.
summary: We study the problem of novel view synthesis of objects from a single image. Existing methods have demonstrated the potential in single-view view synthesis. However, they still fail to recover the fine appearance details, especially in self-occluded areas. This is because a single view only provides limited information. We observe that manmade objects usually exhibit symmetric appearances, which introduce additional prior knowledge. Motivated by this, we investigate the potential performance gains of explicitly embedding symmetry into the scene representation. In this paper, we propose SymmNeRF, a neural radiance field (NeRF) based framework that combines local and global conditioning under the introduction of symmetry priors. In particular, SymmNeRF takes the pixel-aligned image features and the corresponding symmetric features as extra inputs to the NeRF, whose parameters are generated by a hypernetwork. As the parameters are conditioned on the image-encoded latent codes, SymmNeRF is thus scene-independent and can generalize to new scenes. Experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets show that SymmNeRF synthesizes novel views with more details regardless of the pose transformation, and demonstrates good generalization when applied to unseen objects. Code is available at: https://github.com/xingyi-li/SymmNeRF.
abstract_zh: 我们研究单个图像中对象的新颖视图合成问题。现有方法已经证明了单视图视图合成的潜力。然而，他们仍然无法恢复精细的外观细节，特别是在自遮挡区域。这是因为单个视图仅提供有限的信息。我们观察到人造物体通常表现出对称的外观，这引入了额外的先验知识。受此启发，我们研究了将对称性显式嵌入到场景表示中的潜在性能增益。在本文中，我们提出了 SymmNeRF，一种基于神经辐射场（NeRF）的框架，在引入对称先验的情况下结合了局部和全局调节。特别是，SymmNeRF 将像素对齐的图像特征和相应的对称特征作为 NeRF 的额外输入，其参数由超网络生成。由于参数以图像编码的潜在代码为条件，因此 SymmNeRF 是场景无关的，并且可以推广到新场景。对合成数据集和真实世界数据集的实验表明，无论姿势变换如何，SymmNeRF 都能合成具有更多细节的新颖视图，并且在应用于看不见的对象时表现出良好的泛化能力。代码位于：https://github.com/xingyi-li/SymmNeRF。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SymmNeRF框架，通过引入对称性先验解决单视角视图合成中细粒度细节恢复问题，尤其针对自我遮挡区域的重建。
AI.motivation: 现有单视角视图合成方法因单视角信息有限，难以恢复细粒度外观细节（如自我遮挡区域），而人造物体普遍存在对称性特征可提供额外先验知识。
AI.method: 设计SymmNeRF神经辐射场框架，结合局部像素特征与全局对称特征作为输入，通过超网络生成场景无关的NeRF参数，实现对称性先验的显式建模。
AI.result: 在合成与真实数据集实验中，SymmNeRF能生成更丰富的细节且对姿态变换鲁棒，在未见物体上表现出良好的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 显式引入对称性先验可有效提升单视角视图合成质量，SymmNeRF通过参数共享机制实现了跨场景的泛化能力。

search_text: SymmNeRF: Learning to Explore Symmetry Prior for Single-View View Synthesis SymmNeRF：学习探索单视图视图合成的对称先验 We study the problem of novel view synthesis of objects from a single image. Existing methods have demonstrated the potential in single-view view synthesis. However, they still fail to recover the fine appearance details, especially in self-occluded areas. This is because a single view only provides limited information. We observe that manmade objects usually exhibit symmetric appearances, which introduce additional prior knowledge. Motivated by this, we investigate the potential performance gains of explicitly embedding symmetry into the scene representation. In this paper, we propose SymmNeRF, a neural radiance field (NeRF) based framework that combines local and global conditioning under the introduction of symmetry priors. In particular, SymmNeRF takes the pixel-aligned image features and the corresponding symmetric features as extra inputs to the NeRF, whose parameters are generated by a hypernetwork. As the parameters are conditioned on the image-encoded latent codes, SymmNeRF is thus scene-independent and can generalize to new scenes. Experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets show that SymmNeRF synthesizes novel views with more details regardless of the pose transformation, and demonstrates good generalization when applied to unseen objects. Code is available at: https://github.com/xingyi-li/SymmNeRF. 我们研究单个图像中对象的新颖视图合成问题。现有方法已经证明了单视图视图合成的潜力。然而，他们仍然无法恢复精细的外观细节，特别是在自遮挡区域。这是因为单个视图仅提供有限的信息。我们观察到人造物体通常表现出对称的外观，这引入了额外的先验知识。受此启发，我们研究了将对称性显式嵌入到场景表示中的潜在性能增益。在本文中，我们提出了 SymmNeRF，一种基于神经辐射场（NeRF）的框架，在引入对称先验的情况下结合了局部和全局调节。特别是，SymmNeRF 将像素对齐的图像特征和相应的对称特征作为 NeRF 的额外输入，其参数由超网络生成。由于参数以图像编码的潜在代码为条件，因此 SymmNeRF 是场景无关的，并且可以推广到新场景。对合成数据集和真实世界数据集的实验表明，无论姿势变换如何，SymmNeRF 都能合成具有更多细节的新颖视图，并且在应用于看不见的对象时表现出良好的泛化能力。代码位于：https://github.com/xingyi-li/SymmNeRF。 本文提出SymmNeRF框架，通过引入对称性先验解决单视角视图合成中细粒度细节恢复问题，尤其针对自我遮挡区域的重建。 Xingyi Li,Chaoyi Hong,Yiran Wang,Zhiguo Cao,Ke Xian,Guosheng Lin cs.CV

authors_text: Aneta Koleva,Martin Ringsquandl,Mark Buckley,Rakebul Hasan,Volker Tresp
title: Named Entity Recognition in Industrial Tables using Tabular Language Models
title_zh: 使用表格语言模型进行工业表中的命名实体识别
abstract: Specialized transformer-based models for encoding tabular data have gained interest in academia. Although tabular data is omnipresent in industry, applications of table transformers are still missing. In this paper, we study how these models can be applied to an industrial Named Entity Recognition (NER) problem where the entities are mentioned in tabular-structured spreadsheets. The highly technical nature of spreadsheets as well as the lack of labeled data present major challenges for fine-tuning transformer-based models. Therefore, we develop a dedicated table data augmentation strategy based on available domain-specific knowledge graphs. We show that this boosts performance in our low-resource scenario considerably. Further, we investigate the benefits of tabular structure as inductive bias compared to tables as linearized sequences. Our experiments confirm that a table transformer outperforms other baselines and that its tabular inductive bias is vital for convergence of transformer-based models.
summary: Specialized transformer-based models for encoding tabular data have gained interest in academia. Although tabular data is omnipresent in industry, applications of table transformers are still missing. In this paper, we study how these models can be applied to an industrial Named Entity Recognition (NER) problem where the entities are mentioned in tabular-structured spreadsheets. The highly technical nature of spreadsheets as well as the lack of labeled data present major challenges for fine-tuning transformer-based models. Therefore, we develop a dedicated table data augmentation strategy based on available domain-specific knowledge graphs. We show that this boosts performance in our low-resource scenario considerably. Further, we investigate the benefits of tabular structure as inductive bias compared to tables as linearized sequences. Our experiments confirm that a table transformer outperforms other baselines and that its tabular inductive bias is vital for convergence of transformer-based models.
abstract_zh: 用于编码表格数据的专门的基于变压器的模型引起了学术界的兴趣。尽管表格数据在工业中无处不在，但表格变压器的应用仍然缺失。在本文中，我们研究如何将这些模型应用于工业命名实体识别（NER）问题，其中实体在表格结构电子表格中提及。电子表格的高度技术性以及缺乏标记数据给基于变压器的模型进行微调带来了重大挑战。因此，我们基于可用的特定领域知识图开发了专用的表数据增强策略。我们表明，这可以显着提高资源匮乏场景中的性能。此外，我们研究了表格结构作为归纳偏差与表格作为线性化序列相比的好处。我们的实验证实，表格变压器优于其他基线，并且其表格归纳偏差对于基于变压器的模型的收敛至关重要。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何利用表格语言模型解决工业表格中的命名实体识别问题
AI.motivation: 工业表格数据虽普遍但缺乏应用，现有模型面临标注数据不足及技术复杂性挑战
AI.method: 提出基于领域知识图的表格数据增强策略，并对比表格结构与线性化序列的归纳偏置
AI.result: 实验表明表格转换器优于其他方法，其结构归纳偏置对模型收敛至关重要
AI.conclusion: 表格语言模型结合数据增强策略有效解决工业表格NER问题，结构归纳偏置提升性能

title: polyBERT: A chemical language model to enable fully machine-driven ultrafast polymer informatics
title_zh: polyBERT：一种化学语言模型，可实现完全机器驱动的超快聚合物信息学
abstract: Polymers are a vital part of everyday life. Their chemical universe is so large that it presents unprecedented opportunities as well as significant challenges to identify suitable application-specific candidates. We present a complete end-to-end machine-driven polymer informatics pipeline that can search this space for suitable candidates at unprecedented speed and accuracy. This pipeline includes a polymer chemical fingerprinting capability called polyBERT (inspired by Natural Language Processing concepts), and a multitask learning approach that maps the polyBERT fingerprints to a host of properties. polyBERT is a chemical linguist that treats the chemical structure of polymers as a chemical language. The present approach outstrips the best presently available concepts for polymer property prediction based on handcrafted fingerprint schemes in speed by two orders of magnitude while preserving accuracy, thus making it a strong candidate for deployment in scalable architectures including cloud infrastructures.
summary: Polymers are a vital part of everyday life. Their chemical universe is so large that it presents unprecedented opportunities as well as significant challenges to identify suitable application-specific candidates. We present a complete end-to-end machine-driven polymer informatics pipeline that can search this space for suitable candidates at unprecedented speed and accuracy. This pipeline includes a polymer chemical fingerprinting capability called polyBERT (inspired by Natural Language Processing concepts), and a multitask learning approach that maps the polyBERT fingerprints to a host of properties. polyBERT is a chemical linguist that treats the chemical structure of polymers as a chemical language. The present approach outstrips the best presently available concepts for polymer property prediction based on handcrafted fingerprint schemes in speed by two orders of magnitude while preserving accuracy, thus making it a strong candidate for deployment in scalable architectures including cloud infrastructures.
abstract_zh: 聚合物是日常生活的重要组成部分。他们的化学领域如此之大，为识别合适的特定应用候选人提供了前所未有的机遇和重大挑战。我们提出了一个完整的端到端机器驱动的聚合物信息学管道，可以以前所未有的速度和准确性在这个空间中搜索合适的候选者。该管道包括称为 polyBERT 的聚合物化学指纹识别功能（受自然语言处理概念启发），以及将 polyBERT 指纹映射到许多属性的多任务学习方法。 polyBERT 是一位化学语言学家，将聚合物的化学结构视为一种化学语言。本方法在速度上比目前基于手工指纹方案的最佳聚合物性能预测概念快了两个数量级，同时保持了准确性，从而使其成为部署在包括云基础设施在内的可扩展架构中的有力候选者。

AI.tldr: 本文提出polyBERT化学语言模型，通过机器驱动方法实现超快速聚合物候选物筛选。
AI.motivation: 聚合物化学空间庞大，传统人工设计指纹方法效率低，难以满足快速准确筛选需求。
AI.method: 基于自然语言处理思想构建polyBERT化学指纹技术，结合多任务学习映射材料属性。
AI.result: 相较传统方法提速2个数量级且保持精度，适用于云架构等可扩展系统部署。
AI.conclusion: polyBERT在速度与准确性上优于现有方案，为聚合物信息学提供高效解决方案。
