authors_text: Uriel Singer,Adam Polyak,Thomas Hayes,Xi Yin,Jie An,Songyang Zhang,Qiyuan Hu,Harry Yang,Oron Ashual,Oran Gafni,Devi Parikh,Sonal Gupta,Yaniv Taigman
title: Make-A-Video: Text-to-Video Generation without Text-Video Data
title_zh: 制作视频：无需文本视频数据即可生成文本到视频
abstract: We propose Make-A-Video -- an approach for directly translating the tremendous recent progress in Text-to-Image (T2I) generation to Text-to-Video (T2V). Our intuition is simple: learn what the world looks like and how it is described from paired text-image data, and learn how the world moves from unsupervised video footage. Make-A-Video has three advantages: (1) it accelerates training of the T2V model (it does not need to learn visual and multimodal representations from scratch), (2) it does not require paired text-video data, and (3) the generated videos inherit the vastness (diversity in aesthetic, fantastical depictions, etc.) of today's image generation models. We design a simple yet effective way to build on T2I models with novel and effective spatial-temporal modules. First, we decompose the full temporal U-Net and attention tensors and approximate them in space and time. Second, we design a spatial temporal pipeline to generate high resolution and frame rate videos with a video decoder, interpolation model and two super resolution models that can enable various applications besides T2V. In all aspects, spatial and temporal resolution, faithfulness to text, and quality, Make-A-Video sets the new state-of-the-art in text-to-video generation, as determined by both qualitative and quantitative measures.
summary: We propose Make-A-Video -- an approach for directly translating the tremendous recent progress in Text-to-Image (T2I) generation to Text-to-Video (T2V). Our intuition is simple: learn what the world looks like and how it is described from paired text-image data, and learn how the world moves from unsupervised video footage. Make-A-Video has three advantages: (1) it accelerates training of the T2V model (it does not need to learn visual and multimodal representations from scratch), (2) it does not require paired text-video data, and (3) the generated videos inherit the vastness (diversity in aesthetic, fantastical depictions, etc.) of today's image generation models. We design a simple yet effective way to build on T2I models with novel and effective spatial-temporal modules. First, we decompose the full temporal U-Net and attention tensors and approximate them in space and time. Second, we design a spatial temporal pipeline to generate high resolution and frame rate videos with a video decoder, interpolation model and two super resolution models that can enable various applications besides T2V. In all aspects, spatial and temporal resolution, faithfulness to text, and quality, Make-A-Video sets the new state-of-the-art in text-to-video generation, as determined by both qualitative and quantitative measures.
abstract_zh: 我们提出 Make-A-Video——一种将文本到图像 (T2I) 生成的最新巨大进展直接转化为文本到视频 (T2V) 的方法。我们的直觉很简单：从配对的文本图像数据中了解世界是什么样子以及如何描述它，并从无监督的视频片段中了解世界如何移动。 Make-A-Video 具有三个优点：（1）它加速了 T2V 模型的训练（它不需要从头开始学习视觉和多模态表示），（2）它不需要配对的文本视频数据，（3）生成的视频继承了当今图像生成模型的巨大性（审美的多样性，梦幻般的描述等）。我们设计了一种简单而有效的方法来构建具有新颖有效的时空模块的 T2I 模型。首先，我们分解全时间 U-Net 和注意力张量，并在空间和时间上近似它们。其次，我们设计了一个时空管道，通过视频解码器、插值模型和两个超分辨率模型来生成高分辨率和帧速率视频，这些模型可以支持除 T2V 之外的各种应用。在空间和时间分辨率、对文本的忠实度和质量等各个方面，Make-A-Video 都在文本到视频生成方面树立了新的最先进水平，这通过定性和定量测量来确定。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Make-A-Video方法，通过结合文本-图像生成模型与无监督视频数据，实现无需文本-视频配对数据的文本到视频生成。
AI.motivation: 现有文本到视频生成方法依赖稀缺的文本-视频配对数据，且需从头学习多模态表示。本文旨在解决数据获取难题，利用已有文本-图像数据与视频数据加速模型训练。
AI.method: 分解时序U-Net与注意力张量进行时空近似，设计包含视频解码器、插值模型及双超分模块的空间-时序流水线，基于文本-图像模型扩展时空生成能力。
AI.result: 在时空分辨率、文本忠实度及视频质量等指标上达到文本到视频生成领域新SOTA，通过定性和定量评估验证效果。
AI.conclusion: Make-A-Video证明了无需文本-视频数据即可生成高质量视频的可行性，为文本到视频生成提供了高效且通用的解决方案。

search_text: Make-A-Video: Text-to-Video Generation without Text-Video Data 制作视频：无需文本视频数据即可生成文本到视频 We propose Make-A-Video -- an approach for directly translating the tremendous recent progress in Text-to-Image (T2I) generation to Text-to-Video (T2V). Our intuition is simple: learn what the world looks like and how it is described from paired text-image data, and learn how the world moves from unsupervised video footage. Make-A-Video has three advantages: (1) it accelerates training of the T2V model (it does not need to learn visual and multimodal representations from scratch), (2) it does not require paired text-video data, and (3) the generated videos inherit the vastness (diversity in aesthetic, fantastical depictions, etc.) of today's image generation models. We design a simple yet effective way to build on T2I models with novel and effective spatial-temporal modules. First, we decompose the full temporal U-Net and attention tensors and approximate them in space and time. Second, we design a spatial temporal pipeline to generate high resolution and frame rate videos with a video decoder, interpolation model and two super resolution models that can enable various applications besides T2V. In all aspects, spatial and temporal resolution, faithfulness to text, and quality, Make-A-Video sets the new state-of-the-art in text-to-video generation, as determined by both qualitative and quantitative measures. 我们提出 Make-A-Video——一种将文本到图像 (T2I) 生成的最新巨大进展直接转化为文本到视频 (T2V) 的方法。我们的直觉很简单：从配对的文本图像数据中了解世界是什么样子以及如何描述它，并从无监督的视频片段中了解世界如何移动。 Make-A-Video 具有三个优点：（1）它加速了 T2V 模型的训练（它不需要从头开始学习视觉和多模态表示），（2）它不需要配对的文本视频数据，（3）生成的视频继承了当今图像生成模型的巨大性（审美的多样性，梦幻般的描述等）。我们设计了一种简单而有效的方法来构建具有新颖有效的时空模块的 T2I 模型。首先，我们分解全时间 U-Net 和注意力张量，并在空间和时间上近似它们。其次，我们设计了一个时空管道，通过视频解码器、插值模型和两个超分辨率模型来生成高分辨率和帧速率视频，这些模型可以支持除 T2V 之外的各种应用。在空间和时间分辨率、对文本的忠实度和质量等各个方面，Make-A-Video 都在文本到视频生成方面树立了新的最先进水平，这通过定性和定量测量来确定。 本文提出Make-A-Video方法，通过结合文本-图像生成模型与无监督视频数据，实现无需文本-视频配对数据的文本到视频生成。 Uriel Singer,Adam Polyak,Thomas Hayes,Xi Yin,Jie An,Songyang Zhang,Qiyuan Hu,Harry Yang,Oron Ashual,Oran Gafni,Devi Parikh,Sonal Gupta,Yaniv Taigman cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Antoine Loriette,Baptiste Caramiaux,Sebastian Stein,John H. Williamson
title: Using models of baseline gameplay to design for physical rehabilitation
title_zh: 使用基线游戏模型来设计身体康复
abstract: Modified digital games manage to drive motivation in repetitive exercises needed for motor rehabilitation, however designing modifications that satisfy both rehabilitation and engagement goals is challenging. We present a method wherein a statistical model of baseline gameplay identifies design configurations that emulate behaviours compatible with unmodified play. We illustrate this approach through a case study involving upper limb rehabilitation with a custom controller for a Pac-Man game. A participatory design workshop with occupational therapists defined two interaction parameters for gameplay and rehabilitation adjustments. The parameters' effect on the interaction was measured experimentally with 12 participants. We show that a low-latency model, using both user input behaviour and internal game state, identifies values for interaction parameters that reproduce baseline gameplay under degraded control. We discuss how this method can be applied to systematically balance gamification problems involving trade-offs between physical requirements and subjectively engaging experiences.
summary: Modified digital games manage to drive motivation in repetitive exercises needed for motor rehabilitation, however designing modifications that satisfy both rehabilitation and engagement goals is challenging. We present a method wherein a statistical model of baseline gameplay identifies design configurations that emulate behaviours compatible with unmodified play. We illustrate this approach through a case study involving upper limb rehabilitation with a custom controller for a Pac-Man game. A participatory design workshop with occupational therapists defined two interaction parameters for gameplay and rehabilitation adjustments. The parameters' effect on the interaction was measured experimentally with 12 participants. We show that a low-latency model, using both user input behaviour and internal game state, identifies values for interaction parameters that reproduce baseline gameplay under degraded control. We discuss how this method can be applied to systematically balance gamification problems involving trade-offs between physical requirements and subjectively engaging experiences.
abstract_zh: 修改后的数字游戏设法激发运动康复所需的重复练习的动力，但是设计满足康复和参与目标的修改是具有挑战性的。我们提出了一种方法，其中基线游戏玩法的统计模型识别模拟与未修改的游戏兼容的行为的设计配置。我们通过一个案例研究来说明这种方法，该案例涉及使用吃豆人游戏的自定义控制器进行上肢康复。与职业治疗师一起举办的参与式设计研讨会定义了游戏玩法和康复调整的两个交互参数。 12 名参与者通过实验测量了参数对交互的影响。我们展示了一个低延迟模型，使用用户输入行为和内部游戏状态，识别交互参数的值，这些参数在降级控制下重现基线游戏玩法。我们讨论如何应用这种方法来系统地平衡涉及物理要求和主观参与体验之间权衡的游戏化问题。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于基线游戏玩法的统计模型，用于设计能够满足康复和娱乐目标的数字游戏。
AI.motivation: 修改后的数字游戏可以激励患者进行重复性运动康复训练，但设计满足康复和娱乐双重目标的游戏具有挑战性。
AI.method: 通过统计模型分析基线游戏玩法，识别出能够模拟未修改游戏行为的设计配置，并通过参与式设计工作坊与职业治疗师合作定义了两个交互参数，再通过实验测量这些参数对12名参与者的影响。
AI.result: 低延迟模型利用用户输入行为和游戏内部状态，识别出交互参数值，能够在控制退化的情况下再现基线游戏玩法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法可以系统地平衡康复游戏中的物理需求与主观娱乐体验之间的权衡问题。

authors_text: Jianning Li,Jana Fragemann,Seyed-Ahmad Ahmadi,Jens Kleesiek,Jan Egger
title: Training β-VAE by Aggregating a Learned Gaussian Posterior with a Decoupled Decoder
title_zh: 通过使用解耦解码器聚合学习的高斯后验来训练 β-VAE
abstract: The reconstruction loss and the Kullback-Leibler divergence (KLD) loss in a variational autoencoder (VAE) often play antagonistic roles, and tuning the weight of the KLD loss in $β$-VAE to achieve a balance between the two losses is a tricky and dataset-specific task. As a result, current practices in VAE training often result in a trade-off between the reconstruction fidelity and the continuity$/$disentanglement of the latent space, if the weight $β$ is not carefully tuned. In this paper, we present intuitions and a careful analysis of the antagonistic mechanism of the two losses, and propose, based on the insights, a simple yet effective two-stage method for training a VAE. Specifically, the method aggregates a learned Gaussian posterior $z \sim q_θ (z|x)$ with a decoder decoupled from the KLD loss, which is trained to learn a new conditional distribution $p_φ (x|z)$ of the input data $x$. Experimentally, we show that the aggregated VAE maximally satisfies the Gaussian assumption about the latent space, while still achieves a reconstruction error comparable to when the latent space is only loosely regularized by $\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0},I)$. The proposed approach does not require hyperparameter (i.e., the KLD weight $β$) tuning given a specific dataset as required in common VAE training practices. We evaluate the method using a medical dataset intended for 3D skull reconstruction and shape completion, and the results indicate promising generative capabilities of the VAE trained using the proposed method. Besides, through guided manipulation of the latent variables, we establish a connection between existing autoencoder (AE)-based approaches and generative approaches, such as VAE, for the shape completion problem. Codes and pre-trained weights are available at https://github.com/Jianningli/skullVAE
summary: The reconstruction loss and the Kullback-Leibler divergence (KLD) loss in a variational autoencoder (VAE) often play antagonistic roles, and tuning the weight of the KLD loss in $β$-VAE to achieve a balance between the two losses is a tricky and dataset-specific task. As a result, current practices in VAE training often result in a trade-off between the reconstruction fidelity and the continuity$/$disentanglement of the latent space, if the weight $β$ is not carefully tuned. In this paper, we present intuitions and a careful analysis of the antagonistic mechanism of the two losses, and propose, based on the insights, a simple yet effective two-stage method for training a VAE. Specifically, the method aggregates a learned Gaussian posterior $z \sim q_θ (z|x)$ with a decoder decoupled from the KLD loss, which is trained to learn a new conditional distribution $p_φ (x|z)$ of the input data $x$. Experimentally, we show that the aggregated VAE maximally satisfies the Gaussian assumption about the latent space, while still achieves a reconstruction error comparable to when the latent space is only loosely regularized by $\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0},I)$. The proposed approach does not require hyperparameter (i.e., the KLD weight $β$) tuning given a specific dataset as required in common VAE training practices. We evaluate the method using a medical dataset intended for 3D skull reconstruction and shape completion, and the results indicate promising generative capabilities of the VAE trained using the proposed method. Besides, through guided manipulation of the latent variables, we establish a connection between existing autoencoder (AE)-based approaches and generative approaches, such as VAE, for the shape completion problem. Codes and pre-trained weights are available at https://github.com/Jianningli/skullVAE
abstract_zh: 变分自编码器 (VAE) 中的重建损失和 Kullback-Leibler 散度 (KLD) 损失通常起着对立的作用，调整 $β$-VAE 中 KLD 损失的权重以实现两种损失之间的平衡是一项棘手且特定于数据集的任务。因此，如果权重 $β$ 没有仔细调整，当前 VAE 训练的实践通常会导致重建保真度和潜在空间的连续性$/$解开之间的权衡。在本文中，我们提出了对两种损失的对抗机制的直觉和仔细分析，并根据这些见解提出了一种简单而有效的两阶段训练 VAE 的方法。具体来说，该方法将学习到的高斯后验 $z \sim q_θ (z|x)$ 与与 KLD 损失解耦的解码器聚合在一起，该解码器经过训练以学习输入数据 $x$ 的新条件分布 $p_φ (x|z)$。实验表明，聚合的 VAE 最大程度地满足关于潜在空间的高斯假设，同时仍然实现了与潜在空间仅通过 $\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0},I)$ 松散正则化时相当的重建误差。所提出的方法不需要根据常见 VAE 训练实践中所需的特定数据集进行超参数（即 KLD 权重 $β$）调整。我们使用用于 3D 头骨重建和形状完成的医学数据集评估该方法，结果表明使用所提出的方法训练的 VAE 具有良好的生成能力。此外，通过对潜在变量的引导操作，我们在现有的基于自动编码器（AE）的方法和生成方法（例如 VAE）之间建立了联系，以解决形状完成问题。代码和预训练权重可在 https://github.com/Jianningli/skullVAE 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需调整超参数β的β-VAE训练方法，通过聚合学习到的高斯后验与解耦的解码器，解决重构损失与KLD损失的对抗问题。
AI.motivation: 传统β-VAE需精细调整KLD损失权重β以平衡重构保真度与潜在空间连续性/解耦性，但该过程依赖数据集且难以实现最优。
AI.method: 采用两阶段训练：首先学习高斯后验分布qθ(z|x)，再训练与KLD损失解耦的解码器pφ(x|z)，通过聚合两者实现潜在空间的高斯假设与重构性能的平衡。
AI.result: 在医学3D颅骨重建数据集上验证，方法无需β调参即可达到与松散正则化VAE相当的重构误差，并通过潜在变量操控建立了自编码器与生成模型在形状补全任务的联系。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了β-VAE的超参数敏感性问题，在保持潜在空间性质的同时实现了高质量重构，为形状补全任务提供了新的生成模型训练范式。

search_text: Training β-VAE by Aggregating a Learned Gaussian Posterior with a Decoupled Decoder 通过使用解耦解码器聚合学习的高斯后验来训练 β-VAE The reconstruction loss and the Kullback-Leibler divergence (KLD) loss in a variational autoencoder (VAE) often play antagonistic roles, and tuning the weight of the KLD loss in $β$-VAE to achieve a balance between the two losses is a tricky and dataset-specific task. As a result, current practices in VAE training often result in a trade-off between the reconstruction fidelity and the continuity$/$disentanglement of the latent space, if the weight $β$ is not carefully tuned. In this paper, we present intuitions and a careful analysis of the antagonistic mechanism of the two losses, and propose, based on the insights, a simple yet effective two-stage method for training a VAE. Specifically, the method aggregates a learned Gaussian posterior $z \sim q_θ (z|x)$ with a decoder decoupled from the KLD loss, which is trained to learn a new conditional distribution $p_φ (x|z)$ of the input data $x$. Experimentally, we show that the aggregated VAE maximally satisfies the Gaussian assumption about the latent space, while still achieves a reconstruction error comparable to when the latent space is only loosely regularized by $\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0},I)$. The proposed approach does not require hyperparameter (i.e., the KLD weight $β$) tuning given a specific dataset as required in common VAE training practices. We evaluate the method using a medical dataset intended for 3D skull reconstruction and shape completion, and the results indicate promising generative capabilities of the VAE trained using the proposed method. Besides, through guided manipulation of the latent variables, we establish a connection between existing autoencoder (AE)-based approaches and generative approaches, such as VAE, for the shape completion problem. Codes and pre-trained weights are available at https://github.com/Jianningli/skullVAE 变分自编码器 (VAE) 中的重建损失和 Kullback-Leibler 散度 (KLD) 损失通常起着对立的作用，调整 $β$-VAE 中 KLD 损失的权重以实现两种损失之间的平衡是一项棘手且特定于数据集的任务。因此，如果权重 $β$ 没有仔细调整，当前 VAE 训练的实践通常会导致重建保真度和潜在空间的连续性$/$解开之间的权衡。在本文中，我们提出了对两种损失的对抗机制的直觉和仔细分析，并根据这些见解提出了一种简单而有效的两阶段训练 VAE 的方法。具体来说，该方法将学习到的高斯后验 $z \sim q_θ (z|x)$ 与与 KLD 损失解耦的解码器聚合在一起，该解码器经过训练以学习输入数据 $x$ 的新条件分布 $p_φ (x|z)$。实验表明，聚合的 VAE 最大程度地满足关于潜在空间的高斯假设，同时仍然实现了与潜在空间仅通过 $\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0},I)$ 松散正则化时相当的重建误差。所提出的方法不需要根据常见 VAE 训练实践中所需的特定数据集进行超参数（即 KLD 权重 $β$）调整。我们使用用于 3D 头骨重建和形状完成的医学数据集评估该方法，结果表明使用所提出的方法训练的 VAE 具有良好的生成能力。此外，通过对潜在变量的引导操作，我们在现有的基于自动编码器（AE）的方法和生成方法（例如 VAE）之间建立了联系，以解决形状完成问题。代码和预训练权重可在 https://github.com/Jianningli/skullVAE 获取 本文提出一种无需调整超参数β的β-VAE训练方法，通过聚合学习到的高斯后验与解耦的解码器，解决重构损失与KLD损失的对抗问题。 Jianning Li,Jana Fragemann,Seyed-Ahmad Ahmadi,Jens Kleesiek,Jan Egger cs.CV

title: A case study of spatiotemporal forecasting techniques for weather forecasting
title_zh: 天气预报时空预报技术案例研究
abstract: The majority of real-world processes are spatiotemporal, and the data generated by them exhibits both spatial and temporal evolution. Weather is one of the most essential processes in this domain, and weather forecasting has become a crucial part of our daily routine. Weather data analysis is considered the most complex and challenging task. Although numerical weather prediction models are currently state-of-the-art, they are resource-intensive and time-consuming. Numerous studies have proposed time series-based models as a viable alternative to numerical forecasts. Recent research in the area of time series analysis indicates significant advancements, particularly regarding the use of state-space-based models (white box) and, more recently, the integration of machine learning and deep neural network-based models (black box). The most famous examples of such models are RNNs and transformers. These models have demonstrated remarkable results in the field of time-series analysis and have demonstrated effectiveness in modelling temporal correlations. It is crucial to capture both temporal and spatial correlations for a spatiotemporal process, as the values at nearby locations and time affect the values of a spatiotemporal process at a specific point. This self-contained paper explores various regional data-driven weather forecasting methods, i.e., forecasting over multiple latitude-longitude points (matrix-shaped spatial grid) to capture spatiotemporal correlations. The results showed that spatiotemporal prediction models reduced computational costs while improving accuracy. In particular, the proposed tensor train dynamic mode decomposition-based forecasting model has comparable accuracy to the state-of-the-art models without the need for training. We provide convincing numerical experiments to show that the proposed approach is practical.
summary: The majority of real-world processes are spatiotemporal, and the data generated by them exhibits both spatial and temporal evolution. Weather is one of the most essential processes in this domain, and weather forecasting has become a crucial part of our daily routine. Weather data analysis is considered the most complex and challenging task. Although numerical weather prediction models are currently state-of-the-art, they are resource-intensive and time-consuming. Numerous studies have proposed time series-based models as a viable alternative to numerical forecasts. Recent research in the area of time series analysis indicates significant advancements, particularly regarding the use of state-space-based models (white box) and, more recently, the integration of machine learning and deep neural network-based models (black box). The most famous examples of such models are RNNs and transformers. These models have demonstrated remarkable results in the field of time-series analysis and have demonstrated effectiveness in modelling temporal correlations. It is crucial to capture both temporal and spatial correlations for a spatiotemporal process, as the values at nearby locations and time affect the values of a spatiotemporal process at a specific point. This self-contained paper explores various regional data-driven weather forecasting methods, i.e., forecasting over multiple latitude-longitude points (matrix-shaped spatial grid) to capture spatiotemporal correlations. The results showed that spatiotemporal prediction models reduced computational costs while improving accuracy. In particular, the proposed tensor train dynamic mode decomposition-based forecasting model has comparable accuracy to the state-of-the-art models without the need for training. We provide convincing numerical experiments to show that the proposed approach is practical.
abstract_zh: 大多数现实世界的过程都是时空的，它们生成的数据表现出空间和时间的演变。天气是该领域最重要的过程之一，天气预报已成为我们日常生活的重要组成部分。天气数据分析被认为是最复杂和最具挑战性的任务。尽管数值天气预报模型目前是最先进的，但它们是资源密集型且耗时的。许多研究提出基于时间序列的模型作为数值预测的可行替代方案。时间序列分析领域的最新研究表明了显着的进步，特别是在使用基于状态空间的模型（白盒）以及最近的机器学习和基于深度神经网络的模型（黑盒）的集成方面。此类模型最著名的例子是 RNN 和 Transformer。这些模型在时间序列分析领域展示了显着的成果，并在建模时间相关性方面展示了有效性。捕获时空过程的时间和空间相关性至关重要，因为附近位置和时间的值会影响特定点的时空过程的值。这篇独立的论文探讨了各种区域数据驱动的天气预报方法，即对多个经纬度点（矩阵状空间网格）进行预测以捕获时空相关性。结果表明，时空预测模型降低了计算成本，同时提高了准确性。特别是，所提出的基于张量序列动态模式分解的预测模型具有与最先进模型相当的精度，而无需训练。我们提供了令人信服的数值实验来证明所提出的方法是实用的。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了多种基于区域数据驱动的时空预测方法，用于提高天气预报的准确性和效率。
AI.motivation: 传统数值天气预报模型计算成本高且耗时，需探索更高效的替代方案以捕捉时空相关性。
AI.method: 提出基于张量训练动态模态分解（Tensor Train DMD）的预测模型，无需训练即可实现时空关联建模。
AI.result: 实验表明，时空预测模型在降低计算成本的同时提升了精度，所提模型效果与先进方法相当。
AI.conclusion: 张量训练DMD模型为天气预报提供了无需训练的高效解决方案，具有实际应用价值。

title: Batch Normalization Explained
title_zh: 批量归一化解释
abstract: A critically important, ubiquitous, and yet poorly understood ingredient in modern deep networks (DNs) is batch normalization (BN), which centers and normalizes the feature maps. To date, only limited progress has been made understanding why BN boosts DN learning and inference performance; work has focused exclusively on showing that BN smooths a DN's loss landscape. In this paper, we study BN theoretically from the perspective of function approximation; we exploit the fact that most of today's state-of-the-art DNs are continuous piecewise affine (CPA) splines that fit a predictor to the training data via affine mappings defined over a partition of the input space (the so-called "linear regions"). {\em We demonstrate that BN is an unsupervised learning technique that -- independent of the DN's weights or gradient-based learning -- adapts the geometry of a DN's spline partition to match the data.} BN provides a "smart initialization" that boosts the performance of DN learning, because it adapts even a DN initialized with random weights to align its spline partition with the data. We also show that the variation of BN statistics between mini-batches introduces a dropout-like random perturbation to the partition boundaries and hence the decision boundary for classification problems. This per mini-batch perturbation reduces overfitting and improves generalization by increasing the margin between the training samples and the decision boundary.
summary: A critically important, ubiquitous, and yet poorly understood ingredient in modern deep networks (DNs) is batch normalization (BN), which centers and normalizes the feature maps. To date, only limited progress has been made understanding why BN boosts DN learning and inference performance; work has focused exclusively on showing that BN smooths a DN's loss landscape. In this paper, we study BN theoretically from the perspective of function approximation; we exploit the fact that most of today's state-of-the-art DNs are continuous piecewise affine (CPA) splines that fit a predictor to the training data via affine mappings defined over a partition of the input space (the so-called "linear regions"). {\em We demonstrate that BN is an unsupervised learning technique that -- independent of the DN's weights or gradient-based learning -- adapts the geometry of a DN's spline partition to match the data.} BN provides a "smart initialization" that boosts the performance of DN learning, because it adapts even a DN initialized with random weights to align its spline partition with the data. We also show that the variation of BN statistics between mini-batches introduces a dropout-like random perturbation to the partition boundaries and hence the decision boundary for classification problems. This per mini-batch perturbation reduces overfitting and improves generalization by increasing the margin between the training samples and the decision boundary.
abstract_zh: 现代深度网络（DN）中一个极其重要、普遍存在但人们却知之甚少的成分是批量归一化（BN），它对特征图进行集中和归一化。迄今为止，对于 BN 为何能够提升 DN 学习和推理性能的理解，仅取得了有限的进展；工作重点是展示 BN 平滑 DN 的损失情况。本文从函数逼近的角度对BN进行了理论研究；我们利用这样一个事实，即当今大多数最先进的 DN 都是连续分段仿射 (CPA) 样条，它们通过在输入空间分区（所谓的“线性区域”）上定义的仿射映射将预测器拟合到训练数据。 {\em我们证明 BN 是一种无监督学习技术——独立于 DN 的权重或基于梯度的学习——调整 DN 样条分区的几何形状以匹配数据。}BN 提供了“智能初始化”，可以提高 DN 学习的性能，因为它甚至可以调整使用随机权重初始化的 DN，以将其样条分区与数据对齐。我们还表明，小批量之间 BN 统计数据的变化对分区边界以及分类问题的决策边界引入了类似 dropout 的随机扰动。这种每小批量扰动通过增加训练样本和决策边界之间的余量来减少过度拟合并提高泛化能力。

AI.tldr: 本文从函数逼近理论角度解释批量归一化（BN）在深度网络中的作用，揭示其如何通过调整网络的分片结构提升性能。
AI.motivation: 尽管批量归一化在深度学习中广泛应用，但其提升模型性能的机制尚未被充分理解，现有研究仅关注其平滑损失景观的效果。
AI.method: 将现代深度网络视为连续分段仿射（CPA）样条，通过理论分析证明BN是一种无监督学习技术，能自适应调整网络分片结构以匹配数据分布。
AI.result: BN通过初始化阶段对齐分片结构提升学习效率，并利用小批量统计变化引入类似dropout的扰动，增强模型泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: BN的核心贡献在于提供智能初始化并优化决策边界，其理论解释为深度网络设计提供了新视角。

search_text: Batch Normalization Explained 批量归一化解释 A critically important, ubiquitous, and yet poorly understood ingredient in modern deep networks (DNs) is batch normalization (BN), which centers and normalizes the feature maps. To date, only limited progress has been made understanding why BN boosts DN learning and inference performance; work has focused exclusively on showing that BN smooths a DN's loss landscape. In this paper, we study BN theoretically from the perspective of function approximation; we exploit the fact that most of today's state-of-the-art DNs are continuous piecewise affine (CPA) splines that fit a predictor to the training data via affine mappings defined over a partition of the input space (the so-called "linear regions"). {\em We demonstrate that BN is an unsupervised learning technique that -- independent of the DN's weights or gradient-based learning -- adapts the geometry of a DN's spline partition to match the data.} BN provides a "smart initialization" that boosts the performance of DN learning, because it adapts even a DN initialized with random weights to align its spline partition with the data. We also show that the variation of BN statistics between mini-batches introduces a dropout-like random perturbation to the partition boundaries and hence the decision boundary for classification problems. This per mini-batch perturbation reduces overfitting and improves generalization by increasing the margin between the training samples and the decision boundary. 现代深度网络（DN）中一个极其重要、普遍存在但人们却知之甚少的成分是批量归一化（BN），它对特征图进行集中和归一化。迄今为止，对于 BN 为何能够提升 DN 学习和推理性能的理解，仅取得了有限的进展；工作重点是展示 BN 平滑 DN 的损失情况。本文从函数逼近的角度对BN进行了理论研究；我们利用这样一个事实，即当今大多数最先进的 DN 都是连续分段仿射 (CPA) 样条，它们通过在输入空间分区（所谓的“线性区域”）上定义的仿射映射将预测器拟合到训练数据。 {\em我们证明 BN 是一种无监督学习技术——独立于 DN 的权重或基于梯度的学习——调整 DN 样条分区的几何形状以匹配数据。}BN 提供了“智能初始化”，可以提高 DN 学习的性能，因为它甚至可以调整使用随机权重初始化的 DN，以将其样条分区与数据对齐。我们还表明，小批量之间 BN 统计数据的变化对分区边界以及分类问题的决策边界引入了类似 dropout 的随机扰动。这种每小批量扰动通过增加训练样本和决策边界之间的余量来减少过度拟合并提高泛化能力。 本文从函数逼近理论角度解释批量归一化（BN）在深度网络中的作用，揭示其如何通过调整网络的分片结构提升性能。 Randall Balestriero,Richard G. title: On Constructing Spanners from Random Gaussian Projections
title_zh: 关于从随机高斯投影构造扳手
abstract: Graph sketching is a powerful paradigm for analyzing graph structure via linear measurements introduced by Ahn, Guha, and McGregor (SODA'12) that has since found numerous applications in streaming, distributed computing, and massively parallel algorithms, among others. Graph sketching has proven to be quite successful for various problems such as connectivity, minimum spanning trees, edge or vertex connectivity, and cut or spectral sparsifiers. Yet, the problem of approximating shortest path metric of a graph, and specifically computing a spanner, is notably missing from the list of successes. This has turned the status of this fundamental problem into one of the most longstanding open questions in this area. We present a partial explanation of this lack of success by proving a strong lower bound for a large family of graph sketching algorithms that encompasses prior work on spanners and many (but importantly not also all) related cut-based problems mentioned above. Our lower bound matches the algorithmic bounds of the recent result of Filtser, Kapralov, and Nouri (SODA'21), up to lower order terms, for constructing spanners via the same graph sketching family. This establishes near-optimality of these bounds, at least restricted to this family of graph sketching techniques, and makes progress on a conjecture posed in this latter work.
summary: Graph sketching is a powerful paradigm for analyzing graph structure via linear measurements introduced by Ahn, Guha, and McGregor (SODA'12) that has since found numerous applications in streaming, distributed computing, and massively parallel algorithms, among others. Graph sketching has proven to be quite successful for various problems such as connectivity, minimum spanning trees, edge or vertex connectivity, and cut or spectral sparsifiers. Yet, the problem of approximating shortest path metric of a graph, and specifically computing a spanner, is notably missing from the list of successes. This has turned the status of this fundamental problem into one of the most longstanding open questions in this area. We present a partial explanation of this lack of success by proving a strong lower bound for a large family of graph sketching algorithms that encompasses prior work on spanners and many (but importantly not also all) related cut-based problems mentioned above. Our lower bound matches the algorithmic bounds of the recent result of Filtser, Kapralov, and Nouri (SODA'21), up to lower order terms, for constructing spanners via the same graph sketching family. This establishes near-optimality of these bounds, at least restricted to this family of graph sketching techniques, and makes progress on a conjecture posed in this latter work.
abstract_zh: 图草图是一种强大的范例，用于通过 Ahn、Guha 和 McGregor (SODA'12) 引入的线性测量来分析图结构，此后在流式处理、分布式计算和大规模并行算法等领域找到了众多应用。事实证明，图形草图对于各种问题（例如连通性、最小生成树、边或顶点连通性以及切割或光谱稀疏器）非常成功。然而，成功列表中明显缺少近似图的最短路径度量，特别是计算扳手的问题。这使得这一根本问题的地位成为该领域最长期悬而未决的问题之一。我们通过证明一大批图形草图算法的强大下界来部分解释这种缺乏成功的情况，这些算法包含了扳手和上述许多（但重要的是不是全部）相关的基于切割的问题的先前工作。我们的下界与 Filtser、Kapralov 和 Nouri (SODA'21) 的最新结果的算法界限相匹配，直至低阶项，用于通过相同的图形草图系列构建扳手。这建立了这些边界的近乎最优，至少限于这一系列图形绘制技术，并在后一项工作中提出的猜想上取得了进展。

AI.tldr: 论文探讨了通过图草图技术构造图的spanner的挑战，并证明了该方法的强下界，解释了为何此前未能成功。
AI.motivation: 尽管图草图在多个领域应用广泛，但构造图的spanner（稀疏子图）问题长期未被解决，成为该领域的核心挑战之一。
AI.method: 通过证明针对一大类图草图算法的强下界，涵盖先前spanner相关工作及部分割问题方法。
AI.result: 证明的下界与近期结果的算法界匹配，证明了在该算法族下的近似最优性，并推进了相关猜想。
AI.conclusion: 表明在该类图草图技术下，现有方法已近似最优，为解决spanner问题提供了理论限制，并推进了相关猜想。

ai_content.tldr: 论文探讨了通过图草图技术构造图的spanner的挑战，并证明了该方法的强下界，解释了为何此前未能成功。

title: RECALL: Rehearsal-free Continual Learning for Object Classification
title_zh: RECALL：无需排练的对象分类持续学习
comment: Accepted as contributed paper at the 2022 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS 2022)
comments: Accepted as contributed paper at the 2022 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS 2022)
abstract: Convolutional neural networks show remarkable results in classification but struggle with learning new things on the fly. We present a novel rehearsal-free approach, where a deep neural network is continually learning new unseen object categories without saving any data of prior sequences. Our approach is called RECALL, as the network recalls categories by calculating logits for old categories before training new ones. These are then used during training to avoid changing the old categories. For each new sequence, a new head is added to accommodate the new categories. To mitigate forgetting, we present a regularization strategy where we replace the classification with a regression. Moreover, for the known categories, we propose a Mahalanobis loss that includes the variances to account for the changing densities between known and unknown categories. Finally, we present a novel dataset for continual learning, especially suited for object recognition on a mobile robot (HOWS-CL-25), including 150,795 synthetic images of 25 household object categories. Our approach RECALL outperforms the current state of the art on CORe50 and iCIFAR-100 and reaches the best performance on HOWS-CL-25.
summary: Convolutional neural networks show remarkable results in classification but struggle with learning new things on the fly. We present a novel rehearsal-free approach, where a deep neural network is continually learning new unseen object categories without saving any data of prior sequences. Our approach is called RECALL, as the network recalls categories by calculating logits for old categories before training new ones. These are then used during training to avoid changing the old categories. For each new sequence, a new head is added to accommodate the new categories. To mitigate forgetting, we present a regularization strategy where we replace the classification with a regression. Moreover, for the known categories, we propose a Mahalanobis loss that includes the variances to account for the changing densities between known and unknown categories. Finally, we present a novel dataset for continual learning, especially suited for object recognition on a mobile robot (HOWS-CL-25), including 150,795 synthetic images of 25 household object categories. Our approach RECALL outperforms the current state of the art on CORe50 and iCIFAR-100 and reaches the best performance on HOWS-CL-25.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络在分类方面显示出显着的效果，但在动态学习新事物方面却遇到了困难。我们提出了一种新颖的免排练方法，其中深度神经网络不断学习新的未见过的对象类别，而不保存先前序列的任何数据。我们的方法称为 RECALL，因为网络在训练新类别之前通过计算旧类别的逻辑来回忆类别。然后在训练期间使用它们以避免更改旧类别。对于每个新序列，都会添加一个新头以适应新类别。为了减少遗忘，我们提出了一种正则化策略，用回归代替分类。此外，对于已知类别，我们提出了马哈拉诺比斯损失，其中包括用于解释已知类别和未知类别之间密度变化的方差。最后，我们提出了一个用于持续学习的新颖数据集，特别适合移动机器人上的物体识别 (HOWS-CL-25)，包括 25 个家庭物体类别的 150,795 个合成图像。我们的方法 RECALL 优于 CORe50 和 iCIFAR-100 上的当前最新技术，并在 HOWS-CL-25 上达到最佳性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需回放的持续学习方法RECALL，用于在不保存先前数据的情况下不断学习新的物体分类任务。
AI.motivation: 卷积神经网络在分类任务中表现优异，但难以在不遗忘旧知识的情况下实时学习新类别。现有方法通常需要存储历史数据，存在存储开销和隐私问题。
AI.method: 通过为新类别添加新输出头，利用回归正则化替代分类损失，并引入考虑类别密度变化的马氏损失函数。训练时通过计算旧类别的logits来防止遗忘，无需存储历史数据。
AI.result: 在CORe50和iCIFAR-100数据集上优于现有方法，在作者构建的移动机器人物体识别数据集HOWS-CL-25上达到最优性能。
AI.conclusion: RECALL通过创新的损失函数设计和网络结构，在无需存储历史数据的前提下有效解决了持续学习中的遗忘问题。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种无需回放的持续学习方法RECALL，用于在不保存先前数据的情况下不断学习新的物体分类任务。
ai_content.motivation: 卷积神经网络在分类任务中表现优异，但难以在不遗忘旧知识的情况下实时学习新类别。现有方法通常需要存储历史数据，存在存储开销和隐私问题。
ai_content.method: 通过为新类别添加新输出头，利用回归正则化替代分类损失，并引入考虑类别密度变化的马氏损失函数。训练时通过计算旧类别的logits来防止遗忘，无需存储历史数据。
ai_content.result: 在CORe50和iCIFAR-100数据集上优于现有方法，在作者构建的移动机器人物体识别数据集HOWS-CL-25上达到最优性能。
ai_content.conclusion: RECALL通过创新的损失函数设计和网络结构，在无需存储历史数据的前提下有效解决了持续学习中的遗忘问题。

method: 通过为新类别添加新输出头，利用回归正则化替代分类损失，并引入考虑类别密度变化的马氏损失函数。训练时通过计算旧类别的logits来防止遗忘，无需存储历史数据。

search_text: RECALL: Rehearsal-free Continual Learning for Object Classification RECALL：无需排练的对象分类持续学习 Convolutional neural networks show remarkable results in classification but struggle with learning new things on the fly. We present a novel rehearsal-free approach, where a deep neural network is continually learning new unseen object categories without saving any data of prior sequences. Our approach is called RECALL, as the network recalls categories by calculating logits for old categories before training new ones. These are then used during training to avoid changing the old categories. For each new sequence, a new head is added to accommodate the new categories. To mitigate forgetting, we present a regularization strategy where we replace the classification with a regression. Moreover, for the known categories, we propose a Mahalanobis loss that includes the variances to account for the changing densities between known and unknown categories. Finally, we present a novel dataset for continual learning, especially suited for object recognition on a mobile robot (HOWS-CL-25), including 150,795 synthetic images of 25 household object categories. Our approach RECALL outperforms the current state of the art on CORe50 and iCIFAR-100 and reaches the best performance on HOWS-CL-25. 卷积神经网络在分类方面显示出显着的效果，但在动态学习新事物方面却遇到了困难。我们提出了一种新颖的免排练方法，其中深度神经网络不断学习新的未见过的对象类别，而不保存先前序列的任何数据。我们的方法称为 RECALL，因为网络在训练新类别之前通过计算旧类别的逻辑来回忆类别。然后在训练期间使用它们以避免更改旧类别。对于每个新序列，都会添加一个新头以适应新类别。为了减少遗忘，我们提出了一种正则化策略，用回归代替分类。此外，对于已知类别，我们提出了马哈拉诺比斯损失，其中包括用于解释已知类别和未知类别之间密度变化的方差。最后，我们提出了一个用于持续学习的新颖数据集，特别适合移动机器人上的物体识别 (HOWS-CL-25)，包括 25 个家庭物体类别的 150,795 个合成图像。我们的方法 RECALL 优于 CORe50 和 iCIFAR-100 上的当前最新技术，并在 HOWS-CL-25 上达到最佳性能。 本文提出一种无需回放的持续学习方法RECALL，用于在不保存先前数据的情况下不断学习新的物体分类任务。 Markus Knauer,Maximilian Denninger,Rudolph Triebel cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Mete Ahishali,Aysen Degerli,Serkan Kiranyaz,Tahir Hamid,Rashid Mazhar,Moncef Gabbouj
title: R2C-GAN: Restore-to-Classify Generative Adversarial Networks for Blind X-Ray Restoration and COVID-19 Classification
title_zh: R2C-GAN：用于盲 X 射线恢复和 COVID-19 分类的恢复分类生成对抗网络
abstract: Restoration of poor quality images with a blended set of artifacts plays a vital role for a reliable diagnosis. Existing studies have focused on specific restoration problems such as image deblurring, denoising, and exposure correction where there is usually a strong assumption on the artifact type and severity. As a pioneer study in blind X-ray restoration, we propose a joint model for generic image restoration and classification: Restore-to-Classify Generative Adversarial Networks (R2C-GANs). Such a jointly optimized model keeps any disease intact after the restoration. Therefore, this will naturally lead to a higher diagnosis performance thanks to the improved X-ray image quality. To accomplish this crucial objective, we define the restoration task as an Image-to-Image translation problem from poor quality having noisy, blurry, or over/under-exposed images to high quality image domain. The proposed R2C-GAN model is able to learn forward and inverse transforms between the two domains using unpaired training samples. Simultaneously, the joint classification preserves the disease label during restoration. Moreover, the R2C-GANs are equipped with operational layers/neurons reducing the network depth and further boosting both restoration and classification performances. The proposed joint model is extensively evaluated over the QaTa-COV19 dataset for Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) classification. The proposed restoration approach achieves over 90% F1-Score which is significantly higher than the performance of any deep model. Moreover, in the qualitative analysis, the restoration performance of R2C-GANs is approved by a group of medical doctors. We share the software implementation at https://github.com/meteahishali/R2C-GAN.
summary: Restoration of poor quality images with a blended set of artifacts plays a vital role for a reliable diagnosis. Existing studies have focused on specific restoration problems such as image deblurring, denoising, and exposure correction where there is usually a strong assumption on the artifact type and severity. As a pioneer study in blind X-ray restoration, we propose a joint model for generic image restoration and classification: Restore-to-Classify Generative Adversarial Networks (R2C-GANs). Such a jointly optimized model keeps any disease intact after the restoration. Therefore, this will naturally lead to a higher diagnosis performance thanks to the improved X-ray image quality. To accomplish this crucial objective, we define the restoration task as an Image-to-Image translation problem from poor quality having noisy, blurry, or over/under-exposed images to high quality image domain. The proposed R2C-GAN model is able to learn forward and inverse transforms between the two domains using unpaired training samples. Simultaneously, the joint classification preserves the disease label during restoration. Moreover, the R2C-GANs are equipped with operational layers/neurons reducing the network depth and further boosting both restoration and classification performances. The proposed joint model is extensively evaluated over the QaTa-COV19 dataset for Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) classification. The proposed restoration approach achieves over 90% F1-Score which is significantly higher than the performance of any deep model. Moreover, in the qualitative analysis, the restoration performance of R2C-GANs is approved by a group of medical doctors. We share the software implementation at https://github.com/meteahishali/R2C-GAN.
abstract_zh: 使用一组混合伪影恢复低质量图像对于可靠的诊断起着至关重要的作用。现有的研究主要集中在特定的恢复问题上，例如图像去模糊、去噪和曝光校正，这些问题通常对伪影类型和严重程度有很强的假设。作为盲 X 射线恢复的先驱研究，我们提出了一种用于通用图像恢复和分类的联合模型：恢复分类生成对抗网络（R2C-GAN）。这种联合优化的模型可以使任何疾病在恢复后保持完好无损。因此，由于 X 射线图像质量的提高，这自然会带来更高的诊断性能。为了实现这一关键目标，我们将恢复任务定义为图像到图像的转换问题，从具有噪声、模糊或曝光过度/曝光不足的低质量图像到高质量图像域。所提出的 R2C-GAN 模型能够使用不成对的训练样本来学习两个域之间的正向和反向变换。同时，关节分类在恢复过程中保留了疾病标签。此外，R2C-GAN 配备了操作层/神经元，减少了网络深度，进一步提高了恢复和分类性能。所提出的联合模型在 QaTa-COV19 数据集上针对 2019 年冠状病毒病 (COVID-19) 分类进行了广泛评估。所提出的恢复方法实现了 90% 以上的 F1-Score，明显高于任何深度模型的性能。此外，在定性分析中，R2C-GAN的恢复性能得到了一组医生的认可。我们在 https://github.com/meteahishali/R2C-GAN 分享软件实现。

AI.tldr: 本文提出R2C-GAN模型，通过联合优化图像恢复与分类任务，实现盲X光图像恢复及COVID-19分类。
AI.motivation: 现有图像恢复方法针对单一伪影类型且需先验假设，无法处理混合未知伪影；盲恢复需在提升图像质量的同时保留疾病特征以保障分类准确性。
AI.method: 将恢复任务定义为低质量到高质量的图像翻译问题，利用未配对样本学习双向映射，通过操作层减少网络深度并同步保持疾病标签进行分类。
AI.result: 在QaTa-COV19数据集上恢复F1-Score超90%，优于其他深度模型，且获得医学专家定性认可。
AI.conclusion: R2C-GAN通过联合优化恢复与分类，有效提升诊断性能，为盲图像恢复与疾病分类提供新方法。

title: Speeding Up Action Recognition Using Dynamic Accumulation of Residuals in Compressed Domain
title_zh: 使用压缩域中残差的动态累积加速动作识别
abstract: With the widespread use of installed cameras, video-based monitoring approaches have seized considerable attention for different purposes like assisted living. Temporal redundancy and the sheer size of raw videos are the two most common problematic issues related to video processing algorithms. Most of the existing methods mainly focused on increasing accuracy by exploring consecutive frames, which is laborious and cannot be considered for real-time applications. Since videos are mostly stored and transmitted in compressed format, these kinds of videos are available on many devices. Compressed videos contain a multitude of beneficial information, such as motion vectors and quantized coefficients. Proper use of this available information can greatly improve the video understanding methods' performance. This paper presents an approach for using residual data, available in compressed videos directly, which can be obtained by a light partially decoding procedure. In addition, a method for accumulating similar residuals is proposed, which dramatically reduces the number of processed frames for action recognition. Applying neural networks exclusively for accumulated residuals in the compressed domain accelerates performance, while the classification results are highly competitive with raw video approaches.
summary: With the widespread use of installed cameras, video-based monitoring approaches have seized considerable attention for different purposes like assisted living. Temporal redundancy and the sheer size of raw videos are the two most common problematic issues related to video processing algorithms. Most of the existing methods mainly focused on increasing accuracy by exploring consecutive frames, which is laborious and cannot be considered for real-time applications. Since videos are mostly stored and transmitted in compressed format, these kinds of videos are available on many devices. Compressed videos contain a multitude of beneficial information, such as motion vectors and quantized coefficients. Proper use of this available information can greatly improve the video understanding methods' performance. This paper presents an approach for using residual data, available in compressed videos directly, which can be obtained by a light partially decoding procedure. In addition, a method for accumulating similar residuals is proposed, which dramatically reduces the number of processed frames for action recognition. Applying neural networks exclusively for accumulated residuals in the compressed domain accelerates performance, while the classification results are highly competitive with raw video approaches.
abstract_zh: 随着安装摄像头的广泛使用，基于视频的监控方法在辅助生活等不同目的中引起了相当大的关注。时间冗余和原始视频的巨大尺寸是与视频处理算法相关的两个最常见的问题。现有的大多数方法主要集中在通过探索连续帧来提高准确性，这很费力并且不能考虑用于实时应用。由于视频大多以压缩格式存储和传输，因此此类视频可在许多设备上使用。压缩视频包含大量有用信息，例如运动矢量和量化系数。正确使用这些可用信息可以极大地提高视频理解方法的性能。本文提出了一种使用直接在压缩视频中可用的残差数据的方法，该残差数据可以通过轻量部分解码过程获得。此外，还提出了一种累积相似残差的方法，大大减少了动作识别的处理帧数。将神经网络专门应用于压缩域中的累积残差可以提高性能，而分类结果与原始视频方法相比具有很强的竞争力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种在压缩域中利用动态残差积累加速动作识别的方法。
AI.motivation: 现有动作识别方法处理原始视频效率低且无法实时，而压缩视频中蕴含的运动矢量等信息未被充分挖掘。
AI.method: 通过轻量级部分解码提取压缩视频残差数据，设计动态相似残差积累机制减少处理帧数，并在压缩域直接应用神经网络进行分类。
AI.result: 在保持高分类准确率的同时，显著降低计算量和处理时间，性能优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效利用压缩视频固有信息，在保证精度的前提下实现高效动作识别，适用于实时视频分析场景。

ai_content_json.conclusion: 该方法有效利用压缩视频固有信息，在保证精度的前提下实现高效动作识别，适用于实时视频分析场景。

tldr: 本文提出一种在压缩域中利用动态残差积累加速动作识别的方法。
motivation: 现有动作识别方法处理原始视频效率低且无法实时，而压缩视频中蕴含的运动矢量等信息未被充分挖掘。
method: 通过轻量级部分解码提取压缩视频残差数据，设计动态相似残差积累机制减少处理帧数，并在压缩域直接应用神经网络进行分类。
result: 在保持高分类准确率的同时，显著降低计算量和处理时间，性能优于传统方法。
conclusion: 该方法有效利用压缩视频固有信息，在保证精度的前提下实现高效动作识别，适用于实时视频分析场景。
search_text: Speeding Up Action Recognition Using Dynamic Accumulation of Residuals in Compressed Domain 使用压缩域中残差的动态累积加速动作识别 With the widespread use of installed cameras, video-based monitoring approaches have seized considerable attention for different purposes like assisted living. Temporal redundancy and the sheer size of raw videos are the two most common problematic issues related to video processing algorithms. Most of the existing methods mainly focused on increasing accuracy by exploring consecutive frames, which is laborious and cannot be considered for real-time applications. Since videos are mostly stored and transmitted in compressed format, these kinds of videos are available on many devices. Compressed videos contain a multitude of beneficial information, such as motion vectors and quantized coefficients. Proper use of this available information can greatly improve the video understanding methods' performance. This paper presents an approach for using residual data, available in compressed videos directly, which can be obtained by a light partially decoding procedure. In addition, a method for accumulating similar residuals is proposed, which dramatically reduces the number of processed frames for action recognition. Applying neural networks exclusively for accumulated residuals in the compressed domain accelerates performance, while the classification results are highly competitive with raw video approaches. 随着安装摄像头的广泛使用，基于视频的监控方法在辅助生活等不同目的中引起了相当大的关注。时间冗余和原始视频的巨大尺寸是与视频处理算法相关的两个最常见的问题。现有的大多数方法主要集中在通过探索连续帧来提高准确性，这很费力并且不能考虑用于实时应用。由于视频大多以压缩格式存储和传输，因此此类视频可在许多设备上使用。压缩视频包含大量有用信息，例如运动矢量和量化系数。正确使用这些可用信息可以极大地提高视频理解方法的性能。本文提出了一种使用直接在压缩视频中可用的残差数据的方法，该残差数据可以通过轻量部分解码过程获得。此外，还提出了一种累积相似残差的方法，大大减少了动作识别的处理帧数。将神经网络专门应用于压缩域中的累积残差可以提高性能，而分类结果与原始视频方法相比具有很强的竞争力。 本文提出一种在压缩域中利用动态残差积累加速动作识别的方法。 Ali Abdari,Pouria Amirjan,Azadeh Mansouri cs.CV

title: Non-contrastive representation learning for intervals from well logs
title_zh: 从测井曲线中学习区间的非对比表示
comment: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters (2023)
comments: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters (2023)
abstract: The representation learning problem in the oil & gas industry aims to construct a model that provides a representation based on logging data for a well interval. Previous attempts are mainly supervised and focus on similarity task, which estimates closeness between intervals. We desire to build informative representations without using supervised (labelled) data. One of the possible approaches is self-supervised learning (SSL). In contrast to the supervised paradigm, this one requires little or no labels for the data. Nowadays, most SSL approaches are either contrastive or non-contrastive. Contrastive methods make representations of similar (positive) objects closer and distancing different (negative) ones. Due to possible wrong marking of positive and negative pairs, these methods can provide an inferior performance. Non-contrastive methods don't rely on such labelling and are widespread in computer vision. They learn using only pairs of similar objects that are easier to identify in logging data. We are the first to introduce non-contrastive SSL for well-logging data. In particular, we exploit Bootstrap Your Own Latent (BYOL) and Barlow Twins methods that avoid using negative pairs and focus only on matching positive pairs. The crucial part of these methods is an augmentation strategy. Our augmentation strategies and adaption of BYOL and Barlow Twins together allow us to achieve superior quality on clusterization and mostly the best performance on different classification tasks. Our results prove the usefulness of the proposed non-contrastive self-supervised approaches for representation learning and interval similarity in particular.
summary: The representation learning problem in the oil & gas industry aims to construct a model that provides a representation based on logging data for a well interval. Previous attempts are mainly supervised and focus on similarity task, which estimates closeness between intervals. We desire to build informative representations without using supervised (labelled) data. One of the possible approaches is self-supervised learning (SSL). In contrast to the supervised paradigm, this one requires little or no labels for the data. Nowadays, most SSL approaches are either contrastive or non-contrastive. Contrastive methods make representations of similar (positive) objects closer and distancing different (negative) ones. Due to possible wrong marking of positive and negative pairs, these methods can provide an inferior performance. Non-contrastive methods don't rely on such labelling and are widespread in computer vision. They learn using only pairs of similar objects that are easier to identify in logging data. We are the first to introduce non-contrastive SSL for well-logging data. In particular, we exploit Bootstrap Your Own Latent (BYOL) and Barlow Twins methods that avoid using negative pairs and focus only on matching positive pairs. The crucial part of these methods is an augmentation strategy. Our augmentation strategies and adaption of BYOL and Barlow Twins together allow us to achieve superior quality on clusterization and mostly the best performance on different classification tasks. Our results prove the usefulness of the proposed non-contrastive self-supervised approaches for representation learning and interval similarity in particular.
abstract_zh: 石油和天然气行业中的表示学习问题旨在构建一个模型，该模型提供基于井段测井数据的表示。之前的尝试主要是有监督的，并专注于相似性任务，该任务估计间隔之间的接近度。我们希望在不使用监督（标记）数据的情况下构建信息表示。一种可能的方法是自我监督学习（SSL）。与监督范式相比，这种范式需要很少或不需要数据标签。如今，大多数 SSL 方法要么是对比性的，要么是非对比性的。对比方法使相似（正）对象的表示更加接近，并远离不同（负）对象。由于可能错误标记正负对，这些方法可能提供较差的性能。非对比方法不依赖于此类标签，并且在计算机视觉中广泛使用。他们学习仅使用在日志数据中更容易识别的成对相似对象。我们是第一个为测井数据引入非对比 SSL 的公司。特别是，我们利用 Bootstrap Your Own Latent (BYOL) 和 Barlow Twins 方法来避免使用负对，而只关注匹配正对。这些方法的关键部分是增强策略。我们的增强策略以及 BYOL 和 Barlow Twins 的适应使我们能够在集群化方面实现卓越的质量，并且在不同的分类任务上获得最佳的性能。我们的结果证明了所提出的非对比自监督方法对于表示学习和区间相似性的有用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需监督数据的非对比自监督学习方法，用于从测井数据中学习井区间表示。
AI.motivation: 传统测井数据表示方法依赖监督学习和相似性任务，但标注数据获取困难且对比方法易受错误正负样本影响。
AI.method: 采用BYOL和Barlow Twins非对比自监督学习框架，通过设计特定数据增强策略，仅利用正样本对进行表示学习。
AI.result: 在聚类和分类任务中均取得优越性能，尤其在分类任务中表现最佳，验证了非对比方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 非对比自监督学习能有效提升测井数据表示质量，为无标注数据场景下的区间相似性分析提供新思路。

title: A Multiagent Framework for the Asynchronous and Collaborative Extension of Multitask ML Systems
title_zh: 用于多任务机器学习系统异步协作扩展的多代理框架
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2209.07326
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2209.07326
abstract: The traditional ML development methodology does not enable a large number of contributors, each with distinct objectives, to work collectively on the creation and extension of a shared intelligent system. Enabling such a collaborative methodology can accelerate the rate of innovation, increase ML technologies accessibility and enable the emergence of novel capabilities. We believe that this novel methodology for ML development can be demonstrated through a modularized representation of ML models and the definition of novel abstractions allowing to implement and execute diverse methods for the asynchronous use and extension of modular intelligent systems. We present a multiagent framework for the collaborative and asynchronous extension of dynamic large-scale multitask systems.
summary: The traditional ML development methodology does not enable a large number of contributors, each with distinct objectives, to work collectively on the creation and extension of a shared intelligent system. Enabling such a collaborative methodology can accelerate the rate of innovation, increase ML technologies accessibility and enable the emergence of novel capabilities. We believe that this novel methodology for ML development can be demonstrated through a modularized representation of ML models and the definition of novel abstractions allowing to implement and execute diverse methods for the asynchronous use and extension of modular intelligent systems. We present a multiagent framework for the collaborative and asynchronous extension of dynamic large-scale multitask systems.
abstract_zh: 传统的机器学习开发方法无法让大量的贡献者（每个人都有不同的目标）共同致力于创建和扩展共享智能系统。采用这种协作方法可以加快创新速度、提高机器学习技术的可及性并促进新功能的出现。我们相信，这种新颖的机器学习开发方法可以通过机器学习模型的模块化表示和新颖抽象的定义来证明，从而允许实现和执行异步使用和扩展模块化智能系统的各种方法。我们提出了一个多代理框架，用于动态大规模多任务系统的协作和异步扩展。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个多智能体框架，用于实现动态大规模多任务系统的异步协作扩展。
AI.motivation: 传统机器学习开发方法无法支持众多目标不同的贡献者协作构建共享智能系统，限制了创新速度和可及性。
AI.method: 通过模块化表示机器学习模型并定义新型抽象，实现异步使用和扩展模块化智能系统的多样化方法。
AI.result: 展示了支持异步协作扩展的多智能体框架，可处理动态大规模多任务系统。
AI.conclusion: 该方法论能加速机器学习技术创新，提升技术可及性并催生新型能力。

title: Dataset Complexity Assessment Based on Cumulative Maximum Scaled Area Under Laplacian Spectrum
title_zh: 基于拉普拉斯谱下累积最大尺度面积的数据集复杂度评估
comment: Published as a journal paper at Springer MTAP
comments: Published as a journal paper at Springer MTAP
abstract: Dataset complexity assessment aims to predict classification performance on a dataset with complexity calculation before training a classifier, which can also be used for classifier selection and dataset reduction. The training process of deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) is iterative and time-consuming because of hyperparameter uncertainty and the domain shift introduced by different datasets. Hence, it is meaningful to predict classification performance by assessing the complexity of datasets effectively before training DCNN models. This paper proposes a novel method called cumulative maximum scaled Area Under Laplacian Spectrum (cmsAULS), which can achieve state-of-the-art complexity assessment performance on six datasets.
summary: Dataset complexity assessment aims to predict classification performance on a dataset with complexity calculation before training a classifier, which can also be used for classifier selection and dataset reduction. The training process of deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) is iterative and time-consuming because of hyperparameter uncertainty and the domain shift introduced by different datasets. Hence, it is meaningful to predict classification performance by assessing the complexity of datasets effectively before training DCNN models. This paper proposes a novel method called cumulative maximum scaled Area Under Laplacian Spectrum (cmsAULS), which can achieve state-of-the-art complexity assessment performance on six datasets.
abstract_zh: 数据集复杂度评估旨在在训练分类器之前通过复杂度计算来预测数据集的分类性能，也可用于分类器选择和数据集缩减。由于超参数的不确定性和不同数据集引入的域转移，深度卷积神经网络（DCNN）的训练过程是迭代且耗时的。因此，在训练 DCNN 模型之前通过有效评估数据集的复杂性来预测分类性能是有意义的。本文提出了一种称为拉普拉斯谱下累积最大缩放面积（cmsAULS）的新方法，该方法可以在六个数据集上实现最先进的复杂性评估性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于累积最大缩放拉普拉斯谱曲线下面积（cmsAULS）的数据集复杂度评估方法，用于预测分类性能并优化深度卷积神经网络训练前的分类器选择与数据集约简。
AI.motivation: 深度卷积神经网络训练过程因超参数不确定性和领域转移问题导致耗时，需在训练前通过数据集复杂度评估预测分类性能以提升效率。
AI.method: 提出cmsAULS方法，通过计算拉普拉斯谱的累积最大缩放曲线下面积，量化数据集复杂度并实现分类性能预测。
AI.result: cmsAULS在六个数据集上达到最先进复杂度评估性能，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: cmsAULS能有效评估数据集复杂度，为分类器选择和数据集约简提供依据，有助于优化DCNN训练流程。

search_text: Dataset Complexity Assessment Based on Cumulative Maximum Scaled Area Under Laplacian Spectrum 基于拉普拉斯谱下累积最大尺度面积的数据集复杂度评估 Dataset complexity assessment aims to predict classification performance on a dataset with complexity calculation before training a classifier, which can also be used for classifier selection and dataset reduction. The training process of deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) is iterative and time-consuming because of hyperparameter uncertainty and the domain shift introduced by different datasets. Hence, it is meaningful to predict classification performance by assessing the complexity of datasets effectively before training DCNN models. This paper proposes a novel method called cumulative maximum scaled Area Under Laplacian Spectrum (cmsAULS), which can achieve state-of-the-art complexity assessment performance on six datasets. 数据集复杂度评估旨在在训练分类器之前通过复杂度计算来预测数据集的分类性能，也可用于分类器选择和数据集缩减。由于超参数的不确定性和不同数据集引入的域转移，深度卷积神经网络（DCNN）的训练过程是迭代且耗时的。因此，在训练 DCNN 模型之前通过有效评估数据集的复杂性来预测分类性能是有意义的。本文提出了一种称为拉普拉斯谱下累积最大缩放面积（cmsAULS）的新方法，该方法可以在六个数据集上实现最先进的复杂性评估性能。 本文提出一种基于累积最大缩放拉普拉斯谱曲线下面积（cmsAULS）的数据集复杂度评估方法，用于预测分类性能并优化深度卷积神经网络训练前的分类器选择与数据集约简。 Guang Li,Ren Togo,Takahiro Ogawa,Miki Haseyama cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

title: Hyper-Representations as Generative Models: Sampling Unseen Neural Network Weights
title_zh: 作为生成模型的超表示：对看不见的神经网络权重进行采样
comment: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022). arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2207.10951
comments: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022). arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2207.10951
abstract: Learning representations of neural network weights given a model zoo is an emerging and challenging area with many potential applications from model inspection, to neural architecture search or knowledge distillation. Recently, an autoencoder trained on a model zoo was able to learn a hyper-representation, which captures intrinsic and extrinsic properties of the models in the zoo. In this work, we extend hyper-representations for generative use to sample new model weights. We propose layer-wise loss normalization which we demonstrate is key to generate high-performing models and several sampling methods based on the topology of hyper-representations. The models generated using our methods are diverse, performant and capable to outperform strong baselines as evaluated on several downstream tasks: initialization, ensemble sampling and transfer learning. Our results indicate the potential of knowledge aggregation from model zoos to new models via hyper-representations thereby paving the avenue for novel research directions.
summary: Learning representations of neural network weights given a model zoo is an emerging and challenging area with many potential applications from model inspection, to neural architecture search or knowledge distillation. Recently, an autoencoder trained on a model zoo was able to learn a hyper-representation, which captures intrinsic and extrinsic properties of the models in the zoo. In this work, we extend hyper-representations for generative use to sample new model weights. We propose layer-wise loss normalization which we demonstrate is key to generate high-performing models and several sampling methods based on the topology of hyper-representations. The models generated using our methods are diverse, performant and capable to outperform strong baselines as evaluated on several downstream tasks: initialization, ensemble sampling and transfer learning. Our results indicate the potential of knowledge aggregation from model zoos to new models via hyper-representations thereby paving the avenue for novel research directions.
abstract_zh: 学习给定模型动物园的神经网络权重表示是一个新兴且具有挑战性的领域，具有从模型检查到神经架构搜索或知识蒸馏的许多潜在应用。最近，在模型动物园上训练的自动编码器能够学习超表示，它捕获动物园中模型的内在和外在属性。在这项工作中，我们扩展了超表示以用于生成新模型权重的采样。我们提出了逐层损失归一化，我们证明这是生成高性能模型和基于超表示拓扑的几种采样方法的关键。使用我们的方法生成的模型是多样化的、高性能的，并且在初始化、集成采样和迁移学习等几个下游任务的评估中能够超越强大的基线。我们的结果表明通过超表征从模型动物园到新模型的知识聚合的潜力，从而为新的研究方向铺平了道路。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过超表示（Hyper-Representations）生成新神经网络权重的方法，用于模型初始化、集成采样和迁移学习等任务。
AI.motivation: 模型动物园（Model Zoo）中的知识聚合具有广泛应用潜力，但如何生成高性能的新模型权重仍是挑战。
AI.method: 提出分层损失归一化方法，并基于超表示拓扑结构设计多种采样策略，从模型动物园中生成多样化权重。
AI.result: 生成模型在多个下游任务中表现优于强基线，展现出多样性与高性能。
AI.conclusion: 超表示可有效整合模型动物园知识，为新型模型生成与研究方向提供可行路径。

title: In Search of Projectively Equivariant Networks
title_zh: 寻找投影等变网络
comment: v3: Another significant rewrite. Accepted for publication in TMLR. v2: Significant rewrite. The title has been changed: "neural network" -> "network". More general description of projectively equivariant linear layers, with new proposed architectures, and a completely new accompanying experiment section, as a result
comments: v3: Another significant rewrite. Accepted for publication in TMLR. v2: Significant rewrite. The title has been changed: "neural network" -> "network". More general description of projectively equivariant linear layers, with new proposed architectures, and a completely new accompanying experiment section, as a result
abstract: Equivariance of linear neural network layers is well studied. In this work, we relax the equivariance condition to only be true in a projective sense. We propose a way to construct a projectively equivariant neural network through building a standard equivariant network where the linear group representations acting on each intermediate feature space are "multiplicatively modified lifts" of projective group representations. By theoretically studying the relation of projectively and linearly equivariant linear layers, we show that our approach is the most general possible when building a network out of linear layers. The theory is showcased in two simple experiments.
summary: Equivariance of linear neural network layers is well studied. In this work, we relax the equivariance condition to only be true in a projective sense. We propose a way to construct a projectively equivariant neural network through building a standard equivariant network where the linear group representations acting on each intermediate feature space are "multiplicatively modified lifts" of projective group representations. By theoretically studying the relation of projectively and linearly equivariant linear layers, we show that our approach is the most general possible when building a network out of linear layers. The theory is showcased in two simple experiments.
abstract_zh: 线性神经网络层的等方差性得到了很好的研究。在这项工作中，我们放宽了等方差条件，使其仅在投影意义上为真。我们提出了一种通过构建标准等变网络来构建投影等变神经网络的方法，其中作用于每个中间特征空间的线性群表示是投影群表示的“乘法修改提升”。通过从理论上研究投影和线性等变线性层的关系，我们表明，在用线性层构建网络时，我们的方法是最通用的方法。该理论通过两个简单的实验得到了展示。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种构建射影等变神经网络的方法，通过将线性群表示修改为射影群表示的乘法修正提升，以实现更通用的网络结构。
AI.motivation: 传统线性等变网络的严格条件限制了其适用性，而射影等变性在处理具有射影对称性的数据（如图像、3D结构）时更具灵活性。
AI.method: 通过构建标准等变网络，将中间特征空间的线性群表示替换为射影群表示的乘法修正提升，并理论分析其与线性等变性的关系。
AI.result: 理论证明该方法是构建线性层网络的最通用方案，并通过两个简单实验验证了理论的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 射影等变网络框架为处理具有射影对称性的任务提供了更通用的建模方式，具有理论和应用价值。

title: Low-Resolution Action Recognition for Tiny Actions Challenge
title_zh: 微小动作挑战的低分辨率动作识别
comment: This article is the report of the CVPR 2022 ActivityNet workshop Tiny Actions Challenge(https://tinyactions-cvpr22.github.io/). The time of the first submission to the organizers is June 6th
comments: This article is the report of the CVPR 2022 ActivityNet workshop Tiny Actions Challenge(https://tinyactions-cvpr22.github.io/). The time of the first submission to the organizers is June 6th
abstract: Tiny Actions Challenge focuses on understanding human activities in real-world surveillance. Basically, there are two main difficulties for activity recognition in this scenario. First, human activities are often recorded at a distance, and appear in a small resolution without much discriminative clue. Second, these activities are naturally distributed in a long-tailed way. It is hard to alleviate data bias for such heavy category imbalance. To tackle these problems, we propose a comprehensive recognition solution in this paper. First, we train video backbones with data balance, in order to alleviate overfitting in the challenge benchmark. Second, we design a dual-resolution distillation framework, which can effectively guide low-resolution action recognition by super-resolution knowledge. Finally, we apply model en-semble with post-processing, which can further boost per-formance on the long-tailed categories. Our solution ranks Top-1 on the leaderboard.
summary: Tiny Actions Challenge focuses on understanding human activities in real-world surveillance. Basically, there are two main difficulties for activity recognition in this scenario. First, human activities are often recorded at a distance, and appear in a small resolution without much discriminative clue. Second, these activities are naturally distributed in a long-tailed way. It is hard to alleviate data bias for such heavy category imbalance. To tackle these problems, we propose a comprehensive recognition solution in this paper. First, we train video backbones with data balance, in order to alleviate overfitting in the challenge benchmark. Second, we design a dual-resolution distillation framework, which can effectively guide low-resolution action recognition by super-resolution knowledge. Finally, we apply model en-semble with post-processing, which can further boost per-formance on the long-tailed categories. Our solution ranks Top-1 on the leaderboard.
abstract_zh: Tiny Actions Challenge 的重点是了解现实世界监控中的人类活动。基本上，这种情况下的活动识别有两个主要困难。首先，人类活动通常是在远处记录的，并且以较小的分辨率出现，没有太多区分线索。其次，这些活动自然地以长尾方式分布。如此严重的品类失衡，很难缓解数据偏差。为了解决这些问题，我们在本文中提出了一种全面的识别解决方案。首先，我们通过数据平衡训练视频主干，以减轻挑战基准中的过度拟合。其次，我们设计了双分辨率蒸馏框架，可以通过超分辨率知识有效指导低分辨率动作识别。最后，我们将模型集成与后处理一起应用，这可以进一步提高长尾类别的性能。我们的解决方案在排行榜上名列前茅。

AI.tldr: 本文针对监控场景下低分辨率和长尾分布的人类动作识别问题，提出综合解决方案并在Tiny Actions Challenge中取得第一名。
AI.motivation: 现实监控场景中动作识别面临分辨率低导致特征模糊、类别分布长尾导致数据偏差两大挑战，现有方法难以有效应对。
AI.method: 采用数据平衡训练视频主干网络、设计双分辨率蒸馏框架利用超分辨率知识引导低分辨率识别、结合模型集成与后处理优化长尾类别性能。
AI.result: 提出的解决方案在Tiny Actions Challenge排行榜中位列第一，有效解决了低分辨率与类别不平衡难题。
AI.conclusion: 通过多技术融合的解决方案，本文为低分辨率动作识别提供了新思路，验证了平衡训练、知识蒸馏和模型集成在长尾分布场景下的有效性。

search_text: Low-Resolution Action Recognition for Tiny Actions Challenge 微小动作挑战的低分辨率动作识别 Tiny Actions Challenge focuses on understanding human activities in real-world surveillance. Basically, there are two main difficulties for activity recognition in this scenario. First, human activities are often recorded at a distance, and appear in a small resolution without much discriminative clue. Second, these activities are naturally distributed in a long-tailed way. It is hard to alleviate data bias for such heavy category imbalance. To tackle these problems, we propose a comprehensive recognition solution in this paper. First, we train video backbones with data balance, in order to alleviate overfitting in the challenge benchmark. Second, we design a dual-resolution distillation framework, which can effectively guide low-resolution action recognition by super-resolution knowledge. Finally, we apply model en-semble with post-processing, which can further boost per-formance on the long-tailed categories. Our solution ranks Top-1 on the leaderboard. Tiny Actions Challenge 的重点是了解现实世界监控中的人类活动。基本上，这种情况下的活动识别有两个主要困难。首先，人类活动通常是在远处记录的，并且以较小的分辨率出现，没有太多区分线索。其次，这些活动自然地以长尾方式分布。如此严重的品类失衡，很难缓解数据偏差。为了解决这些问题，我们在本文中提出了一种全面的识别解决方案。首先，我们通过数据平衡训练视频主干，以减轻挑战基准中的过度拟合。其次，我们设计了双分辨率蒸馏框架，可以通过超分辨率知识有效指导低分辨率动作识别。最后，我们将模型集成与后处理一起应用，这可以进一步提高长尾类别的性能。我们的解决方案在排行榜上名列前茅。 本文针对监控场景下低分辨率和长尾分布的人类动作识别问题，提出综合解决方案并在Tiny Actions Challenge中取得第一名。 Boyu Chen,Yu Qiao,Yali Wang cs.CV

title: TruEyes: Utilizing Microtasks in Mobile Apps for Crowdsourced Labeling of Machine Learning Datasets
title_zh: TruEyes：利用移动应用程序中的微任务对机器学习数据集进行众包标记
abstract: The growing use of supervised machine learning in research and industry has increased the need for labeled datasets. Crowdsourcing has emerged as a popular method to create data labels. However, working on large batches of tasks leads to worker fatigue, negatively impacting labeling quality. To address this, we present TruEyes, a collaborative crowdsourcing system, enabling the distribution of micro-tasks to mobile app users. TruEyes allows machine learning practitioners to publish labeling tasks, mobile app developers to integrate task ads for monetization, and users to label data instead of watching advertisements. To evaluate the system, we conducted an experiment with N=296 participants. Our results show that the quality of the labeled data is comparable to traditional crowdsourcing approaches and most users prefer task ads over traditional ads. We discuss extensions to the system and address how mobile advertisement space can be used as a productive resource in the future.
summary: The growing use of supervised machine learning in research and industry has increased the need for labeled datasets. Crowdsourcing has emerged as a popular method to create data labels. However, working on large batches of tasks leads to worker fatigue, negatively impacting labeling quality. To address this, we present TruEyes, a collaborative crowdsourcing system, enabling the distribution of micro-tasks to mobile app users. TruEyes allows machine learning practitioners to publish labeling tasks, mobile app developers to integrate task ads for monetization, and users to label data instead of watching advertisements. To evaluate the system, we conducted an experiment with N=296 participants. Our results show that the quality of the labeled data is comparable to traditional crowdsourcing approaches and most users prefer task ads over traditional ads. We discuss extensions to the system and address how mobile advertisement space can be used as a productive resource in the future.
abstract_zh: 监督机器学习在研究和工业中的使用不断增加，增加了对标记数据集的需求。众包已成为创建数据标签的流行方法。然而，处理大批量任务会导致工人疲劳，从而对标签质量产生负面影响。为了解决这个问题，我们推出了 TruEyes，这是一个协作众包系统，可以将微任务分配给移动应用程序用户。 TruEyes 允许机器学习从业者发布标记任务，移动应用程序开发人员集成任务广告以实现货币化，以及用户标记数据而不是观看广告。为了评估该系统，我们对 N=296 名参与者进行了实验。我们的结果表明，标记数据的质量与传统的众包方法相当，并且大多数用户更喜欢任务广告而不是传统广告。我们讨论了系统的扩展，并讨论了如何将移动广告空间用作未来的生产资源。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TruEyes系统，通过在移动应用中利用微任务进行众包标注，以提高机器学习数据集的标注效率和质量。
AI.motivation: 传统众包标注方法因任务批量处理导致标注者疲劳，影响数据质量，需探索更高效且用户友好的标注方式。
AI.method: 设计TruEyes系统，允许机器学习从业者发布标注任务，移动应用开发者集成任务广告，用户通过完成微任务代替观看广告进行数据标注。
AI.result: 实验显示标注数据质量与传统众包方法相当，且多数用户更偏好任务广告而非传统广告。
AI.conclusion: TruEyes系统有效利用移动广告空间作为生产资源，为未来数据标注提供了可行的替代方案。

title: Fully Lattice Linear Algorithms
title_zh: 全格线性算法
abstract: This paper focuses on analyzing and differentiating between lattice linear problems and algorithms. It introduces a new class of algorithms called \textit{(fully) lattice linear algorithms}. A property of these algorithms is that they induce a partial order among all states and form \textit{multiple lattices}. An initial state locks in one of these lattices. We present a lattice linear self-stabilizing algorithm for minimal dominating set.
summary: This paper focuses on analyzing and differentiating between lattice linear problems and algorithms. It introduces a new class of algorithms called \textit{(fully) lattice linear algorithms}. A property of these algorithms is that they induce a partial order among all states and form \textit{multiple lattices}. An initial state locks in one of these lattices. We present a lattice linear self-stabilizing algorithm for minimal dominating set.
abstract_zh: 本文重点分析和区分格线性问题和算法。它引入了一类新的算法，称为 \textit{（完全）晶格线性算法}。这些算法的一个特性是它们在所有状态之间引入偏序并形成 \textit{多个格}。初始状态锁定在这些晶格之一中。我们提出了一种最小支配集的点阵线性自稳定算法。

AI.tldr: 本文主要分析并区分格线性问题与算法，提出全格线性算法，并设计用于最小支配集的自稳定算法。
AI.motivation: 传统算法在处理复杂问题时存在效率或结构限制，需探索更高效的格结构算法。
AI.method: 通过引入全格线性算法，利用状态间的偏序关系和多格结构，设计自稳定算法。
AI.result: 成功构建了全格线性自稳定算法，并应用于最小支配集问题，证明其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 全格线性算法为解决分布式系统问题提供了新思路，尤其在自稳定性和结构优化方面具有潜力。

title: Facial Landmark Predictions with Applications to Metaverse
title_zh: 面部标志预测及其在 Metaverse 中的应用
comment: mber:This paper appears in the Proceedings of 2022 IEEE 8th World Forum on Internet of Things (WF-IoT). Please feel free to contact us for questions or remarks
comments: mber:This paper appears in the Proceedings of 2022 IEEE 8th World Forum on Internet of Things (WF-IoT). Please feel free to contact us for questions or remarks
abstract: This research aims to make metaverse characters more realistic by adding lip animations learnt from videos in the wild. To achieve this, our approach is to extend Tacotron 2 text-to-speech synthesizer to generate lip movements together with mel spectrogram in one pass. The encoder and gate layer weights are pre-trained on LJ Speech 1.1 data set while the decoder is retrained on 93 clips of TED talk videos extracted from LRS 3 data set. Our novel decoder predicts displacement in 20 lip landmark positions across time, using labels automatically extracted by OpenFace 2.0 landmark predictor. Training converged in 7 hours using less than 5 minutes of video. We conducted ablation study for Pre/Post-Net and pre-trained encoder weights to demonstrate the effectiveness of transfer learning between audio and visual speech data.
summary: This research aims to make metaverse characters more realistic by adding lip animations learnt from videos in the wild. To achieve this, our approach is to extend Tacotron 2 text-to-speech synthesizer to generate lip movements together with mel spectrogram in one pass. The encoder and gate layer weights are pre-trained on LJ Speech 1.1 data set while the decoder is retrained on 93 clips of TED talk videos extracted from LRS 3 data set. Our novel decoder predicts displacement in 20 lip landmark positions across time, using labels automatically extracted by OpenFace 2.0 landmark predictor. Training converged in 7 hours using less than 5 minutes of video. We conducted ablation study for Pre/Post-Net and pre-trained encoder weights to demonstrate the effectiveness of transfer learning between audio and visual speech data.
abstract_zh: 这项研究旨在通过添加从野外视频中学到的嘴唇动画，使虚拟世界的角色更加真实。为了实现这一目标，我们的方法是扩展 Tacotron 2 文本到语音合成器，一次性生成嘴唇运动和梅尔频谱图。编码器和门层权重在 LJ Speech 1.1 数据集上进行预训练，而解码器则在从 LRS 3 数据集提取的 93 个 TED 演讲视频片段上进行重新训练。我们的新型解码器使用 OpenFace 2.0 地标预测器自动提取的标签来预测 20 个唇部地标位置随时间的位移。使用不到 5 分钟的视频，训练在 7 小时内完成。我们对 Pre/Post-Net 和预训练编码器权重进行了消融研究，以证明音频和视觉语音数据之间迁移学习的有效性。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出一种基于Tacotron 2的端到端方法，通过视频学习唇部动画来提升元宇宙角色的拟真度。
AI.motivation: 元宇宙虚拟角色需要更逼真的唇部动作与语音同步，传统方法难以实现自然的跨模态对齐。
AI.method: 扩展Tacotron 2模型同时生成梅尔频谱图和唇部关键点位移，利用LJ Speech预训练编码器，用LRS3数据集微调解码器，通过OpenFace提取20个唇部关键点标签进行时序预测。
AI.result: 在7小时内完成训练且仅需5分钟视频数据，消融实验验证了预训练权重和前后网络的有效性，实现了跨模态语音-视觉迁移学习。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过音频到视觉的迁移学习有效生成唇部动画，为元宇宙角色提供高效逼真的面部表情生成方案。

search_text: Facial Landmark Predictions with Applications to Metaverse 面部标志预测及其在 Metaverse 中的应用 This research aims to make metaverse characters more realistic by adding lip animations learnt from videos in the wild. To achieve this, our approach is to extend Tacotron 2 text-to-speech synthesizer to generate lip movements together with mel spectrogram in one pass. The encoder and gate layer weights are pre-trained on LJ Speech 1.1 data set while the decoder is retrained on 93 clips of TED talk videos extracted from LRS 3 data set. Our novel decoder predicts displacement in 20 lip landmark positions across time, using labels automatically extracted by OpenFace 2.0 landmark predictor. Training converged in 7 hours using less than 5 minutes of video. We conducted ablation study for Pre/Post-Net and pre-trained encoder weights to demonstrate the effectiveness of transfer learning between audio and visual speech data. 这项研究旨在通过添加从野外视频中学到的嘴唇动画，使虚拟世界的角色更加真实。为了实现这一目标，我们的方法是扩展 Tacotron 2 文本到语音合成器，一次性生成嘴唇运动和梅尔频谱图。编码器和门层权重在 LJ Speech 1.1 数据集上进行预训练，而解码器则在从 LRS 3 数据集提取的 93 个 TED 演讲视频片段上进行重新训练。我们的新型解码器使用 OpenFace 2.0 地标预测器自动提取的标签来预测 20 个唇部地标位置随时间的位移。使用不到 5 分钟的视频，训练在 7 小时内完成。我们对 Pre/Post-Net 和预训练编码器权重进行了消融研究，以证明音频和视觉语音数据之间迁移学习的有效性。 该论文提出一种基于Tacotron 2的端到端方法，通过视频学习唇部动画来提升元宇宙角色的拟真度。 Qiao Han,Jun Zhao,Kwok-Yan Lam cs.CV,cs.LG,cs.NI,cs.SI

title: Creative Painting with Latent Diffusion Models
title_zh: 使用潜在扩散模型进行创意绘画
abstract: Artistic painting has achieved significant progress during recent years. Using an autoencoder to connect the original images with compressed latent spaces and a cross attention enhanced U-Net as the backbone of diffusion, latent diffusion models (LDMs) have achieved stable and high fertility image generation. In this paper, we focus on enhancing the creative painting ability of current LDMs in two directions, textual condition extension and model retraining with Wikiart dataset. Through textual condition extension, users' input prompts are expanded with rich contextual knowledge for deeper understanding and explaining the prompts. Wikiart dataset contains 80K famous artworks drawn during recent 400 years by more than 1,000 famous artists in rich styles and genres. Through the retraining, we are able to ask these artists to draw novel and creative painting on modern topics. Direct comparisons with the original model show that the creativity and artistry are enriched.
summary: Artistic painting has achieved significant progress during recent years. Using an autoencoder to connect the original images with compressed latent spaces and a cross attention enhanced U-Net as the backbone of diffusion, latent diffusion models (LDMs) have achieved stable and high fertility image generation. In this paper, we focus on enhancing the creative painting ability of current LDMs in two directions, textual condition extension and model retraining with Wikiart dataset. Through textual condition extension, users' input prompts are expanded with rich contextual knowledge for deeper understanding and explaining the prompts. Wikiart dataset contains 80K famous artworks drawn during recent 400 years by more than 1,000 famous artists in rich styles and genres. Through the retraining, we are able to ask these artists to draw novel and creative painting on modern topics. Direct comparisons with the original model show that the creativity and artistry are enriched.
abstract_zh: 近年来，艺术绘画取得了长足的进步。使用自动编码器将原始图像与压缩的潜在空间连接起来，并使用交叉注意增强的 U-Net 作为扩散的骨干，潜在扩散模型（LDM）实现了稳定且高生育力的图像生成。在本文中，我们重点从文本条件扩展和使用 Wikiart 数据集进行模型再训练两个方向来增强当前 LDM 的创意绘画能力。通过文本条件扩展，用丰富的上下文知识扩展用户的输入提示，以便更深入地理解和解释提示。 Wikiart 数据集包含近 400 年来由 1000 多位著名艺术家创作的 80K 幅著名艺术作品，风格和流派丰富。通过再培训，我们可以要求这些艺术家针对现代题材画出新颖、富有创意的绘画作品。与原模型的直接对比可见创意性和艺术性的丰富。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过扩展文本条件和使用Wikiart数据集重新训练，提升潜在扩散模型（LDMs）的创意绘画能力。
AI.motivation: 尽管潜在扩散模型在图像生成方面表现出色，但其在创意绘画领域的表现仍有提升空间，需增强艺术创造力和风格多样性。
AI.method: 通过扩展用户输入提示的文本条件以丰富上下文理解，并基于包含8万件艺术作品的Wikiart数据集对模型进行重新训练。
AI.result: 重新训练后的模型能够根据现代主题生成新颖且具有艺术性的绘画作品，与原模型相比创意和艺术性显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 结合文本条件扩展与艺术数据集重新训练的方法，有效增强了潜在扩散模型在创意绘画任务中的表现。
