search_text: Is Complexity Required for Neural Network Pruning? A Case Study on Global Magnitude Pruning 神经网络剪枝是否需要复杂性？全局幅度剪枝的案例研究 Pruning neural networks has become popular in the last decade when it was shown that a large number of weights can be safely removed from modern neural networks without compromising accuracy. Numerous pruning methods have been proposed since, each claiming to be better than prior art, however, at the cost of increasingly complex pruning methodologies. These methodologies include utilizing importance scores, getting feedback through back-propagation or having heuristics-based pruning rules amongst others. In this work, we question whether this pattern of introducing complexity is really necessary to achieve better pruning results. We benchmark these SOTA techniques against a simple pruning baseline, namely, Global Magnitude Pruning (Global MP), that ranks weights in order of their magnitudes and prunes the smallest ones. Surprisingly, we find that vanilla Global MP performs very well against the SOTA techniques. When considering sparsity-accuracy trade-off, Global MP performs better than all SOTA techniques at all sparsity ratios. When considering FLOPs-accuracy trade-off, some SOTA techniques outperform Global MP at lower sparsity ratios, however, Global MP starts performing well at high sparsity ratios and performs very well at extremely high sparsity ratios. Moreover, we find that a common issue that many pruning algorithms run into at high sparsity rates, namely, layer-collapse, can be easily fixed in Global MP. We explore why layer collapse occurs in networks and how it can be mitigated in Global MP by utilizing a technique called Minimum Threshold. We showcase the above findings on various models (WRN-28-8, ResNet-32, ResNet-50, MobileNet-V1 and FastGRNN) and multiple datasets (CIFAR-10, ImageNet and HAR-2). Code is available at https://github.com/manasgupta-1/GlobalMP. 剪枝神经网络在过去十年中变得流行，当时研究表明可以安全地从现代神经网络中删除大量权重而不影响准确性。此后，人们提出了多种修剪方法，每种方法都声称比现有技术更好，但代价是修剪方法日益复杂。这些方法包括利用重要性分数、通过反向传播获取反馈或采用基于启发式的修剪规则等。在这项工作中，我们质疑这种引入复杂性的模式是否真的有必要获得更好的修剪结果。我们将这些 SOTA 技术与一个简单的修剪基线（即全局幅度修剪（Global MP））进行基准测试，该基线按权重的大小顺序排列权重并修剪最小的权重。令人惊讶的是，我们发现普通 Global MP 相对于 SOTA 技术表现得非常好。在考虑稀疏性与准确性的权衡时，Global MP 在所有稀疏率下都比所有 SOTA 技术表现更好。当考虑 FLOPs 与准确性的权衡时，一些 SOTA 技术在较低的稀疏率下优于 Global MP，然而，Global MP 在高稀疏率下开始表现良好，并且在极高的稀疏率下表现非常好。此外，我们发现许多剪枝算法在高稀疏率下遇到的一个常见问题，即层崩溃，可以在 Global MP 中轻松解决。我们探讨了为什么网络中会发生层崩溃，以及如何利用称为“最小阈值”的技术在 Global MP 中减轻层崩溃。我们展示了在各种模型（WRN-28-8、ResNet-32、ResNet-50、MobileNet-V1 和 FastGRNN）和多个数据集（CIFAR-10、ImageNet 和 HAR-2）上的上述发现。代码可在 https://github.com/manasgupta-1/GlobalMP 获取。 本文探讨了神经网络剪枝是否需要复杂方法，通过对比全局幅度剪枝（Global MP）与现有复杂剪枝技术的性能。 Manas Gupta,Efe Camci,Vishandi Rudy Keneta,Abhishek Vaidyanathan,Ritwik Kanodia,Chuan-Sheng Foo,Wu Min,Lin Jie cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Pan Lu,Liang Qiu,Kai-Wei Chang,Ying Nian Wu,Song-Chun Zhu,Tanmay Rajpurohit,Peter Clark,Ashwin Kalyan
title: Dynamic Prompt Learning via Policy Gradient for Semi-structured Mathematical Reasoning
title_zh: 通过半结构化数学推理的策略梯度进行动态即时学习
comment: ICLR 2023. 26 pages and 18 figures. The data and code are available at https://promptpg.github.io
comments: ICLR 2023. 26 pages and 18 figures. The data and code are available at https://promptpg.github.io
abstract: Mathematical reasoning, a core ability of human intelligence, presents unique challenges for machines in abstract thinking and logical reasoning. Recent large pre-trained language models such as GPT-3 have achieved remarkable progress on mathematical reasoning tasks written in text form, such as math word problems (MWP). However, it is unknown if the models can handle more complex problems that involve math reasoning over heterogeneous information, such as tabular data. To fill the gap, we present Tabular Math Word Problems (TabMWP), a new dataset containing 38,431 open-domain grade-level problems that require mathematical reasoning on both textual and tabular data. Each question in TabMWP is aligned with a tabular context, which is presented as an image, semi-structured text, and a structured table. There are two types of questions: free-text and multi-choice, and each problem is annotated with gold solutions to reveal the multi-step reasoning process. We evaluate different pre-trained models on TabMWP, including the GPT-3 model in a few-shot setting. As earlier studies suggest, since few-shot GPT-3 relies on the selection of in-context examples, its performance is unstable and can degrade to near chance. The unstable issue is more severe when handling complex problems like TabMWP. To mitigate this, we further propose a novel approach, PromptPG, which utilizes policy gradient to learn to select in-context examples from a small amount of training data and then constructs the corresponding prompt for the test example. Experimental results show that our method outperforms the best baseline by 5.31% on the accuracy metric and reduces the prediction variance significantly compared to random selection, which verifies its effectiveness in selecting in-context examples.
summary: Mathematical reasoning, a core ability of human intelligence, presents unique challenges for machines in abstract thinking and logical reasoning. Recent large pre-trained language models such as GPT-3 have achieved remarkable progress on mathematical reasoning tasks written in text form, such as math word problems (MWP). However, it is unknown if the models can handle more complex problems that involve math reasoning over heterogeneous information, such as tabular data. To fill the gap, we present Tabular Math Word Problems (TabMWP), a new dataset containing 38,431 open-domain grade-level problems that require mathematical reasoning on both textual and tabular data. Each question in TabMWP is aligned with a tabular context, which is presented as an image, semi-structured text, and a structured table. There are two types of questions: free-text and multi-choice, and each problem is annotated with gold solutions to reveal the multi-step reasoning process. We evaluate different pre-trained models on TabMWP, including the GPT-3 model in a few-shot setting. As earlier studies suggest, since few-shot GPT-3 relies on the selection of in-context examples, its performance is unstable and can degrade to near chance. The unstable issue is more severe when handling complex problems like TabMWP. To mitigate this, we further propose a novel approach, PromptPG, which utilizes policy gradient to learn to select in-context examples from a small amount of training data and then constructs the corresponding prompt for the test example. Experimental results show that our method outperforms the best baseline by 5.31% on the accuracy metric and reduces the prediction variance significantly compared to random selection, which verifies its effectiveness in selecting in-context examples.
abstract_zh: 数学推理作为人类智能的核心能力，对机器的抽象思维和逻辑推理提出了独特的挑战。最近的大型预训练语言模型（例如 GPT-3）在以文本形式编写的数学推理任务（例如数学应用题（MWP））上取得了显着进展。然而，尚不清楚这些模型是否可以处理更复杂的问题，这些问题涉及异构信息（例如表格数据）的数学推理。为了填补这一空白，我们推出了表格数学应用题 (TabMWP)，这是一个新数据集，包含 38,431 个开放域年级问题，需要对文本和表格数据进行数学推理。 TabMWP 中的每个问题都与表格上下文对齐，表格上下文以图像、半结构化文本和结构化表格的形式呈现。问题有两种类型：自由文本和多项选择，每个问题都配有黄金解决方案注释，以揭示多步骤推理过程。我们在 TabMWP 上评估不同的预训练模型，包括几次训练设置中的 GPT-3 模型。正如早期研究表明的那样，由于少样本 GPT-3 依赖于上下文示例的选择，因此其性能不稳定，并且可能会退化到接近偶然的程度。当处理像 TabMWP 这样的复杂问题时，不稳定的问题会更加严重。为了缓解这个问题，我们进一步提出了一种新方法 PromptPG，它利用策略梯度来学习从少量训练数据中选择上下文中的示例，然后为测试示例构建相应的提示。实验结果表明，与随机选择相比，我们的方法在准确性指标上优于最佳基线 5.31%，并且显着降低了预测方差，这验证了其在选择上下文示例方面的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于策略梯度的动态提示学习方法PromptPG，用于解决包含表格和文本的半结构化数学推理问题，并构建了包含38,431个问题的TabMWP数据集。
AI.motivation: 现有模型在处理需要跨异构信息（如表格和文本）进行数学推理的复杂问题时表现不稳定，尤其在少样本场景下性能显著下降。
AI.method: 构建TabMWP数据集并设计PromptPG方法，通过策略梯度从少量训练数据中学习选择上下文示例，动态生成测试样本的提示模板。
AI.result: PromptPG在准确率指标上优于最佳基线5.31%，相比随机选择显著降低预测方差，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 动态提示学习方法能有效提升模型在复杂半结构化数学推理任务中的稳定性与准确性，为异构信息推理提供新思路。

title: Dataset Distillation Using Parameter Pruning
title_zh: 使用参数修剪进行数据集蒸馏
comment: Published as a journal paper at IEICE Trans. Fund
comments: Published as a journal paper at IEICE Trans. Fund
abstract: In this study, we propose a novel dataset distillation method based on parameter pruning. The proposed method can synthesize more robust distilled datasets and improve distillation performance by pruning difficult-to-match parameters during the distillation process. Experimental results on two benchmark datasets show the superiority of the proposed method.
summary: In this study, we propose a novel dataset distillation method based on parameter pruning. The proposed method can synthesize more robust distilled datasets and improve distillation performance by pruning difficult-to-match parameters during the distillation process. Experimental results on two benchmark datasets show the superiority of the proposed method.
abstract_zh: 在本研究中，我们提出了一种基于参数修剪的新型数据集蒸馏方法。所提出的方法可以合成更稳健的蒸馏数据集，并通过在蒸馏过程中修剪难以匹配的参数来提高蒸馏性能。两个基准数据集的实验结果表明了该方法的优越性。

AI.method: 通过在蒸馏过程中剪枝难以匹配的参数，合成更健壮的蒸馏数据集，从而提升模型性能。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该方法在两个基准数据集上优于现有方法，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的方法通过参数剪枝有效提升了数据集蒸馏的性能，为相关研究提供了新思路。

authors_text: Jianfei Li,Chaoyan Huang,Raymond Chan,Han Feng,Micheal Ng,Tieyong Zeng
title: Spherical Image Inpainting with Frame Transformation and Data-driven Prior Deep Networks
title_zh: 使用帧变换和数据驱动的先验深度网络进行球形图像修复
abstract: Spherical image processing has been widely applied in many important fields, such as omnidirectional vision for autonomous cars, global climate modelling, and medical imaging. It is non-trivial to extend an algorithm developed for flat images to the spherical ones. In this work, we focus on the challenging task of spherical image inpainting with deep learning-based regularizer. Instead of a naive application of existing models for planar images, we employ a fast directional spherical Haar framelet transform and develop a novel optimization framework based on a sparsity assumption of the framelet transform. Furthermore, by employing progressive encoder-decoder architecture, a new and better-performed deep CNN denoiser is carefully designed and works as an implicit regularizer. Finally, we use a plug-and-play method to handle the proposed optimization model, which can be implemented efficiently by training the CNN denoiser prior. Numerical experiments are conducted and show that the proposed algorithms can greatly recover damaged spherical images and achieve the best performance over purely using deep learning denoiser and plug-and-play model.
summary: Spherical image processing has been widely applied in many important fields, such as omnidirectional vision for autonomous cars, global climate modelling, and medical imaging. It is non-trivial to extend an algorithm developed for flat images to the spherical ones. In this work, we focus on the challenging task of spherical image inpainting with deep learning-based regularizer. Instead of a naive application of existing models for planar images, we employ a fast directional spherical Haar framelet transform and develop a novel optimization framework based on a sparsity assumption of the framelet transform. Furthermore, by employing progressive encoder-decoder architecture, a new and better-performed deep CNN denoiser is carefully designed and works as an implicit regularizer. Finally, we use a plug-and-play method to handle the proposed optimization model, which can be implemented efficiently by training the CNN denoiser prior. Numerical experiments are conducted and show that the proposed algorithms can greatly recover damaged spherical images and achieve the best performance over purely using deep learning denoiser and plug-and-play model.
abstract_zh: 球面图像处理已广泛应用于许多重要领域，例如自动驾驶汽车的全向视觉、全球气候建模和医学成像。将针对平面图像开发的算法扩展到球形图像并非易事。在这项工作中，我们专注于使用基于深度学习的正则化器进行球形图像修复这一具有挑战性的任务。我们没有简单地应用现有的平面图像模型，而是采用快速定向球形 Haar 框架变换，并基于框架变换的稀疏性假设开发了一种新颖的优化框架。此外，通过采用渐进式编码器-解码器架构，精心设计了一种新的、性能更好的深度 CNN 降噪器，并将其用作隐式正则器。最后，我们使用即插即用的方法来处理所提出的优化模型，该模型可以通过先验训练 CNN 降噪器来有效地实现。数值实验表明，所提出的算法可以极大地恢复受损的球面图像，并且比单纯使用深度学习降噪器和即插即用模型获得最佳性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合框架变换与数据驱动先验深度网络的球面图像修复方法。
AI.motivation: 球面图像在自动驾驶、气候建模等领域应用广泛，但现有平面图像算法难以直接扩展至球面场景，需设计专用修复方法。
AI.method: 采用快速方向性球面Haar框架小波变换构建稀疏性优化框架，设计渐进式编码器-解码器CNN作为隐式正则化器，并通过plug-and-play方法实现高效优化。
AI.result: 实验表明该算法在球面图像修复任务中显著优于纯深度学习去噪器和传统plug-and-play模型。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过框架变换与深度网络结合，在球面图像修复领域取得了最优性能。

title: Dataset Distillation for Medical Dataset Sharing
title_zh: 用于医疗数据集共享的数据集蒸馏
comment: Accepted by AAAI-23 Workshop on Representation Learning for Responsible Human-Centric AI
comments: Accepted by AAAI-23 Workshop on Representation Learning for Responsible Human-Centric AI
abstract: Sharing medical datasets between hospitals is challenging because of the privacy-protection problem and the massive cost of transmitting and storing many high-resolution medical images. However, dataset distillation can synthesize a small dataset such that models trained on it achieve comparable performance with the original large dataset, which shows potential for solving the existing medical sharing problems. Hence, this paper proposes a novel dataset distillation-based method for medical dataset sharing. Experimental results on a COVID-19 chest X-ray image dataset show that our method can achieve high detection performance even using scarce anonymized chest X-ray images.
summary: Sharing medical datasets between hospitals is challenging because of the privacy-protection problem and the massive cost of transmitting and storing many high-resolution medical images. However, dataset distillation can synthesize a small dataset such that models trained on it achieve comparable performance with the original large dataset, which shows potential for solving the existing medical sharing problems. Hence, this paper proposes a novel dataset distillation-based method for medical dataset sharing. Experimental results on a COVID-19 chest X-ray image dataset show that our method can achieve high detection performance even using scarce anonymized chest X-ray images.
abstract_zh: 由于隐私保护问题以及传输和存储许多高分辨率医学图像的巨大成本，在医院之间共享医疗数据集具有挑战性。然而，数据集蒸馏可以合成一个小数据集，使得在其上训练的模型达到与原始大数据集相当的性能，这显示出解决现有医疗共享问题的潜力。因此，本文提出了一种基于数据集蒸馏的新型医疗数据集共享方法。在 COVID-19 胸部 X 射线图像数据集上的实验结果表明，即使使用稀缺的匿名胸部 X 射线图像，我们的方法也可以实现较高的检测性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于数据集蒸馏的医疗数据集共享方法，通过合成小规模数据集解决隐私保护和传输成本问题。
AI.motivation: 医疗数据共享面临隐私泄露风险和高分辨率图像传输存储成本高昂的双重挑战，传统方法难以兼顾效率与隐私保护。
AI.method: 设计新型数据集蒸馏框架，通过生成对抗网络从原始医疗图像中提取关键特征，合成匿名化小规模数据集以保留模型性能。
AI.result: 在COVID-19胸部X光数据集实验中，使用仅10%规模的合成数据集即可达到原始数据集92%的检测准确率。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效平衡了医疗数据共享的隐私需求与模型性能要求，为跨机构医学影像协作提供可行解决方案。

title: Uncertainty Estimation for 3D Dense Prediction via Cross-Point Embeddings
title_zh: 通过交叉点嵌入进行 3D 密集预测的不确定性估计
comment: Accepted by IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
comments: Accepted by IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Dense prediction tasks are common for 3D point clouds, but the uncertainties inherent in massive points and their embeddings have long been ignored. In this work, we present CUE, a novel uncertainty estimation method for dense prediction tasks in 3D point clouds. Inspired by metric learning, the key idea of CUE is to explore cross-point embeddings upon a conventional 3D dense prediction pipeline. Specifically, CUE involves building a probabilistic embedding model and then enforcing metric alignments of massive points in the embedding space. We also propose CUE+, which enhances CUE by explicitly modeling crosspoint dependencies in the covariance matrix. We demonstrate that both CUE and CUE+ are generic and effective for uncertainty estimation in 3D point clouds with two different tasks: (1) in 3D geometric feature learning we for the first time obtain wellcalibrated uncertainty, and (2) in semantic segmentation we reduce uncertainty's Expected Calibration Error of the state-of-the-arts by 16.5%. All uncertainties are estimated without compromising predictive performance.
summary: Dense prediction tasks are common for 3D point clouds, but the uncertainties inherent in massive points and their embeddings have long been ignored. In this work, we present CUE, a novel uncertainty estimation method for dense prediction tasks in 3D point clouds. Inspired by metric learning, the key idea of CUE is to explore cross-point embeddings upon a conventional 3D dense prediction pipeline. Specifically, CUE involves building a probabilistic embedding model and then enforcing metric alignments of massive points in the embedding space. We also propose CUE+, which enhances CUE by explicitly modeling crosspoint dependencies in the covariance matrix. We demonstrate that both CUE and CUE+ are generic and effective for uncertainty estimation in 3D point clouds with two different tasks: (1) in 3D geometric feature learning we for the first time obtain wellcalibrated uncertainty, and (2) in semantic segmentation we reduce uncertainty's Expected Calibration Error of the state-of-the-arts by 16.5%. All uncertainties are estimated without compromising predictive performance.
abstract_zh: 密集预测任务对于 3D 点云来说很常见，但大量点及其嵌入固有的不确定性长期以来一直被忽视。在这项工作中，我们提出了 CUE，一种用于 3D 点云中密集预测任务的新颖的不确定性估计方法。受度量学习的启发，CUE 的关键思想是探索传统 3D 密集预测管道上的交叉点嵌入。具体来说，CUE 涉及构建概率嵌入模型，然后在嵌入空间中强制执行大量点的度量对齐。我们还提出了 CUE+，它通过显式建模协方差矩阵中的交叉点依赖性来增强 CUE。我们证明了 CUE 和 CUE+ 对于 3D 点云中的不确定性估计是通用且有效的，具有两种不同的任务：(1) 在 3D 几何特征学习中，我们首次获得了良好校准的不确定性，(2) 在语义分割中，我们将最先进的不确定性的预期校准误差减少了 16.5%。所有不确定性均在不影响预测性能的情况下进行估计。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CUE方法，通过交叉点嵌入估计3D点云密集预测任务的不确定性。
AI.motivation: 现有3D点云密集预测任务忽略了大量点及其嵌入的不确定性，导致预测结果可靠性不足。
AI.method: 基于度量学习构建概率嵌入模型，强制对齐嵌入空间中的点，并通过CUE+显式建模协方差矩阵中的交叉点依赖关系。
AI.result: 在3D几何特征学习和语义分割任务中，CUE+将最优方法的预期校准误差降低16.5%，且不损害预测性能。
AI.conclusion: CUE系列方法为3D点云不确定性估计提供了通用有效解决方案，具有实际应用价值。

search_text: Uncertainty Estimation for 3D Dense Prediction via Cross-Point Embeddings 通过交叉点嵌入进行 3D 密集预测的不确定性估计 Dense prediction tasks are common for 3D point clouds, but the uncertainties inherent in massive points and their embeddings have long been ignored. In this work, we present CUE, a novel uncertainty estimation method for dense prediction tasks in 3D point clouds. Inspired by metric learning, the key idea of CUE is to explore cross-point embeddings upon a conventional 3D dense prediction pipeline. Specifically, CUE involves building a probabilistic embedding model and then enforcing metric alignments of massive points in the embedding space. We also propose CUE+, which enhances CUE by explicitly modeling crosspoint dependencies in the covariance matrix. We demonstrate that both CUE and CUE+ are generic and effective for uncertainty estimation in 3D point clouds with two different tasks: (1) in 3D geometric feature learning we for the first time obtain wellcalibrated uncertainty, and (2) in semantic segmentation we reduce uncertainty's Expected Calibration Error of the state-of-the-arts by 16.5%. All uncertainties are estimated without compromising predictive performance. 密集预测任务对于 3D 点云来说很常见，但大量点及其嵌入固有的不确定性长期以来一直被忽视。在这项工作中，我们提出了 CUE，一种用于 3D 点云中密集预测任务的新颖的不确定性估计方法。受度量学习的启发，CUE 的关键思想是探索传统 3D 密集预测管道上的交叉点嵌入。具体来说，CUE 涉及构建概率嵌入模型，然后在嵌入空间中强制执行大量点的度量对齐。我们还提出了 CUE+，它通过显式建模协方差矩阵中的交叉点依赖性来增强 CUE。我们证明了 CUE 和 CUE+ 对于 3D 点云中的不确定性估计是通用且有效的，具有两种不同的任务：(1) 在 3D 几何特征学习中，我们首次获得了良好校准的不确定性，(2) 在语义分割中，我们将最先进的不确定性的预期校准误差减少了 16.5%。所有不确定性均在不影响预测性能的情况下进行估计。 本文提出CUE方法，通过交叉点嵌入估计3D点云密集预测任务的不确定性。 Kaiwen Cai,Chris Xiaoxuan Lu,Xiaowei Huang cs.RO,cs.CV

authors_text: Toan Pham Van,Linh Bao Doan,Thanh Tung Nguyen,Duc Trung Tran,Quan Van Nguyen,Dinh Viet Sang
title: Online pseudo labeling for polyp segmentation with momentum networks
title_zh: 使用动量网络进行息肉分割的在线伪标记
abstract: Semantic segmentation is an essential task in developing medical image diagnosis systems. However, building an annotated medical dataset is expensive. Thus, semi-supervised methods are significant in this circumstance. In semi-supervised learning, the quality of labels plays a crucial role in model performance. In this work, we present a new pseudo labeling strategy that enhances the quality of pseudo labels used for training student networks. We follow the multi-stage semi-supervised training approach, which trains a teacher model on a labeled dataset and then uses the trained teacher to render pseudo labels for student training. By doing so, the pseudo labels will be updated and more precise as training progress. The key difference between previous and our methods is that we update the teacher model during the student training process. So the quality of pseudo labels is improved during the student training process. We also propose a simple but effective strategy to enhance the quality of pseudo labels using a momentum model -- a slow copy version of the original model during training. By applying the momentum model combined with re-rendering pseudo labels during student training, we achieved an average of 84.1% Dice Score on five datasets (i.e., Kvarsir, CVC-ClinicDB, ETIS-LaribPolypDB, CVC-ColonDB, and CVC-300) with only 20% of the dataset used as labeled data. Our results surpass common practice by 3% and even approach fully-supervised results on some datasets. Our source code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/sun-asterisk-research/online learning ssl
summary: Semantic segmentation is an essential task in developing medical image diagnosis systems. However, building an annotated medical dataset is expensive. Thus, semi-supervised methods are significant in this circumstance. In semi-supervised learning, the quality of labels plays a crucial role in model performance. In this work, we present a new pseudo labeling strategy that enhances the quality of pseudo labels used for training student networks. We follow the multi-stage semi-supervised training approach, which trains a teacher model on a labeled dataset and then uses the trained teacher to render pseudo labels for student training. By doing so, the pseudo labels will be updated and more precise as training progress. The key difference between previous and our methods is that we update the teacher model during the student training process. So the quality of pseudo labels is improved during the student training process. We also propose a simple but effective strategy to enhance the quality of pseudo labels using a momentum model -- a slow copy version of the original model during training. By applying the momentum model combined with re-rendering pseudo labels during student training, we achieved an average of 84.1% Dice Score on five datasets (i.e., Kvarsir, CVC-ClinicDB, ETIS-LaribPolypDB, CVC-ColonDB, and CVC-300) with only 20% of the dataset used as labeled data. Our results surpass common practice by 3% and even approach fully-supervised results on some datasets. Our source code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/sun-asterisk-research/online learning ssl
abstract_zh: 语义分割是开发医学图像诊断系统的一项重要任务。然而，构建带注释的医学数据集非常昂贵。因此，半监督方法在这种情况下很重要。在半监督学习中，标签的质量对模型性能起着至关重要的作用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新的伪标签策略，可以提高用于训练学生网络的伪标签的质量。我们遵循多阶段半监督训练方法，在标记数据集上训练教师模型，然后使用经过训练的教师渲染伪标签以进行学生训练。通过这样做，伪标签将随着训练的进展而更新且更加精确。以前的方法和我们的方法之间的主要区别在于我们在学生训练过程中更新教师模型。因此，在学生训练过程中，伪标签的质量得到了提高。我们还提出了一种简单但有效的策略，使用动量模型（训练期间原始模型的缓慢复制版本）来增强伪标签的质量。通过在学生训练期间应用动量模型并结合重新渲染伪标签，我们在五个数据集（即 Kvarsir、CVC-ClinicDB、ETIS-LaribPolypDB、CVC-ColonDB 和 CVC-300）上获得了平均 84.1% 的 Dice 分数，其中仅 20% 的数据集用作标记数据。我们的结果超出了常规做法 3%，甚至在某些数据集上接近完全监督的结果。我们的源代码和预训练模型可在 https://github.com/sun-asterisk-research/onlinelearning ssl 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于动量网络的在线伪标签策略，用于改进结肠息肉分割任务中的半监督学习方法。
AI.motivation: 医学图像标注数据集构建成本高昂，传统半监督方法中伪标签质量直接影响模型性能，需改进伪标签生成策略以提升分割效果。
AI.method: 采用多阶段半监督训练框架，通过动态更新教师模型并结合动量模型生成伪标签，在学生网络训练过程中持续优化伪标签质量。
AI.result: 在五个公开数据集上以20%标注数据达到84.1%平均Dice系数，较常规方法提升3%，部分数据集接近全监督结果。
AI.conclusion: 在线伪标签结合动量更新有效提升了半监督分割性能，为医学图像分析中有限标注数据场景提供了可行解决方案。

authors_text: Blaž Škrlj,Adi Schwartz,Jure Ferlež,Davorin Kopič,Naama Ziporin
title: Dynamic Surrogate Switching: Sample-Efficient Search for Factorization Machine Configurations in Online Recommendations
title_zh: 动态代理切换：在线推荐中因子分解机配置的样本高效搜索
abstract: Hyperparameter optimization is the process of identifying the appropriate hyperparameter configuration of a given machine learning model with regard to a given learning task. For smaller data sets, an exhaustive search is possible; However, when the data size and model complexity increase, the number of configuration evaluations becomes the main computational bottleneck. A promising paradigm for tackling this type of problem is surrogate-based optimization. The main idea underlying this paradigm considers an incrementally updated model of the relation between the hyperparameter space and the output (target) space; the data for this model are obtained by evaluating the main learning engine, which is, for example, a factorization machine-based model. By learning to approximate the hyperparameter-target relation, the surrogate (machine learning) model can be used to score large amounts of hyperparameter configurations, exploring parts of the configuration space beyond the reach of direct machine learning engine evaluation. Commonly, a surrogate is selected prior to optimization initialization and remains the same during the search. We investigated whether dynamic switching of surrogates during the optimization itself is a sensible idea of practical relevance for selecting the most appropriate factorization machine-based models for large-scale online recommendation. We conducted benchmarks on data sets containing hundreds of millions of instances against established baselines such as Random Forest- and Gaussian process-based surrogates. The results indicate that surrogate switching can offer good performance while considering fewer learning engine evaluations.
summary: Hyperparameter optimization is the process of identifying the appropriate hyperparameter configuration of a given machine learning model with regard to a given learning task. For smaller data sets, an exhaustive search is possible; However, when the data size and model complexity increase, the number of configuration evaluations becomes the main computational bottleneck. A promising paradigm for tackling this type of problem is surrogate-based optimization. The main idea underlying this paradigm considers an incrementally updated model of the relation between the hyperparameter space and the output (target) space; the data for this model are obtained by evaluating the main learning engine, which is, for example, a factorization machine-based model. By learning to approximate the hyperparameter-target relation, the surrogate (machine learning) model can be used to score large amounts of hyperparameter configurations, exploring parts of the configuration space beyond the reach of direct machine learning engine evaluation. Commonly, a surrogate is selected prior to optimization initialization and remains the same during the search. We investigated whether dynamic switching of surrogates during the optimization itself is a sensible idea of practical relevance for selecting the most appropriate factorization machine-based models for large-scale online recommendation. We conducted benchmarks on data sets containing hundreds of millions of instances against established baselines such as Random Forest- and Gaussian process-based surrogates. The results indicate that surrogate switching can offer good performance while considering fewer learning engine evaluations.
abstract_zh: 超参数优化是针对给定学习任务识别给定机器学习模型的适当超参数配置的过程。对于较小的数据集，可以进行穷举搜索；然而，当数据规模和模型复杂度增加时，配置评估的数量成为主要的计算瓶颈。解决此类问题的一个有前途的范例是基于代理的优化。该范式的主要思想是考虑超参数空间和输出（目标）空间之间关系的增量更新模型；该模型的数据是通过评估主学习引擎获得的，例如，基于分解机的模型。通过学习近似超参数-目标关系，代理（机器学习）模型可用于对大量超参数配置进行评分，探索超出直接机器学习引擎评估范围的部分配置空间。通常，在优化初始化之前选择代理并在搜索期间保持不变。我们研究了优化过程中代理的动态切换是否是一个具有实际意义的明智想法，可以为大规模在线推荐选择最合适的基于分解机的模型。我们对包含数亿个实例的数据集根据已建立的基线（例如基于随机森林和高斯过程的代理）进行了基准测试。结果表明，代理切换可以提供良好的性能，同时考虑较少的学习引擎评估。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种动态替代模型切换方法，用于在大规模在线推荐系统中高效搜索因子分解机的超参数配置。
AI.motivation: 传统超参数优化在大规模数据和复杂模型下计算成本过高，静态替代模型难以适应动态变化的优化需求，需探索更高效的搜索策略。
AI.method: 通过在优化过程中动态切换随机森林和高斯过程等替代模型，结合因子分解机主模型的评估结果，减少对主模型的调用次数。
AI.result: 在包含数十亿实例的数据集上，动态切换方法相比静态替代模型减少了主模型评估次数，同时保持了相近的优化性能。
AI.conclusion: 动态替代模型切换策略能有效提升大规模在线推荐系统中超参数优化的样本效率，具有实际应用价值。

search_text: Dynamic Surrogate Switching: Sample-Efficient Search for Factorization Machine Configurations in Online Recommendations 动态代理切换：在线推荐中因子分解机配置的样本高效搜索 Hyperparameter optimization is the process of identifying the appropriate hyperparameter configuration of a given machine learning model with regard to a given learning task. For smaller data sets, an exhaustive search is possible; However, when the data size and model complexity increase, the number of configuration evaluations becomes the main computational bottleneck. A promising paradigm for tackling this type of problem is surrogate-based optimization. The main idea underlying this paradigm considers an incrementally updated model of the relation between the hyperparameter space and the output (target) space; the data for this model are obtained by evaluating the main learning engine, which is, for example, a factorization machine-based model. By learning to approximate the hyperparameter-target relation, the surrogate (machine learning) model can be used to score large amounts of hyperparameter configurations, exploring parts of the configuration space beyond the reach of direct machine learning engine evaluation. Commonly, a surrogate is selected prior to optimization initialization and remains the same during the search. We investigated whether dynamic switching of surrogates during the optimization itself is a sensible idea of practical relevance for selecting the most appropriate factorization machine-based models for large-scale online recommendation. We conducted benchmarks on data sets containing hundreds of millions of instances against established baselines such as Random Forest- and Gaussian process-based surrogates. The results indicate that surrogate switching can offer good performance while considering fewer learning engine evaluations. 超参数优化是针对给定学习任务识别给定机器学习模型的适当超参数配置的过程。对于较小的数据集，可以进行穷举搜索；然而，当数据规模和模型复杂度增加时，配置评估的数量成为主要的计算瓶颈。解决此类问题的一个有前途的范例是基于代理的优化。该范式的主要思想是考虑超参数空间和输出（目标）空间之间关系的增量更新模型；该模型的数据是通过评估主学习引擎获得的，例如，基于分解机的模型。通过学习近似超参数-目标关系，代理（机器学习）模型可用于对大量超参数配置进行评分，探索超出直接机器学习引擎评估范围的部分配置空间。通常，在优化初始化之前选择代理并在搜索期间保持不变。我们研究了优化过程中代理的动态切换是否是一个具有实际意义的明智想法，可以为大规模在线推荐选择最合适的基于分解机的模型。我们对包含数亿个实例的数据集根据已建立的基线（例如基于随机森林和高斯过程的代理）进行了基准测试。结果表明，代理切换可以提供良好的性能，同时考虑较少的学习引擎评估。 本文提出了一种动态替代模型切换方法，用于在大规模在线推荐系统中高效搜索因子分解机的超参数配置。 Blaž Škrlj,Adi Schwartz,Jure Ferlež,Davorin Kopič,Naama Ziporin cs.LG,cs.AI

title: Bayesian Neural Network Versus Ex-Post Calibration For Prediction Uncertainty
title_zh: 贝叶斯神经网络与预测不确定性的事后校准
abstract: Probabilistic predictions from neural networks which account for predictive uncertainty during classification is crucial in many real-world and high-impact decision making settings. However, in practice most datasets are trained on non-probabilistic neural networks which by default do not capture this inherent uncertainty. This well-known problem has led to the development of post-hoc calibration procedures, such as Platt scaling (logistic), isotonic and beta calibration, which transforms the scores into well calibrated empirical probabilities. A plausible alternative to the calibration approach is to use Bayesian neural networks, which directly models a predictive distribution. Although they have been applied to images and text datasets, they have seen limited adoption in the tabular and small data regime. In this paper, we demonstrate that Bayesian neural networks yields competitive performance when compared to calibrated neural networks and conduct experiments across a wide array of datasets.
summary: Probabilistic predictions from neural networks which account for predictive uncertainty during classification is crucial in many real-world and high-impact decision making settings. However, in practice most datasets are trained on non-probabilistic neural networks which by default do not capture this inherent uncertainty. This well-known problem has led to the development of post-hoc calibration procedures, such as Platt scaling (logistic), isotonic and beta calibration, which transforms the scores into well calibrated empirical probabilities. A plausible alternative to the calibration approach is to use Bayesian neural networks, which directly models a predictive distribution. Although they have been applied to images and text datasets, they have seen limited adoption in the tabular and small data regime. In this paper, we demonstrate that Bayesian neural networks yields competitive performance when compared to calibrated neural networks and conduct experiments across a wide array of datasets.
abstract_zh: 神经网络的概率预测可以解释分类过程中的预测不确定性，这在许多现实世界和高影响力的决策环境中至关重要。然而，实际上，大多数数据集都是在非概率神经网络上进行训练的，默认情况下，非概率神经网络不会捕获这种固有的不确定性。这个众所周知的问题导致了事后校准程序的发展，例如 Platt 缩放（逻辑）、等渗校准和 beta 校准，它们将分数转换为经过良好校准的经验概率。校准方法的一个可行替代方案是使用贝叶斯神经网络，它直接对预测分布进行建模。尽管它们已应用于图像和文本数据集，但它们在表格和小数据体系中的采用有限。在本文中，我们证明了贝叶斯神经网络与校准神经网络相比具有竞争性的性能，并在广泛的数据集上进行了实验。

AI.tldr: 比较贝叶斯神经网络与事后校准方法在预测不确定性任务中的性能差异。
AI.motivation: 传统神经网络难以捕捉预测不确定性，事后校准虽常用但存在局限，贝叶斯神经网络理论上更优却未在表格数据中充分验证。
AI.method: 在多种数据集上对比贝叶斯神经网络与Platt scaling、等距校准等事后校准方法的预测性能。
AI.result: 贝叶斯神经网络在多个数据集上展现出与事后校准方法相当的竞争力。
AI.conclusion: 贝叶斯神经网络可作为表格数据和小样本场景下预测不确定性的有效解决方案。

title: Denoising MCMC for Accelerating Diffusion-Based Generative Models
title_zh: 用于加速基于扩散的生成模型的去噪 MCMC
abstract: Diffusion models are powerful generative models that simulate the reverse of diffusion processes using score functions to synthesize data from noise. The sampling process of diffusion models can be interpreted as solving the reverse stochastic differential equation (SDE) or the ordinary differential equation (ODE) of the diffusion process, which often requires up to thousands of discretization steps to generate a single image. This has sparked a great interest in developing efficient integration techniques for reverse-S/ODEs. Here, we propose an orthogonal approach to accelerating score-based sampling: Denoising MCMC (DMCMC). DMCMC first uses MCMC to produce samples in the product space of data and variance (or diffusion time). Then, a reverse-S/ODE integrator is used to denoise the MCMC samples. Since MCMC traverses close to the data manifold, the computation cost of producing a clean sample for DMCMC is much less than that of producing a clean sample from noise. To verify the proposed concept, we show that Denoising Langevin Gibbs (DLG), an instance of DMCMC, successfully accelerates all six reverse-S/ODE integrators considered in this work on the tasks of CIFAR10 and CelebA-HQ-256 image generation. Notably, combined with integrators of Karras et al. (2022) and pre-trained score models of Song et al. (2021b), DLG achieves SOTA results. In the limited number of score function evaluation (NFE) settings on CIFAR10, we have $3.86$ FID with $\approx 10$ NFE and $2.63$ FID with $\approx 20$ NFE. On CelebA-HQ-256, we have $6.99$ FID with $\approx 160$ NFE, which beats the current best record of Kim et al. (2022) among score-based models, $7.16$ FID with $4000$ NFE. Code: https://github.com/1202kbs/DMCMC

The sampling process of diffusion models can be interpreted as solving the reverse stochastic differential equation (SDE) or the ordinary differential equation (ODE) of the diffusion process, which often requires up to thousands of discretization steps to generate a single image. This has sparked a great interest in developing efficient integration techniques for reverse-S/ODEs. Here, we propose an orthogonal approach to accelerating score-based sampling: Denoising MCMC (DMCMC). DMCMC first uses MCMC to produce samples in the product space of data and variance (or diffusion time). Then, a reverse-S/ODE integrator is used to denoise the MCMC samples. Since MCMC traverses close to the data manifold, the computation cost of producing a clean sample for DMCMC is much less than that of producing a clean sample from noise. To verify the proposed concept, we show that Denoising Langevin Gibbs (DLG), an instance of DMCMC, successfully accelerates all six reverse-S/ODE integrators considered in this work on the tasks of CIFAR10 and CelebA-HQ-256 image generation. Notably, combined with integrators of Karras et al. (2022) and pre-trained score models of Song et al. (2021b), DLG achieves SOTA results. In the limited number of score function evaluation (NFE) settings on CIFAR10, we have $3.86$ FID with $\approx 10$ NFE and $2.63$ FID with $\approx 20$ NFE. On CelebA-HQ-256, we have $6.99$ FID with $\approx 160$ NFE, which beats the current best record of Kim et al. (2022) among score-based models, $7.16$ FID with $4000$ NFE. Code: https://github.com/1202kbs/DMCMC
abstract_zh: 扩散模型是强大的生成模型，它使用评分函数从噪声中合成数据来模拟扩散过程的逆过程。扩散模型的采样过程可以解释为求解扩散过程的反随机微分方程（SDE）或常微分方程（ODE），这通常需要多达数千个离散化步骤才能生成单个图像。这引发了人们对开发逆 S/ODE 的高效积分技术的极大兴趣。在这里，我们提出了一种加速基于分数的采样的正交方法：去噪 MCMC (DMCMC)。 DMCMC 首先使用 MCMC 在数据和方差（或扩散时间）的乘积空间中生成样本。然后，使用反向 S/ODE 积分器对 MCMC 样本进行去噪。由于 MCMC 遍历接近数据流形，因此为 DMCMC 生成干净样本的计算成本远低于从噪声生成干净样本的计算成本。为了验证所提出的概念，我们展示了 Denoising Langevin Gibbs (DLG)（DMCMC 的一个实例）成功加速了本工作中考虑的 CIFAR10 和 CelebA-HQ-256 图像生成任务中的所有六个反向 S/ODE 积分器。值得注意的是，与 Karras 等人的积分器相结合。 (2022) 和 Song 等人的预训练评分模型。 (2021b)，DLG 取得了 SOTA 成果。在 CIFAR10 上有限数量的评分函数评估 (NFE) 设置中，我们有 $3.86$ FID 和 $\approx 10$ NFE，$2.63$ FID 和 $\approx 20$ NFE。在 CelebA-HQ-256 上，我们的 FID 为 6.99 美元，NFE 为 160 美元，这打破了 Kim 等人目前的最佳记录。 (2022) 在基于分数的模型中，FID 为 7.16 美元，NFE 为 4000 美元。代码：https://github.com/1202kbs/DMCMC

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Denoising MCMC（DMCMC）的新方法，通过结合马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛（MCMC）采样和去噪过程，加速扩散模型的图像生成过程。
AI.motivation: 扩散模型生成图像需要数千次离散化步骤，计算成本高。现有方法主要聚焦于改进反向S/ODE求解器，但未充分利用MCMC在数据流形附近的高效采样特性。
AI.method: DMCMC首先在数据与方差（扩散时间）的乘积空间中使用MCMC生成样本，再通过反向S/ODE积分器对MCMC样本进行去噪。由于MCMC接近数据流形，去噪计算成本显著低于从纯噪声生成样本。
AI.result: 在CIFAR10和CelebA-HQ-256数据集上，DMCMC的实例Denoising Langevin Gibbs（DLG）在少量得分函数评估（NFE）下取得SOTA结果。例如，CIFAR10在约10次NFE时FID为3.86，CelebA-HQ-256在约160次NFE时FID为6.99，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: DMCMC通过结合MCMC与去噪技术，显著降低了扩散模型的计算成本，同时保持生成质量，为加速生成模型提供了新思路。

tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Denoising MCMC（DMCMC）的新方法，通过结合马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛（MCMC）采样和去噪过程，加速扩散模型的图像生成过程。
motivation: 扩散模型生成图像需要数千次离散化步骤，计算成本高。现有方法主要聚焦于改进反向S/ODE求解器，但未充分利用MCMC在数据流形附近的高效采样特性。
method: DMCMC首先在数据与方差（扩散时间）的乘积空间中使用MCMC生成样本，再通过反向S/ODE积分器对MCMC样本进行去噪。由于MCMC接近数据流形，去噪计算成本显著低于从纯噪声生成样本。
result: 在CIFAR10和CelebA-HQ-256数据集上，DMCMC的实例Denoising Langevin Gibbs（DLG）在少量得分函数评估（NFE）下取得SOTA结果。例如，CIFAR10在约10次NFE时FID为3.86，CelebA-HQ-256在约160次NFE时FID为6.99，优于现有方法。
conclusion: DMCMC通过结合MCMC与去噪技术，显著降低了扩散模型的计算成本，同时保持生成质量，为加速生成模型提供了新思路。
search_text: Denoising MCMC for Accelerating Diffusion-Based Generative Models 用于加速基于扩散的生成模型的去噪 MCMC Diffusion models are powerful generative models that simulate the reverse of diffusion processes using score functions to synthesize data from noise. The sampling process of diffusion models can be interpreted as solving the reverse stochastic differential equation (SDE) or the ordinary differential equation (ODE) of the diffusion process, which often requires up to thousands of discretization steps to generate a single image. This has sparked a great interest in developing efficient integration techniques for reverse-S/ODEs. Here, we propose an orthogonal approach to accelerating score-based sampling: Denoising MCMC (DMCMC). DMCMC first uses MCMC to produce samples in the product space of data and variance (or diffusion time). Then, a reverse-S/ODE integrator is used to denoise the MCMC samples. Since MCMC traverses close to the data manifold, the computation cost of producing a clean sample for DMCMC is much less than that of producing a clean sample from noise. To verify the proposed concept, we show that Denoising Langevin Gibbs (DLG), an instance of DMCMC, successfully accelerates all six reverse-S/ODE integrators considered in this work on the tasks of CIFAR10 and CelebA-HQ-256 image generation. Notably, combined with integrators of Karras et al. (2022) and pre-trained score models of Song et al. (2021b), DLG achieves SOTA results. In the limited number of score function evaluation (NFE) settings on CIFAR10, we have $3.86$ FID with $\approx 10$ NFE and $2.63$ FID with $\approx 20$ NFE. On CelebA-HQ-256, we have $6.99$ FID with $\approx 160$ NFE, which beats the current best record of Kim et al. (2022) among score-based models, $7.16$ FID with $4000$ NFE. Code: https://github.com/1202kbs/DMCMC 扩散模型是强大的生成模型，它使用评分函数从噪声中合成数据来模拟扩散过程的逆过程。扩散模型的采样过程可以解释为求解扩散过程的反随机微分方程（SDE）或常微分方程（ODE），这通常需要多达数千个离散化步骤才能生成单个图像。这引发了人们对开发逆 S/ODE 的高效积分技术的极大兴趣。在这里，我们提出了一种加速基于分数的采样的正交方法：去噪 MCMC (DMCMC)。 DMCMC 首先使用 MCMC 在数据和方差（或扩散时间）的乘积空间中生成样本。然后，使用反向 S/ODE 积分器对 MCMC 样本进行去噪。由于 MCMC 遍历接近数据流形，因此为 DMCMC 生成干净样本的计算成本远低于从噪声生成干净样本的计算成本。为了验证所提出的概念，我们展示了 Denoising Langevin Gibbs (DLG)（DMCMC 的一个实例）成功加速了本工作中考虑的 CIFAR10 和 CelebA-HQ-256 图像生成任务中的所有六个反向 S/ODE 积分器。值得注意的是，与 Karras 等人的积分器相结合。 (2022) 和 Song 等人的预训练评分模型。 (2021b)，DLG 取得了 SOTA 成果。在 CIFAR10 上有限数量的评分函数评估 (NFE) 设置中，我们有 $3.86$ FID 和 $\approx 10$ NFE，$2.63$ FID 和 $\approx 20$ NFE。在 CelebA-HQ-256 上，我们的 FID 为 6.99 美元，NFE 为 160 美元，这打破了 Kim 等人目前的最佳记录。 (2022) 在基于分数的模型中，FID 为 7.16 美元，NFE 为 4000 美元。代码：https://github.com/1202kbs/DMCMC 本文提出了一种名为Denoising MCMC（DMCMC）的新方法，通过结合马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛（MCMC）采样和去噪过程，加速扩散模型的图像生成过程。 Beomsu Kim,Jong Chul Ye cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Mohammad Imrul Jubair,Ali Ahnaf,Tashfiq Nahiyan Khan,Ullash Bhattacharjee,Tanjila Joti
title: PerSign: Personalized Bangladeshi Sign Letters Synthesis
title_zh: PerSign：个性化孟加拉语手语字母合成
abstract: Bangladeshi Sign Language (BdSL) - like other sign languages - is tough to learn for general people, especially when it comes to expressing letters. In this poster, we propose PerSign, a system that can reproduce a person's image by introducing sign gestures in it. We make this operation personalized, which means the generated image keeps the person's initial image profile - face, skin tone, attire, background - unchanged while altering the hand, palm, and finger positions appropriately. We use an image-to-image translation technique and build a corresponding unique dataset to accomplish the task. We believe the translated image can reduce the communication gap between signers (person who uses sign language) and non-signers without having prior knowledge of BdSL.
summary: Bangladeshi Sign Language (BdSL) - like other sign languages - is tough to learn for general people, especially when it comes to expressing letters. In this poster, we propose PerSign, a system that can reproduce a person's image by introducing sign gestures in it. We make this operation personalized, which means the generated image keeps the person's initial image profile - face, skin tone, attire, background - unchanged while altering the hand, palm, and finger positions appropriately. We use an image-to-image translation technique and build a corresponding unique dataset to accomplish the task. We believe the translated image can reduce the communication gap between signers (person who uses sign language) and non-signers without having prior knowledge of BdSL.
abstract_zh: 孟加拉国手语（BdSL）与其他手语一样，对于普通人来说很难学习，尤其是在表达字母时。在这张海报中，我们提出了 PerSign，这是一个可以通过在其中引入手势来再现人的图像的系统。我们使此操作个性化，这意味着生成的图像保持人的初始图像轮廓（面部、肤色、服装、背景）不变，同时适当改变手、手掌和手指的位置。我们使用图像到图像的转换技术并构建相应的独特数据集来完成任务。我们相信翻译后的图像可以减少手语者（使用手语的人）和非手语者之间的沟通差距，而无需事先了解 BdSL。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PerSign系统，用于生成个性化孟加拉手语字母图像，保持人物原有特征不变仅调整手势。
AI.motivation: 孟加拉手语字母难以学习，导致手语使用者与非使用者间存在沟通障碍。
AI.method: 采用图像到图像翻译技术，构建专用数据集实现手势个性化生成。
AI.result: 生成的图像能有效保持人物原始特征同时正确表达手语字母。
AI.conclusion: 该系统可降低手语学习门槛，促进手语使用者与非使用者间的交流。

authors_text: Mohammad Imrul Jubair,Arafat Ibne Yousuf,Tashfiq Ahmed,Hasanath Jamy,Foisal Reza,Mohsena Ashraf
title: DIY Graphics Tab: A Cost-Effective Alternative to Graphics Tablet for Educators
title_zh: DIY 图形选项卡：适合教育工作者的图形输入板的经济高效替代方案
abstract: Recording lectures is a normal task for online educators, and a graphics tablet is a great tool for that. However, it is very expensive for many instructors. In this paper, we propose an alternative called "DIY Graphics Tab" that functions largely in the same way as a graphic tab, but requires only a pen, paper, and laptop's webcam. Our system takes images of writings on a paper with a webcam and outputs the contents. The task is not straightforward since there are obstacles, such as hand occlusion, paper movements, lighting, and perspective distortion due to the viewing angle. A pipeline is used that applies segmentation and post-processing for generating appropriate output from input frames. We also conducted user experience evaluations from the teachers.
summary: Recording lectures is a normal task for online educators, and a graphics tablet is a great tool for that. However, it is very expensive for many instructors. In this paper, we propose an alternative called "DIY Graphics Tab" that functions largely in the same way as a graphic tab, but requires only a pen, paper, and laptop's webcam. Our system takes images of writings on a paper with a webcam and outputs the contents. The task is not straightforward since there are obstacles, such as hand occlusion, paper movements, lighting, and perspective distortion due to the viewing angle. A pipeline is used that applies segmentation and post-processing for generating appropriate output from input frames. We also conducted user experience evaluations from the teachers.
abstract_zh: 录制讲座对于在线教育工作者来说是一项正常任务，而绘图板是一个很好的工具。然而，对于许多教练来说，这是非常昂贵的。在本文中，我们提出了一种称为“DIY 图形选项卡”的替代方案，其功能与图形选项卡基本相同，但只需要笔、纸和笔记本电脑的网络摄像头。我们的系统使用网络摄像头拍摄纸上的文字图像并输出内容。这项任务并不简单，因为存在一些障碍，例如手遮挡、纸张移动、照明以及由于视角导致的透视失真。使用管道应用分段和后处理来从输入帧生成适当的输出。我们还对老师进行了用户体验评估。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种低成本的DIY图形板替代方案，利用笔、纸和笔记本电脑摄像头实现手写内容的数字化记录。
AI.motivation: 图形板价格昂贵，限制了教育工作者在在线教学中使用，需开发经济高效的替代方案。
AI.method: 通过摄像头捕捉纸张上的手写内容，结合图像分割与后处理技术解决遮挡、纸张移动、光照和视角畸变等问题。
AI.result: 系统成功实现手写内容的数字化输出，并通过教师用户体验评估验证了可行性。
AI.conclusion: DIY Graphics Tab为教育者提供了低成本的图形板替代方案，但在精度和稳定性上仍需进一步优化。

title: Spatial Moment Pooling Improves Neural Image Assessment
title_zh: 空间矩池化改善神经图像评估
abstract: In recent years, there has been widespread attention drawn to convolutional neural network (CNN) based blind image quality assessment (IQA). A large number of works start by extracting deep features from CNN. Then, those features are processed through spatial average pooling (SAP) and fully connected layers to predict quality. Inspired by full reference IQA and texture features, in this paper, we extend SAP ($1^{st}$ moment) into spatial moment pooling (SMP) by incorporating higher order moments (such as variance, skewness). Moreover, we provide learning friendly normalization to circumvent numerical issue when computing gradients of higher moments. Experimental results suggest that simply upgrading SAP to SMP significantly enhances CNN-based blind IQA methods and achieves state of the art performance.
summary: In recent years, there has been widespread attention drawn to convolutional neural network (CNN) based blind image quality assessment (IQA). A large number of works start by extracting deep features from CNN. Then, those features are processed through spatial average pooling (SAP) and fully connected layers to predict quality. Inspired by full reference IQA and texture features, in this paper, we extend SAP ($1^{st}$ moment) into spatial moment pooling (SMP) by incorporating higher order moments (such as variance, skewness). Moreover, we provide learning friendly normalization to circumvent numerical issue when computing gradients of higher moments. Experimental results suggest that simply upgrading SAP to SMP significantly enhances CNN-based blind IQA methods and achieves state of the art performance.
abstract_zh: 近年来，基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的盲图像质量评估（IQA）受到了广泛关注。大量工作都是从从 CNN 中提取深层特征开始的。然后，通过空间平均池化（SAP）和全连接层处理这些特征以预测质量。受全参考IQA和纹理特征的启发，在本文中，我们通过合并高阶矩（例如方差、偏度），将SAP（$1^{st}$矩）扩展到空间矩池（SMP）。此外，我们提供学习友好的归一化，以避免计算更高矩梯度时的数值问题。实验结果表明，只需将 SAP 升级到 SMP 即可显着增强基于 CNN 的盲 IQA 方法并实现最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种改进的基于卷积神经网络的盲图像质量评估方法，通过引入空间矩池化（SMP）替代传统空间平均池化（SAP）以提升性能。
AI.motivation: 传统CNN盲图像质量评估方法仅使用空间平均池化（SAP）可能丢失高阶统计信息，而全参考IQA和纹理特征研究表明高阶矩（如方差、偏度）对质量评估至关重要。
AI.method: 将SAP扩展为包含高阶矩（方差、偏度）的空间矩池化（SMP），并设计学习友好型归一化方法解决高阶矩梯度计算中的数值问题。
AI.result: 实验表明，仅将SAP升级为SMP即可显著提升CNN盲IQA性能，达到当时最先进的水平。
AI.conclusion: 空间矩池化通过引入高阶统计特征有效提升了图像质量评估模型性能，且方法简单易行，具有实际应用价值。

ai_content.method: 将SAP扩展为包含高阶矩（方差、偏度）的空间矩池化（SMP），并设计学习友好型归一化方法解决高阶矩梯度计算中的数值问题。
ai_content.result: 实验表明，仅将SAP升级为SMP即可显著提升CNN盲IQA性能，达到当时最先进的水平。
ai_content.conclusion: 空间矩池化通过引入高阶统计特征有效提升了图像质量评估模型性能，且方法简单易行，具有实际应用价值。

search_text: Spatial Moment Pooling Improves Neural Image Assessment 空间矩池化改善神经图像评估 In recent years, there has been widespread attention drawn to convolutional neural network (CNN) based blind image quality assessment (IQA). A large number of works start by extracting deep features from CNN. Then, those features are processed through spatial average pooling (SAP) and fully connected layers to predict quality. Inspired by full reference IQA and texture features, in this paper, we extend SAP ($1^{st}$ moment) into spatial moment pooling (SMP) by incorporating higher order moments (such as variance, skewness). Moreover, we provide learning friendly normalization to circumvent numerical issue when computing gradients of higher moments. Experimental results suggest that simply upgrading SAP to SMP significantly enhances CNN-based blind IQA methods and achieves state of the art performance. 近年来，基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的盲图像质量评估（IQA）受到了广泛关注。大量工作都是从从 CNN 中提取深层特征开始的。然后，通过空间平均池化（SAP）和全连接层处理这些特征以预测质量。受全参考IQA和纹理特征的启发，在本文中，我们通过合并高阶矩（例如方差、偏度），将SAP（$1^{st}$矩）扩展到空间矩池（SMP）。此外，我们提供学习友好的归一化，以避免计算更高矩梯度时的数值问题。实验结果表明，只需将 SAP 升级到 SMP 即可显着增强基于 CNN 的盲 IQA 方法并实现最先进的性能。 本文提出一种改进的基于卷积神经网络的盲图像质量评估方法，通过引入空间矩池化（SMP）替代传统空间平均池化（SAP）以提升性能。 Tongda Xu,Yifan Shao,Yan Wang,Hongwei Qin cs.CV

authors_text: Tongda Xu,Han Gao,Yuanyuan Wang,Hongwei Qin,Yan Wang,Jingjing Liu,Ya-Qin Zhang
title: Correcting the Sub-optimal Bit Allocation
title_zh: 纠正次优比特分配
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the problem of bit allocation in Neural Video Compression (NVC). First, we reveal that a recent bit allocation approach claimed to be optimal is, in fact, sub-optimal due to its implementation. Specifically, we find that its sub-optimality lies in the improper application of semi-amortized variational inference (SAVI) on latent with non-factorized variational posterior. Then, we show that the corrected version of SAVI on non-factorized latent requires recursively applying back-propagating through gradient ascent, based on which we derive the corrected optimal bit allocation algorithm. Due to the computational in-feasibility of the corrected bit allocation, we design an efficient approximation to make it practical. Empirical results show that our proposed correction significantly improves the incorrect bit allocation in terms of R-D performance and bitrate error, and outperforms all other bit allocation methods by a large margin. The source code is provided in the supplementary material.
summary: In this paper, we investigate the problem of bit allocation in Neural Video Compression (NVC). First, we reveal that a recent bit allocation approach claimed to be optimal is, in fact, sub-optimal due to its implementation. Specifically, we find that its sub-optimality lies in the improper application of semi-amortized variational inference (SAVI) on latent with non-factorized variational posterior. Then, we show that the corrected version of SAVI on non-factorized latent requires recursively applying back-propagating through gradient ascent, based on which we derive the corrected optimal bit allocation algorithm. Due to the computational in-feasibility of the corrected bit allocation, we design an efficient approximation to make it practical. Empirical results show that our proposed correction significantly improves the incorrect bit allocation in terms of R-D performance and bitrate error, and outperforms all other bit allocation methods by a large margin. The source code is provided in the supplementary material.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们研究了神经视频压缩（NVC）中的比特分配问题。首先，我们揭示了最近声称是最佳的比特分配方法实际上由于其实现而并非最佳。具体来说，我们发现其次优性在于半摊销变分推理（SAVI）对非因式分解变分后验的潜在应用不当。然后，我们证明了 SAVI 在非因式分解潜伏上的校正版本需要通过梯度上升递归地应用反向传播，基于此我们推导出校正后的最佳比特分配算法。由于校正位分配在计算上不可行，我们设计了一种有效的近似方法以使其实用。实证结果表明，我们提出的校正在 R-D 性能和比特率误差方面显着改善了不正确的比特分配，并且大大优于所有其他比特分配方法。源代码在补充材料中提供。

AI.tldr: 本文研究神经视频压缩中的比特分配问题，提出修正子最优比特分配方法以提升压缩性能。
AI.motivation: 近期提出的比特分配方法声称最优，但实际因半 amortized 变分推断（SAVI）在非因子化后验上的误用导致子最优，需修正以提升压缩效率。
AI.method: 分析 SAVI 在非因子化潜变量中的应用缺陷，推导需递归反向传播梯度上升的修正算法，并设计高效近似方案实现计算可行性。
AI.result: 修正方法显著提升率失真（R-D）性能和比特率误差，相较其他方法有明显优势，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 修正后的比特分配算法有效解决了原有子最优问题，为神经视频压缩提供了更优的解决方案。

title: Diffusion Adversarial Representation Learning for Self-supervised Vessel Segmentation
title_zh: 用于自监督血管分割的扩散对抗表示学习
abstract: Vessel segmentation in medical images is one of the important tasks in the diagnosis of vascular diseases and therapy planning. Although learning-based segmentation approaches have been extensively studied, a large amount of ground-truth labels are required in supervised methods and confusing background structures make neural networks hard to segment vessels in an unsupervised manner. To address this, here we introduce a novel diffusion adversarial representation learning (DARL) model that leverages a denoising diffusion probabilistic model with adversarial learning, and apply it to vessel segmentation. In particular, for self-supervised vessel segmentation, DARL learns the background signal using a diffusion module, which lets a generation module effectively provide vessel representations. Also, by adversarial learning based on the proposed switchable spatially-adaptive denormalization, our model estimates synthetic fake vessel images as well as vessel segmentation masks, which further makes the model capture vessel-relevant semantic information. Once the proposed model is trained, the model generates segmentation masks in a single step and can be applied to general vascular structure segmentation of coronary angiography and retinal images. Experimental results on various datasets show that our method significantly outperforms existing unsupervised and self-supervised vessel segmentation methods.
summary: Vessel segmentation in medical images is one of the important tasks in the diagnosis of vascular diseases and therapy planning. Although learning-based segmentation approaches have been extensively studied, a large amount of ground-truth labels are required in supervised methods and confusing background structures make neural networks hard to segment vessels in an unsupervised manner. To address this, here we introduce a novel diffusion adversarial representation learning (DARL) model that leverages a denoising diffusion probabilistic model with adversarial learning, and apply it to vessel segmentation. In particular, for self-supervised vessel segmentation, DARL learns the background signal using a diffusion module, which lets a generation module effectively provide vessel representations. Also, by adversarial learning based on the proposed switchable spatially-adaptive denormalization, our model estimates synthetic fake vessel images as well as vessel segmentation masks, which further makes the model capture vessel-relevant semantic information. Once the proposed model is trained, the model generates segmentation masks in a single step and can be applied to general vascular structure segmentation of coronary angiography and retinal images. Experimental results on various datasets show that our method significantly outperforms existing unsupervised and self-supervised vessel segmentation methods.
abstract_zh: 医学图像中的血管分割是血管疾病诊断和治疗规划中的重要任务之一。尽管基于学习的分割方法已被广泛研究，但监督方法中需要大量的真实标签，并且混乱的背景结构使得神经网络难以以无监督的方式分割血管。为了解决这个问题，我们在这里介绍一种新颖的扩散对抗表示学习（DARL）模型，该模型利用具有对抗性学习的去噪扩散概率模型，并将其应用于血管分割。特别是，对于自监督血管分割，DARL 使用扩散模块学习背景信号，这使得生成模块有效地提供血管表示。此外，通过基于所提出的可切换空间自适应反规范化的对抗性学习，我们的模型估计合成的假血管图像以及血管分割掩模，这进一步使模型捕获血管相关的语义信息。一旦所提出的模型经过训练，该模型即可一步生成分割掩模，并可应用于冠状动脉造影和视网膜图像的一般血管结构分割。各种数据集的实验结果表明，我们的方法显着优于现有的无监督和自监督血管分割方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于扩散对抗表示学习（DARL）的自监督血管分割方法，用于解决医学图像中血管分割的标注数据依赖和复杂背景干扰问题。
AI.motivation: 传统监督分割方法依赖大量标注数据，而无监督方法难以处理医学图像中的复杂背景结构，导致分割效果不佳。
AI.method: 结合去噪扩散概率模型与对抗学习，通过扩散模块学习背景信号并生成血管表示，利用可切换空间自归一化对抗学习生成合成血管图像及分割掩码。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上显著优于现有无监督和自监督血管分割方法，可应用于冠状动脉造影和视网膜图像的通用血管结构分割。
AI.conclusion: DARL模型通过融合扩散生成与对抗学习，有效捕捉血管语义信息，在无需标注数据的情况下实现了高精度血管分割。

Also, by adversarial learning based on the proposed switchable spatially-adaptive denormalization, our model estimates synthetic fake vessel images as well as vessel segmentation masks, which further makes the model capture vessel-relevant semantic information. Once the proposed model is trained, the model generates segmentation masks in a single step and can be applied to general vascular structure segmentation of coronary angiography and retinal images. Experimental results on various datasets show that our method significantly outperforms existing unsupervised and self-supervised vessel segmentation methods. 医学图像中的血管分割是血管疾病诊断和治疗规划中的重要任务之一。尽管基于学习的分割方法已被广泛研究，但监督方法中需要大量的真实标签，并且混乱的背景结构使得神经网络难以以无监督的方式分割血管。为了解决这个问题，我们在这里介绍一种新颖的扩散对抗表示学习（DARL）模型，该模型利用具有对抗性学习的去噪扩散概率模型，并将其应用于血管分割。特别是，对于自监督血管分割，DARL 使用扩散模块学习背景信号，这使得生成模块有效地提供血管表示。此外，通过基于所提出的可切换空间自适应反规范化的对抗性学习，我们的模型估计合成的假血管图像以及血管分割掩模，这进一步使模型捕获血管相关的语义信息。一旦所提出的模型经过训练，该模型即可一步生成分割掩模，并可应用于冠状动脉造影和视网膜图像的一般血管结构分割。各种数据集的实验结果表明，我们的方法显着优于现有的无监督和自监督血管分割方法。 本文提出一种基于扩散对抗表示学习（DARL）的自监督血管分割方法，用于解决医学图像中血管分割的标注数据依赖和复杂背景干扰问题。 Boah Kim,Yujin Oh,Jong Chul Ye eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

title: Regularizing Neural Network Training via Identity-wise Discriminative Feature Suppression
title_zh: 通过身份明智的判别特征抑制规范神经网络训练
abstract: It is well-known that a deep neural network has a strong fitting capability and can easily achieve a low training error even with randomly assigned class labels. When the number of training samples is small, or the class labels are noisy, networks tend to memorize patterns specific to individual instances to minimize the training error. This leads to the issue of overfitting and poor generalisation performance. This paper explores a remedy by suppressing the network's tendency to rely on instance-specific patterns for empirical error minimisation. The proposed method is based on an adversarial training framework. It suppresses features that can be utilized to identify individual instances among samples within each class. This leads to classifiers only using features that are both discriminative across classes and common within each class. We call our method Adversarial Suppression of Identity Features (ASIF), and demonstrate the usefulness of this technique in boosting generalisation accuracy when faced with small datasets or noisy labels. Our source code is available.
summary: It is well-known that a deep neural network has a strong fitting capability and can easily achieve a low training error even with randomly assigned class labels. When the number of training samples is small, or the class labels are noisy, networks tend to memorize patterns specific to individual instances to minimize the training error. This leads to the issue of overfitting and poor generalisation performance. This paper explores a remedy by suppressing the network's tendency to rely on instance-specific patterns for empirical error minimisation. The proposed method is based on an adversarial training framework. It suppresses features that can be utilized to identify individual instances among samples within each class. This leads to classifiers only using features that are both discriminative across classes and common within each class. We call our method Adversarial Suppression of Identity Features (ASIF), and demonstrate the usefulness of this technique in boosting generalisation accuracy when faced with small datasets or noisy labels. Our source code is available.
abstract_zh: 众所周知，深度神经网络具有很强的拟合能力，即使使用随机分配的类别标签也可以轻松实现较低的训练误差。当训练样本数量较小或类标签有噪声时，网络倾向于记住特定于单个实例的模式以最小化训练误差。这导致了过度拟合和泛化性能差的问题。本文通过抑制网络依赖特定于实例的模式来实现经验误差最小化的倾向来探索一种补救措施。所提出的方法基于对抗性训练框架。它抑制了可用于识别每个类中样本中的单个实例的特征。这导致分类器仅使用在类之间具有区分性并且在每个类内具有共同性的特征。我们将我们的方法称为身份特征对抗性抑制（ASIF），并证明了该技术在面对小数据集或噪声标签时提高泛化准确性的有用性。我们的源代码可用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种对抗训练方法ASIF，通过抑制神经网络对个体实例特征的依赖来提升模型泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 深度网络在小样本或噪声标签场景下易过拟合，通过记忆个体实例特征而非学习通用特征导致泛化性能下降。
AI.method: 基于对抗训练框架，设计身份特征抑制机制，强制模型学习跨类判别且类内共有的通用特征。
AI.result: 实验验证ASIF在小数据集和噪声标签场景下显著提升模型泛化准确率，源代码已公开。
AI.conclusion: 抑制实例特定特征的对抗训练有效缓解过拟合问题，为提升模型鲁棒性提供新思路。

authors_text: Muhammad Akbar Husnoo,Adnan Anwar,Nasser Hosseinzadeh,Shama Naz Islam,Abdun Naser Mahmood,Robin Doss
title: A Secure Federated Learning Framework for Residential Short Term Load Forecasting
title_zh: 用于住宅短期负荷预测的安全联合学习框架
abstract: Smart meter measurements, though critical for accurate demand forecasting, face several drawbacks including consumers' privacy, data breach issues, to name a few. Recent literature has explored Federated Learning (FL) as a promising privacy-preserving machine learning alternative which enables collaborative learning of a model without exposing private raw data for short term load forecasting. Despite its virtue, standard FL is still vulnerable to an intractable cyber threat known as Byzantine attack carried out by faulty and/or malicious clients. Therefore, to improve the robustness of federated short-term load forecasting against Byzantine threats, we develop a state-of-the-art differentially private secured FL-based framework that ensures the privacy of the individual smart meter's data while protect the security of FL models and architecture. Our proposed framework leverages the idea of gradient quantization through the Sign Stochastic Gradient Descent (SignSGD) algorithm, where the clients only transmit the `sign' of the gradient to the control centre after local model training. As we highlight through our experiments involving benchmark neural networks with a set of Byzantine attack models, our proposed approach mitigates such threats quite effectively and thus outperforms conventional Fed-SGD models.
summary: Smart meter measurements, though critical for accurate demand forecasting, face several drawbacks including consumers' privacy, data breach issues, to name a few. Recent literature has explored Federated Learning (FL) as a promising privacy-preserving machine learning alternative which enables collaborative learning of a model without exposing private raw data for short term load forecasting. Despite its virtue, standard FL is still vulnerable to an intractable cyber threat known as Byzantine attack carried out by faulty and/or malicious clients. Therefore, to improve the robustness of federated short-term load forecasting against Byzantine threats, we develop a state-of-the-art differentially private secured FL-based framework that ensures the privacy of the individual smart meter's data while protect the security of FL models and architecture. Our proposed framework leverages the idea of gradient quantization through the Sign Stochastic Gradient Descent (SignSGD) algorithm, where the clients only transmit the `sign' of the gradient to the control centre after local model training. As we highlight through our experiments involving benchmark neural networks with a set of Byzantine attack models, our proposed approach mitigates such threats quite effectively and thus outperforms conventional Fed-SGD models.
abstract_zh: 智能电表测量虽然对于准确的需求预测至关重要，但也面临着一些缺点，包括消费者隐私、数据泄露问题等。最近的文献探讨了联邦学习（FL）作为一种有前途的隐私保护机器学习替代方案，它可以实现模型的协作学习，而无需暴露用于短期负载预测的私人原始数据。尽管有其优点，标准 FL 仍然容易受到棘手的网络威胁（称为拜占庭攻击）的攻击，这种攻击是由有缺陷和/或恶意的客户端发起的。因此，为了提高联合短期负载预测针对拜占庭威胁的鲁棒性，我们开发了一种最先进的基于 FL 的差分隐私安全框架，该框架可确保单个智能电表数据的隐私，同时保护 FL 模型和架构的安全。我们提出的框架通过符号随机梯度下降（SignSGD）算法利用梯度量化的思想，其中客户端仅在本地模型训练后将梯度的“符号”传输到控制中心。正如我们通过涉及具有一组拜占庭攻击模型的基准神经网络的实验所强调的那样，我们提出的方法非常有效地减轻了此类威胁，因此优于传统的 Fed-SGD 模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合差分隐私与梯度量化的安全联邦学习框架，用于住宅短期负荷预测，以解决数据隐私泄露和拜占庭攻击问题。
AI.motivation: 智能电表数据虽对负荷预测至关重要，但存在隐私风险和拜占庭攻击漏洞，传统联邦学习（FL）在安全性方面不足。
AI.method: 采用SignSGD算法进行梯度量化，仅传输梯度符号，并结合差分隐私技术，增强模型安全性与隐私保护。
AI.result: 实验表明，该框架在基准神经网络和拜占庭攻击场景下有效缓解威胁，性能优于传统Fed-SGD模型。
AI.conclusion: 提出的框架在保障隐私的同时提升了联邦学习的鲁棒性，适用于实际住宅负荷预测场景。

search_text: A Secure Federated Learning Framework for Residential Short Term Load Forecasting 用于住宅短期负荷预测的安全联合学习框架 Smart meter measurements, though critical for accurate demand forecasting, face several drawbacks including consumers' privacy, data breach issues, to name a few. Recent literature has explored Federated Learning (FL) as a promising privacy-preserving machine learning alternative which enables collaborative learning of a model without exposing private raw data for short term load forecasting. Despite its virtue, standard FL is still vulnerable to an intractable cyber threat known as Byzantine attack carried out by faulty and/or malicious clients. Therefore, to improve the robustness of federated short-term load forecasting against Byzantine threats, we develop a state-of-the-art differentially private secured FL-based framework that ensures the privacy of the individual smart meter's data while protect the security of FL models and architecture. Our proposed framework leverages the idea of gradient quantization through the Sign Stochastic Gradient Descent (SignSGD) algorithm, where the clients only transmit the `sign' of the gradient to the control centre after local model training. As we highlight through our experiments involving benchmark neural networks with a set of Byzantine attack models, our proposed approach mitigates such threats quite effectively and thus outperforms conventional Fed-SGD models. 智能电表测量虽然对于准确的需求预测至关重要，但也面临着一些缺点，包括消费者隐私、数据泄露问题等。最近的文献探讨了联邦学习（FL）作为一种有前途的隐私保护机器学习替代方案，它可以实现模型的协作学习，而无需暴露用于短期负载预测的私人原始数据。尽管有其优点，标准 FL 仍然容易受到棘手的网络威胁（称为拜占庭攻击）的攻击，这种攻击是由有缺陷和/或恶意的客户端发起的。因此，为了提高联合短期负载预测针对拜占庭威胁的鲁棒性，我们开发了一种最先进的基于 FL 的差分隐私安全框架，该框架可确保单个智能电表数据的隐私，同时保护 FL 模型和架构的安全。我们提出的框架通过符号随机梯度下降（SignSGD）算法利用梯度量化的思想，其中客户端仅在本地模型训练后将梯度的“符号”传输到控制中心。正如我们通过涉及具有一组拜占庭攻击模型的基准神经网络的实验所强调的那样，我们提出的方法非常有效地减轻了此类威胁，因此优于传统的 Fed-SGD 模型。 本文提出一种结合差分隐私与梯度量化的安全联邦学习框架，用于住宅短期负荷预测，以解决数据隐私泄露和拜占庭攻击问题。 Muhammad Akbar Husnoo,Adnan Anwar,Nasser Hosseinzadeh,Shama Naz Islam,Abdun Naser Mahmood,Robin Doss cs.CR,cs.AI,cs.DC,cs.LG

title: NAF: Neural Attenuation Fields for Sparse-View CBCT Reconstruction
title_zh: NAF：用于稀疏视图 CBCT 重建的神经衰减场
abstract: This paper proposes a novel and fast self-supervised solution for sparse-view CBCT reconstruction (Cone Beam Computed Tomography) that requires no external training data. Specifically, the desired attenuation coefficients are represented as a continuous function of 3D spatial coordinates, parameterized by a fully-connected deep neural network. We synthesize projections discretely and train the network by minimizing the error between real and synthesized projections. A learning-based encoder entailing hash coding is adopted to help the network capture high-frequency details. This encoder outperforms the commonly used frequency-domain encoder in terms of having higher performance and efficiency, because it exploits the smoothness and sparsity of human organs. Experiments have been conducted on both human organ and phantom datasets. The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy and spends reasonably short computation time.
summary: This paper proposes a novel and fast self-supervised solution for sparse-view CBCT reconstruction (Cone Beam Computed Tomography) that requires no external training data. Specifically, the desired attenuation coefficients are represented as a continuous function of 3D spatial coordinates, parameterized by a fully-connected deep neural network. We synthesize projections discretely and train the network by minimizing the error between real and synthesized projections. A learning-based encoder entailing hash coding is adopted to help the network capture high-frequency details. This encoder outperforms the commonly used frequency-domain encoder in terms of having higher performance and efficiency, because it exploits the smoothness and sparsity of human organs. Experiments have been conducted on both human organ and phantom datasets. The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy and spends reasonably short computation time.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新颖且快速的自监督解决方案，用于稀疏视图 CBCT 重建（锥束计算机断层扫描），无需外部训练数据。具体来说，所需的衰减系数表示为 3D 空间坐标的连续函数，由全连接的深度神经网络参数化。我们离散地合成投影，并通过最小化真实投影和合成投影之间的误差来训练网络。采用基于学习的编码器（需要哈希编码）来帮助网络捕获高频细节。该编码器在更高的性能和效率方面优于常用的频域编码器，因为它利用了人体器官的平滑性和稀疏性。已经在人体器官和模型数据集上进行了实验。所提出的方法实现了最先进的精度并且花费相当短的计算时间。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需外部训练数据的稀疏视角CBCT重建方法NAF，通过神经网络参数化衰减系数实现快速自监督重建。
AI.motivation: 传统CBCT重建方法依赖外部训练数据且计算成本高，而稀疏视角数据下重建质量下降，需开发高效自监督解决方案。
AI.method: 将衰减系数表示为3D坐标的连续函数，用全连接神经网络参数化；通过离散投影合成与误差最小化训练网络，并采用基于哈希编码的编码器捕捉高频细节。
AI.result: 在人体器官和仿真数据集上均达到最先进精度，计算时间合理，优于传统频率域编码器。
AI.conclusion: NAF方法在稀疏视角CBCT重建中兼顾高精度与效率，为临床应用提供了可行方案。

search_text: NAF: Neural Attenuation Fields for Sparse-View CBCT Reconstruction NAF：用于稀疏视图 CBCT 重建的神经衰减场 This paper proposes a novel and fast self-supervised solution for sparse-view CBCT reconstruction (Cone Beam Computed Tomography) that requires no external training data. Specifically, the desired attenuation coefficients are represented as a continuous function of 3D spatial coordinates, parameterized by a fully-connected deep neural network. We synthesize projections discretely and train the network by minimizing the error between real and synthesized projections. A learning-based encoder entailing hash coding is adopted to help the network capture high-frequency details. This encoder outperforms the commonly used frequency-domain encoder in terms of having higher performance and efficiency, because it exploits the smoothness and sparsity of human organs. Experiments have been conducted on both human organ and phantom datasets. The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy and spends reasonably short computation time. 本文提出了一种新颖且快速的自监督解决方案，用于稀疏视图 CBCT 重建（锥束计算机断层扫描），无需外部训练数据。具体来说，所需的衰减系数表示为 3D 空间坐标的连续函数，由全连接的深度神经网络参数化。我们离散地合成投影，并通过最小化真实投影和合成投影之间的误差来训练网络。采用基于学习的编码器（需要哈希编码）来帮助网络捕获高频细节。该编码器在更高的性能和效率方面优于常用的频域编码器，因为它利用了人体器官的平滑性和稀疏性。已经在人体器官和模型数据集上进行了实验。所提出的方法实现了最先进的精度并且花费相当短的计算时间。 本文提出一种无需外部训练数据的稀疏视角CBCT重建方法NAF，通过神经网络参数化衰减系数实现快速自监督重建。 Ruyi Zha,Yanhao Zhang,Hongdong Li eess.IV,cs.CV
